EDCS-00380907.text_edition: \n Aurelius Annianus p Iulianus lius Sabinus Valerius Iulianus Iunius Antonius Iulius Valerius Valerius Bassinus\n
EDCS-00380907.translation: Aurelius Annianus, son of Julianus Sabinus, Valerius Julianus, son of Junius Antonius, Julius Valerius, son of Valerius Bassinus.
EDCS-00380907.summary: This inscription records a list of names, likely a family or group, with patronymic relationships indicated, date unknown.

EDCS-00380911.summary: This inscription records the names Minicius Marcus, possibly a dedication or ownership marker, date unknown.

EDCS-00380914.text_edition: \n Legio XIII gemina Iulius Iulianus CXIIII\n
EDCS-00380914.summary: This inscription mentions the Thirteenth Legion Gemina and Iulius Iulianus, possibly indicating a unit roster or numerical record, date unknown.

EDCS-00380918.summary: This inscription states that Diocl[---] made something, likely referring to the erection of a monument, date unknown.

EDCS-00380919.summary: This inscription records the name Gaius Gemelinus, possibly a dedication or ownership marker, date unknown.

EDCS-03000781.summary: This inscription is an abbreviated dedication or ownership marker to Gaius Julius M., date unknown.

EDCS-03000782.summary: This inscription is an abbreviated dedication or ownership marker to Lucius Ophonius A., date unknown.

EDCS-03000783.summary: This inscription is an abbreviated dedication or ownership marker to Lucius Ophonius L., date unknown.

EDCS-03000784.summary: This inscription is an abbreviated dedication or ownership marker to Quintus Q. Q., date unknown.

EDCS-03300768.text_edition: \n Aeterno Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Alexander ex viso\n
EDCS-03300768.translation: To the Eternal, Best, and Greatest, Publius Aelius Alexander, from a vision.
EDCS-03300768.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter by Publius Aelius Alexander, who claims to have been inspired by a vision, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-03300770.text_edition: \n Apollini Domesticus Publi Aeli Proculi beneficiarii praefecti legionis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-03300770.translation: Publius Aelius Proculus, a staff officer of the prefect of the legion, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Apollo the Protector of the Household.
EDCS-03300770.summary: This inscription records a soldier's votive offering to Apollo, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300771.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the deified Empress Faustina, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300772.translation: Aelius Bassus of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.
EDCS-03300772.summary: This inscription records the name of Aelius Bassus, a soldier of the Legio XIII Gemina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300773.translation: Aelius Iulius of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.
EDCS-03300773.summary: This inscription records the name of Aelius Iulius, a soldier of the Legio XIII Gemina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300774.translation: Aelius Philippus of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.
EDCS-03300774.summary: This inscription records the name of Aelius Philippus, a soldier of the Legio XIII Gemina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300775.translation: Antonius Eventus of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.
EDCS-03300775.summary: This inscription records the name of Antonius Eventus, a soldier of the Legio XIII Gemina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300776.translation: Antonius Firmus of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.
EDCS-03300776.summary: This inscription records the name of Antonius Firmus, a soldier of the Legio XIII Gemina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300777.translation: Aurelius Callistratus of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.
EDCS-03300777.summary: This inscription records the name of Aurelius Callistratus, a soldier of the Legio XIII Gemina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300778.translation: Aurelius Godes of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.
EDCS-03300778.summary: This inscription records the name of Aurelius Godes, a soldier of the Legio XIII Gemina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300779.translation: Aurelius Mommo of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.
EDCS-03300779.summary: This inscription records the name of Aurelius Mommo, a soldier of the Legio XIII Gemina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300780.text_edition: \n Legio XIII gemina Luci Auri Secundi\n
EDCS-03300780.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina of Lucius Aurelius Secundus.
EDCS-03300780.summary: This inscription likely names the Thirteenth Legion Gemina and its owner or perhaps a soldier, Lucius Aurelius Secundus, possibly marking equipment or a structure.

EDCS-03300781.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina, possibly Euphron.
EDCS-03300781.summary: This inscription identifies the Thirteenth Legion Gemina and the name Euphron, likely indicating ownership or a soldier's name, possibly dating from the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-03300782.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina of Flavius Martinus.
EDCS-03300782.summary: This inscription records the Thirteenth Legion Gemina and the name Flavius Martinus, potentially indicating ownership or a soldier, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-03300783.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina of Flavius Vitalis.
EDCS-03300783.summary: This inscription names the Thirteenth Legion Gemina and Flavius Vitalis, likely marking ownership or a soldier and dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-03300784.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina of Julius Deiotarus.
EDCS-03300784.summary: This inscription mentions the Thirteenth Legion Gemina and Julius Deiotarus, possibly indicating ownership or a soldier, from the Roman imperial era.

EDCS-03300785.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina of Julius Julianus.
EDCS-03300785.summary: This inscription records the Thirteenth Legion Gemina and Julius Julianus, likely marking ownership or a soldier, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-03300786.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina of Iulius Logetus.
EDCS-03300786.summary: This inscription identifies the Thirteenth Legion Gemina and Iulius Logetus, potentially indicating ownership or a soldier, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-03300787.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina of Iulius Maximus.
EDCS-03300787.summary: This inscription records the Thirteenth Legion Gemina and Iulius Maximus, likely indicating ownership or a soldier, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-03300788.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina of Iulius Serenus.
EDCS-03300788.summary: This inscription names the Thirteenth Legion Gemina and Iulius Serenus, potentially marking ownership or a soldier, from the Roman imperial era.

EDCS-03300789.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina of Justus.
EDCS-03300789.summary: This inscription records the Thirteenth Legion Gemina and the name Justus, likely indicating ownership or a soldier, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-03300790.text_edition: \n Legio XIII gemina Locretius sic Acvila sic\n
EDCS-03300790.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina, Locretius Sicaquila.
EDCS-03300790.summary: This inscription records a soldier from the Thirteenth Legion Gemina named Locretius Sicaquila, likely an identification marking.

EDCS-03300791.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina, Ofellius Annianus.
EDCS-03300791.summary: This inscription records a soldier from the Thirteenth Legion Gemina named Ofellius Annianus, likely an identification marking.

EDCS-03300792.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina, of Rufus.
EDCS-03300792.summary: This inscription records a soldier from the Thirteenth Legion Gemina named Rufus, likely an identification marking.

EDCS-03300793.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina, Ulpius Fronto.
EDCS-03300793.summary: This inscription records a soldier from the Thirteenth Legion Gemina named Ulpius Fronto, likely an identification marking.

EDCS-03300794.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina, Valerius C[---].
EDCS-03300794.summary: This inscription records a soldier from the Thirteenth Legion Gemina named Valerius C[---], likely an identification marking.

EDCS-03300795.summary: This inscription records a soldier from the Thirteenth Legion Gemina named Victor, likely an identification marking.

EDCS-03300796.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Valerius vixit annis XL Aurelia Banea coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-03300796.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Valerius lived 40 years. Aurelia Banea dedicated this to her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-03300796.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the death of Aurelius Valerius at age 40, dedicated by his wife Aurelia Banea.

EDCS-03300798.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Dules Maximi vixit annis XLVII IVAL AXIS annis ANIA Nice coniu lio posuit\n
EDCS-03300798.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Dules son of Maximus lived 47 years. Ivalaxis lived [---] years. Nice dedicated this to her husband.
EDCS-03300798.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the death of Dules son of Maximus at age 47 and his wife Nice, likely with missing information.

EDCS-03700693.summary: This inscription is likely an owner's mark or a name identification, possibly on an object or property.

EDCS-03700697.summary: This inscription records a soldier from the Thirteenth Legion Gemina named Iulianus, likely an identification marking.

EDCS-03700698.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Antoniae Paulae Quartus miles legionis XIII g(eminae) et Valerius Crescentinus \n
EDCS-03700698.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quartus, a soldier of the 13th Gemina legion, and Valerius Crescentinus [dedicated this].
EDCS-03700698.summary: This is a tombstone inscription honoring Antonia Paula, dedicated by a soldier and another individual, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03700699.text_edition: \n Ulpius Do pro s(alute) commeantium votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-03700699.translation: Ulpius Do willingly fulfilled his vow for the safety of travelers.
EDCS-03700699.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Ulpius Do for the well-being of travelers, likely a votive offering from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03700700.text_edition: \n Aesculapio et Hygiae Marcus Gallius Epictetus Augustalis coloniae Apulensis et Gallia Severina eius et Gallius Surus et Gallius Severinus et Gallius Severianus filii pro salute sua suorumque somno monitus porticum fecit per pedes XXXVI\n
EDCS-03700700.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygieia, Marcus Gallius Epictetus, an Augustalis of the colony of Apula, and his wife Gallia Severina, and his sons Gallius Surus, Gallius Severinus, and Gallius Severianus, prompted by a dream, built this portico 36 feet long for their own well-being and that of their family.
EDCS-03700700.summary: This inscription records the construction of a portico to the gods Aesculapius and Hygieia by Marcus Gallius Epictetus and his family for their health, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03700701.text_edition: \n Herculi defensori Titus Claudius Durianus in reditu ex Urbe sospes votum solvit libens merito de sua pecunia\n
EDCS-03700701.translation: To Hercules the Defender, Titus Claudius Durianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow from his own funds upon a safe return from Rome.
EDCS-03700701.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Hercules by Titus Claudius Durianus, commemorating his safe return from Rome, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03700702.text_edition: \n Silvano sancto Solon ex voto fecit dedicavitque Kalendas Pisone et Iul(iano) conssulibus\n
EDCS-03700702.translation: To the holy Silvanus, Solon made and dedicated this in fulfillment of a vow during the consulship of Piso and Iulianus.
EDCS-03700702.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the god Silvanus by Solon, dated to the consulship of Piso and Iulianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03700704.text_edition: \n Nymphis sanctissimis Marcus Aurelius Mossianus optio primi pili legionis XIII geminae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-03700704.translation: To the most holy Nymphs, Marcus Aurelius Mossianus, an optio of the first cohort of the 13th Gemina legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-03700704.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the Nymphs by Marcus Aurelius Mossianus, an officer in the 13th Gemina legion, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03700705.text_edition: \n Genio Imperatoris Antonini Augusti Pii patris patriae Marcus Statius Priscus legatus Augusti pro praetore votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-03700705.translation: To the Genius of Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the country, Marcus Statius Priscus, imperial legate with proconsular power, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-03700705.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the Genius of Emperor Antoninus Pius by Marcus Statius Priscus, a provincial governor, likely from the Antonine period.

EDCS-04900900.text_edition: \n Mercurio Augusto sacrum Decimus Iunius Teles votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-04900900.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus. Decimus Iunius Teles willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-04900900.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Mercury Augustus by Decimus Iunius Teles, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-04900901.text_edition: \n Herculi cives Romani per Satriaanum et Decimum magistros\n
EDCS-04900901.translation: To Hercules, the Roman citizens, through Satrianus and Decimus, their magistrates.
EDCS-04900901.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Hercules by Roman citizens, acting through their magistrates Satrianus and Decimus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-04900902.text_edition: \n Terrae Matri cives Romani per Satrianum et Decumum magistros\n
EDCS-04900902.translation: To Mother Earth, the Roman citizens, through Satrianus and Decimus, their magistrates.
EDCS-04900902.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mother Earth by Roman citizens, acting through their magistrates Satrianus and Decimus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-04900903.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Dis hospitibus huiusce loci Marcus Pollius Marci filius Oufentina Hispanus Mediolano |(centurio) legionis V Macedonicae piae constantis et Caedicia Marcia eius cum suis\n
EDCS-04900903.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to the resident gods, Marcus Pollius Hispanus, son of Marcus, of the Oufentina tribe, a centurion of the 5th Macedonian Legion, pious and constant, and his wife Caedicia Marcia, with their family.
EDCS-04900903.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter and household gods by a military officer and his family, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-04900904.text_edition: \n Fortunae Domesticae Moschus Augusti libertus tabularius et Aurelia Asclepiodote ex voto\n
EDCS-04900904.translation: To the Household Fortune, Moschus, freedman of Augustus and a scribe, and Aurelia Asclepiodote, according to their vow.
EDCS-04900904.summary: This is a votive dedication to the household gods made by a freedman official and a woman.

EDCS-04900905.text_edition: \n Aurelius Valens sisquiplicarius sic alae Inlyricorum sic vixit nns XLV Tzetzi coniux coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-04900905.translation: To Aurelius Valens, a sisqueliplarius (a type of soldier) of the I and II Alae Illyricorum, lived 45 years. Tzetzi dedicated this to his deserving wife.
EDCS-04900905.summary: This is a funerary monument for Aurelius Valens, a soldier who lived to 45, set up by his wife Tzetzi.

EDCS-04900906.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelia Kara vixit annos XL Ulpius Dassius veteranus ex decurione coniugi pientissimae bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-04900906.translation: To the Divine Spirits, Aelia Kara lived 40 years. Ulpius Dassius, a veteran formerly a decurion, made this for his most dutiful and deserving wife.
EDCS-04900906.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelia Kara, set up by her husband, a former military officer.

EDCS-04900909.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Diana by an individual associated with the province of Dacia Ripensis.

EDCS-04900911.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Diana by Cornelia Marcellina.

EDCS-04900912.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Hygieia (goddess of health) by Cornelia Marcellina.

EDCS-04900913.summary: This is a dedication to the Nymphs by Baebius Ingenuus.

EDCS-04900914.summary: This is a dedication to the Nymphs by Licinia Cale.

EDCS-04900915.translation: To the Nymphs, Decebalus son of Lucius, dedicated this.
EDCS-04900915.summary: This is a dedication to the Nymphs by Decebalus, son of Lucius.

EDCS-04900916.text_edition: \n Nymphis sanctis Augustis simul et tibi sancta Deana fontique vestro rettulit sua vota libens saluti ter refirmatus aquis Germisarae Marcus Aurelius Theodotus votum solvit libens merito Imperatore Commodo Felice consule VI VIII Kalendas Commodas\n
EDCS-04900916.translation: Marcus Aurelius Theodotus, having been thrice restored to health by the waters of Germisara, has gladly fulfilled his vow to the holy August Nymphs, and to you, holy Deana, and to your spring, for his health. He paid his vow gladly and deservedly, when Commodus was consul for the sixth time, on the eighth day before the Kalends of Commodus.
EDCS-04900916.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Marcus Aurelius Theodotus to the Nymphs and Deana at Germisara for his health, dating to the consulship of Commodus in the late 2nd century AD.

EDCS-04900918.text_edition: \n Nymphis sacrum Lucius Livius Marcellus IIviralis coloniae pro filiis ex visu libens merito\n
EDCS-04900918.translation: Sacred to the Nymphs. Lucius Livius Marcellus, as duumvir of the colony, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for his sons, after seeing them in a vision.
EDCS-04900918.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Lucius Livius Marcellus, a duumvir, to the Nymphs for his sons, likely from the late 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-04900919.text_edition: \n Nymphis Titus Fabius Aquileiensis tribunus numeri singularium Britannicianorum\n
EDCS-04900919.translation: To the Nymphs. Titus Fabius from Aquileia, tribune of the elite cohort of the Britanniciani.
EDCS-04900919.summary: This inscription records the dedication of Titus Fabius, a tribune in the elite cohort of the Britanniciani, to the Nymphs, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-05000698.summary: This is a very brief inscription possibly commemorating Tiberius Claudius Claudius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05000699.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Arranius Epaphroditus et Arranius Niger filius posuerunt\n
EDCS-05000699.translation: Marcus Arranius Epaphroditus and Arranius Niger, his son, erected this to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-05000699.summary: This inscription records the dedication of Marcus Arranius Epaphroditus and his son Arranius Niger to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05000700.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Vibius Nicostratus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-05000700.translation: Caius Vibius Nicostratus paid his vow gladly and deservedly to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-05000700.summary: This inscription records Caius Vibius Nicostratus fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05000701.text_edition: \n Chrestus Zoili ex voto pro filio Demosthene testamento libens merito solvit\n
EDCS-05000701.translation: Chrestus, freedman of Zoilus, paid his vow gladly and deservedly for his son Demosthenes by his will.
EDCS-05000701.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Chrestus, a freedman of Zoilus, for his son Demosthenes, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05000703.translation: Flavinus, son of Castor, by order of Hercules.
EDCS-05000703.summary: This inscription records Flavinus, son of Castor, acting under the inspiration or command of Hercules, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05000704.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Aurelius Diocles beneficiarius procuratoris\n
EDCS-05000704.translation: Titus Aurelius Diocles, beneficiarius of the procurator, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-05000704.summary: This inscription records Titus Aurelius Diocles, a staff member of a procurator, dedicating something to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05000706.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Statiae Festae Caius Restitutius Iustus et Caius Iulius Priscinus votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-05000706.translation: Caius Restitutius Iustus and Caius Iulius Priscinus paid their vows gladly and deservedly to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the health of Statia Festa.
EDCS-05000706.summary: This inscription records Caius Restitutius Iustus and Caius Iulius Priscinus fulfilling vows to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the well-being of Statia Festa, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05000708.text_edition: \n Aurelius Occon Quietia votum libentes m(erito)? p(osuerunt)\n
EDCS-05000708.translation: Aurelius Occon and Quietia gladly, as is fitting, made their vow.
EDCS-05000708.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Aurelius Occon and Quietia, likely a family group, to an unspecified deity.

EDCS-05000710.text_edition: \n Deae virgini Dianae stabili venatrici examinatrici Aurelius Marcellus praefectus eqquitum\n
EDCS-05000710.translation: To the maiden goddess Diana, steady huntress, sharp examiner, Aurelius Marcellus, prefect of horsemen, made his vow.
EDCS-05000710.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Aurelius Marcellus, prefect of horsemen, to the goddess Diana.

EDCS-05000711.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori Marcus Iunius Iunianus praefectus equitum votum solvit\n
EDCS-05000711.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Protector, Marcus Iunius Iunianus, prefect of horsemen, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05000711.summary: This inscription records a votive offering fulfilled by Marcus Iunius Iunianus, prefect of horsemen, to Jupiter.

EDCS-05200580.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Quintus Marius Proculus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-05200580.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Quintus Marius Proculus, an attendant of a consular official, gladly and as is fitting fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05200580.summary: This inscription records a votive offering fulfilled by Quintus Marius Proculus, an attendant of a consular official, to Jupiter.

EDCS-05200581.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Calpurnius Priscinus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-05200581.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Caius Calpurnius Priscinus, an attendant of a consular official, gladly and as is fitting fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05200581.summary: This inscription records a votive offering fulfilled by Caius Calpurnius Priscinus, an attendant of a consular official, to Jupiter.

EDCS-05200582.text_edition: \n Mercurio Augusto sacrum Nasidius Primus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-05200582.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus; Nasidius Primus fulfilled his vow gladly and as is fitting.
EDCS-05200582.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Nasidius Primus to Mercury Augustus.

EDCS-05200583.text_edition: \n Neptuno Augusto votum solvit libens merito Nassidius sic Primus\n
EDCS-05200583.translation: To Neptune Augustus, Nassidius sic Primus fulfilled his vow gladly and as is fitting.
EDCS-05200583.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Nassidius Primus to Neptune Augustus.

EDCS-05200584.text_edition: \n Maelantonio Dasas et Dazurius pro salutae sic Maniatium votum solverunt libentes\n
EDCS-05200584.translation: Maniatium, for the safety of Maelantonio Dasas and Dazurius, fulfilled their vow gladly.
EDCS-05200584.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Maniatium for the safety of Maelantonio Dasas and Dazurius.

EDCS-05200590.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Iucundius Verus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-05200590.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Caius Iucundius Verus, an attendant of a consular official, gladly and as is fitting fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05200590.summary: This inscription records a votive offering fulfilled by Caius Iucundius Verus, an attendant of a consular official, to Jupiter.

EDCS-05200592.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the goddess Naonia by Julia Beuci.

EDCS-05200593.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Calpurnius Priscinus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit merito libens\n
EDCS-05200593.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Gaius Calpurnius Priscinus, beneficiary of the governor, willingly fulfilled his vow as was deserved.
EDCS-05200593.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter by Gaius Calpurnius Priscinus, a military beneficiary, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05200594.text_edition: \n Aerecurae Aelius Scenus Bala filius votum solvit libens\n
EDCS-05200594.translation: To Aerecura, Aelius Scenus, son of Bala, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05200594.summary: This is a votive inscription to the goddess Aerecura by Aelius Scenus, possibly from the Roman imperial period, indicating local religious practice.

EDCS-05200595.text_edition: \n Iano Gemino kastellum Ansis votum solvit libens\n
EDCS-05200595.translation: To Janus Geminus, the fortress of Ansium, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05200595.summary: This inscription records a vow made by the fortress of Ansium to the god Janus Geminus, suggesting a military context from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05200596.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Tritio Gar votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-05200596.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Tritius Gar, willingly and as deserved fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05200596.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter by Tritius Gar, likely dating to the Roman imperial period and indicating personal piety.

EDCS-05200597.text_edition: \n Terrae Matri Batonianus posuit votum libens merito\n
EDCS-05200597.translation: Batonianus erected this in honor of the Mother Goddess, willingly and as deserved fulfilling his vow.
EDCS-05200597.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Mother Goddess by Batonianus, likely from the Roman imperial period, reflecting local religious cults.

EDCS-05200598.text_edition: \n Neptuni sic sacrum Surio Sumeletis votum solvit libens\n
EDCS-05200598.translation: To Neptune, thus sacred, Surio, son of Sumeletis, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05200598.summary: This is a votive inscription to Neptune by Surio, son of Sumeletis, likely from the Roman imperial period, dedicated to the sea god.

EDCS-05200599.text_edition: \n Nimphis sic sacrum Implaius Sumeletis votum solvit libens\n
EDCS-05200599.translation: To the Nymphs, thus sacred, Implaius, son of Sumeletis, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05200599.summary: This is a votive inscription to the Nymphs by Implaius, son of Sumeletis, likely from the Roman imperial period, honoring nature spirits.

EDCS-05200600.summary: This is a simple votive inscription by Zosimos, likely from the Roman imperial period, recording the fulfillment of a personal vow.

EDCS-05200602.translation: Sacred to the Star, Aelius Quintus.
EDCS-05200602.summary: This inscription dedicates something as sacred to the Star (possibly a celestial body or a deified concept), by Aelius Quintus, likely Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05200605.text_edition: \n Aelius Equester veteranus ex |(centurione) Aureliae Iuiuni coniugi virginiae bene merenti con sic quen sic vixit annis LX posuit titulum et sibi\n
EDCS-05200605.translation: Aelius Equester, a veteran, set up this inscription and memorial for Aurelia Iuvinis, his wife, a virgin, who deserved well, who lived for 60 years.
EDCS-05200605.summary: This memorial inscription, likely from the Roman imperial period, records a veteran Aelius Equester setting up a monument for his deserving wife, Aurelia Iuvinis, who died at age 60.

EDCS-06100498.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Caio Vibio Afinio Gallo Veldumiano Volusiano Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate II patri patriae consuli II proconsuli ordo coloniae Aureliae Apulensis Chrysopolis numini eius dedicavit\n
EDCS-06100498.translation: To Emperor Caesar Gaius Vibius Afinius Gallus Veldumianus Volusianus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the second time, father of the fatherland, consul for the second time, proconsul, the council of the colonia Aurelia Apulensis Chrysopolis dedicated [this monument] to his divine majesty.
EDCS-06100498.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by the city council of Chrysopolis to the emperor Gallus, likely in the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-07000829.translation: To Silvanus Domesticanus, Aelius Frontianus [dedicated this].
EDCS-07000829.summary: This is a dedication to the god Silvanus by Aelius Frontianus, probably a private offering.

EDCS-07000832.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Fortunatus Caesaris nostri verna Valerianus votum solvit libens\n
EDCS-07000832.translation: Fortunatus, a household slave (verna) of our Caesar Valerianus, willingly paid his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-07000832.summary: This inscription records a vow paid by a freedman to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely during the reign of Emperor Valerian (mid-3rd century CE).

EDCS-07000833.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Marci Valeri Martini decurionis coloniae votum libens posuit\n
EDCS-07000833.translation: For the health of Marcus Valerius Martinus, a decurion of the colony, [someone] willingly set up this vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-07000833.summary: This inscription records a vow offered to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the health of Marcus Valerius Martinus, a local official.

EDCS-07000834.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Baebius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07000834.translation: Publius Aelius Baebius willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-07000834.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Publius Aelius Baebius.

EDCS-07000837.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Flavius Maximus Surus sacerdos vixit nnos XLV Castor et Foebus sic pientissimi bene merenti posuerunt\n
EDCS-07000837.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Flavius Maximus Surus, priest, lived 45 years. Castor and Foebus, his most dutiful [heirs], set this up for him as he deserved well.
EDCS-07000837.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Flavius Maximus Surus, a priest, set up by his heirs, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07000842.text_edition: \n Iuppiter Maximus \xce\xa3abidas Hutri votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07000842.translation: Iuppiter Maximus Ḥabidas Hutri willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-07000842.summary: This inscription records a vow to Jupiter Maximus Ḥabidas by someone named Hutri.

EDCS-07000843.text_edition: \n Pro salute et victoria Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Antonini Commodi Pii Felicis Augusti nostri restitutoris commerciorum et Genio publici portorii Illyrici Claudius Xenophon procurator Augusti nostri per Marcionem et Pollionem vilicos\n
EDCS-07000843.translation: For the health and victory of our Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Commodus Pius Felix Augustus, restorer of trade, and for the Genius of the public customs office of Illyricum, Claudius Xenophon, procurator of our emperor, through Marcion and Pollio, overseers (vilici).
EDCS-07000843.summary: This inscription records prayers for the emperor Commodus and the Genius of the Illyrican customs office, mentioning imperial officials and their subordinates, likely from the late 2nd century CE.

EDCS-07000844.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Imperatoris Marci Aureli Antonini Augusti et Commodi [Caesaris] et Genio publici portorii vectigalis Illyrici procurante Pompeio Longo procuratore Augusti Felix eius vilicus\n
EDCS-07000844.translation: For the health of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus and Commodus Caesar, and for the Genius of the public toll office of Illyricum, Felix, his overseer (vilicus), under the administration of Pompeius Longus, procurator of our emperor.
EDCS-07000844.summary: This inscription records prayers for the emperors Marcus Aurelius and Commodus and the Illyrican customs office, mentioning imperial administrators, likely from the late 2nd century CE.

EDCS-07400623.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelio Dubitato missicio alae Batavorum ex ala numeri Illyricorum qui vixit annos XXX heredes et coniunx fecerunt hic situs est\n
EDCS-07400623.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aelius Dubitatus, discharged soldier (missicius) from the ala of the Batavians, from the cohort of the Illyricans, who lived 30 years, his heirs and wife made this; he is buried here.
EDCS-07400623.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aelius Dubitatus, a retired soldier of mixed auxiliary units, set up by his family, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07400629.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Fll(aviae) Ursulae vixit annos LXXXIII Flavia Caemina liberta pro pietate patronae suae posuit bene merenti\n
EDCS-07400629.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Flavia Ursula lived for 83 years. Flavia Caemina, her freedwoman, placed this for her deserving patroness out of dutiful affection.
EDCS-07400629.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Flavia Ursula, set up by her freedwoman Flavia Caemina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07400630.text_edition: \n VA miles legionis XIII geminae exceptor legati Augusti vixit nnos XX Marcus Valerius Maximinus actarius cohortis \n
EDCS-07400630.translation: Marcus Valerius Maximinus, actuarius of a cohort, a soldier of the XIII Gemina legion, the escort of the imperial legate, lived 20 years.
EDCS-07400630.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Valerius Maximinus, who served as an actuarius and escort to a legate, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07400631.text_edition: \n Deo sancto aeterno Aurelius l ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-07400631.translation: Aurelius offered this to the holy, eternal god according to his vow.
EDCS-07400631.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to a nameless 'holy, eternal god' by Aurelius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07400633.summary: This is a votive inscription by Aurelius Saturninus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07600401.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Naevius Felicio cum suis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07600401.translation: Marcus Naevius Felicio, with his family, willingly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, as was deserved.
EDCS-07600401.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Naevius Felicio and his family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07600402.text_edition: \n Deo Silvano Caius Iulius Metrobianus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07600402.translation: Caius Iulius Metrobianus willingly fulfilled his vow to the god Silvanus, as was deserved.
EDCS-07600402.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Silvanus by Caius Iulius Metrobianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07600409.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ulpius Adiutor vixit annos LXXX Ulpia Iaia coniux vixit annos L filii et filia suis parentibus posierunt sic bene merentibus\n
EDCS-07600409.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Ulpius Adiutor lived 80 years. His wife, Ulpia Iaia, lived 50 years. Their sons and daughter placed this for their parents, who deserved it well.
EDCS-07600409.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ulpius Adiutor and his wife Ulpia Iaia, set up by their children, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07600410.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gaius Valerius Dius vixit annos LX\n
EDCS-07600410.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Valerius Dius lived 60 years.
EDCS-07600410.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Valerius Dius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08400449.text_edition: \n Numini Saturno regi patri deorum et Latonae Publius Raecius Primus beneficiarius legati legionis V Macedonicae piae fidelis posuit ex voto domo Zigali Afer\n
EDCS-08400449.translation: Publius Raecius Primus, a beneficiary of the legate of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, pious and faithful, placed this according to his vow to the divine, kingly, ancestral spirit of Saturn and to Latona; from Zigali, an African from home.
EDCS-08400449.summary: This is a complex votive inscription dedicated to Saturn and Latona by Publius Raecius Primus, a soldier of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500532.text_edition: \n Viator Firmus Aelius Priscus Aelius Domitianus Aelius Florus Aelius Valens Aelius Quintus Atressus Firmus Alexander Germanus Valens Secundus Macer Vialis Maximus Rufinus Valentinus Capito Crescens Longinus Iulius Messius Iulius Siro Pomponius Valens Attius Maximus Aelius Valens Aelius Quadratus Aelius Iulianus Aelius Talanus Licinius Priscus Attius Longinus Aelius Iustus Aelius Valens Aelius Balbinus Flavius Niger Iulius Verus Iulius Bassianus Aelius Bassus Aelius Festus Flavius Flavos sic Claudius Candidianus Caelius Saturn(inus) Aelius Aelius Ulpius Verus Aelius Appius Valerius Verus Cuspius Valens Aelius Mestrius Antonius Valens ex equite Egnatius Libo Marcius Firminus Flavius Venustus Valerius Succesus Aelius Agrippa Aelius Quintus Aelius Firmus Aelius Crescens Aelius Capito Aelius Tertius Aelius Valens Aelius Propincus \n
EDCS-08500532.translation: The following individuals: Viator Firmus, Aelius Priscus, Aelius Domitianus, Aelius Florus, Aelius Valens, Aelius Quintus, Atressus Firmus, Alexander Germanus, Valens Secundus, Macer Vialis, Maximus Rufinus, Valentinus Capito, Crescens Longinus, Iulius Messius, Iulius Siro, Pomponius Valens, Attius Maximus, Aelius Valens, Aelius Quadratus, Aelius Iulianus, Aelius Talanus, Licinius Priscus, Attius Longinus, Aelius Iustus, Aelius Valens, Aelius Balbinus, Flavius Niger, Iulius Verus, Iulius Bassianus, Aelius Bassus, Aelius Festus, Flavius Flavos, the following Claudius Candidianus, Caelius Saturninus, Aelius Aelius, Ulpius Verus, Aelius Appius, Valerius Verus, Cuspius Valens, Aelius Mestrius, Antonius Valens, formerly of the equestrian order Egnatius Libo, Marcius Firminus, Flavius Venustus, Valerius Succesus, Aelius Agrippa, Aelius Quintus, Aelius Firmus, Aelius Crescens, Aelius Capito, Aelius Tertius, Aelius Valens, Aelius Propincus.
EDCS-08500532.summary: This is a list of numerous individuals, likely representing a group or collegium, possibly associated with a military or civic association, with many bearing the name 'Aelius', suggesting a patron's influence or family connections, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500533.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Gai C Hastae consularis trium Daciarum stratores eius legionis XIII geminae agentes Proculo legato\n
EDCS-08500533.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the safety of Gaius Cornelius Hastas, consular governor of the three Dacian provinces, his staff officers of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, serving under Proculus, the legate.
EDCS-08500533.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the staff officers of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina for the safety of their consular governor, Gaius Cornelius Hastas.

EDCS-08500536.summary: This fragmentary inscription records a date, mentioning the consulship of Vopiscus.

EDCS-08500537.text_edition: \n ---aere collato I IVOVIVSI Iulius Ru SC SED ellus s Surillus Marcus miles Sigillus Aquileiensis Maximus Nicomedis Iulius Statorius Gaianus Agrippae Aurelius Amamus Atius Surus Caius Maioris Aurelius Charito Cassius Valens Aurelius Sossius miles Aurelius Marcus miles Aurelius Valerius Romanius Arist Aurelius Silvanus Hermes Domnionis Aurelius Proclus Gaianus A Crescens Aurelius Aurelius A Aper I Caius Antonius Iulianus Act Nus Aurelius CIC Valerius V \n
EDCS-08500537.translation: --- with money collected, Julius Ru... and Surillus, Marcus, a soldier, Sigillus from Aquileia, Maximus son of Nicomedes, Julius Statorius Gaianus, Agrippa, Aurelius Amamus, Atius Surus, Gaius son of Maioris, Aurelius Charito, Cassius Valens, Aurelius Sossius, a soldier, Aurelius Marcus, a soldier, Aurelius Valerius Romanius, Aristus, Aurelius Silvanus, Hermes son of Domnion, Aurelius Proclus Gaianus, A. Crescens, Aurelius Aper, Caius Antonius Julianus, Actus, Aurelius..., Valerius...
EDCS-08500537.summary: This is a long list of names, likely commemorating a group of individuals or a collective effort, possibly related to a funerary monument or a civic project, with many individuals identified as soldiers.

EDCS-08500539.text_edition: \n sacrum Publius Aelius Rusticus decurio municipii Ampelensium votum vix satis reddidit\n
EDCS-08500539.translation: To the sacred gods, Publius Aelius Rusticus, a decurion of the municipality of Ampelenses, has fulfilled his vow, not without difficulty.
EDCS-08500539.summary: This is a votive inscription by Publius Aelius Rusticus, a decurion, fulfilling a vow made to the gods.

EDCS-08500540.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Val(erius) Telegonus pro <se> et suos sic votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08500540.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Gaius Valerius Telegonus, for himself and his family, has willingly and deservedly paid his vow in this way.
EDCS-08500540.summary: This is a votive inscription by Gaius Valerius Telegonus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, fulfilling a vow for himself and his family.

EDCS-08500541.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo ex voto E Mucianus pro se et pro SOSOSE\n
EDCS-08500541.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, from a vow, E. Mucianus for himself and for...
EDCS-08500541.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely a votive offering by E. Mucianus for himself and others.

EDCS-08500542.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Curneliae sic Quintae vixit annos XX et \n
EDCS-08500542.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, Corneliana Quintae lived 20 years and...
EDCS-08500542.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Corneliana Quintae, recording her age at death.

EDCS-08500548.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Rufus Diomedis vixit annos IV \n
EDCS-08500548.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, Rufus son of Diomedes lived 4 years.
EDCS-08500548.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Rufus son of Diomedes, recording his age at death.

EDCS-08500549.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Pontiae Syusae vixit annos V et Pontiae Asiae matri eius vixit annos \n
EDCS-08500549.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, Pontia Syusae lived 5 years, and to her mother Pontia Asia, who lived...
EDCS-08500549.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pontia Syusae and her mother Pontia Asia, recording their ages at death.

EDCS-08500550.text_edition: \n vixit annos LX Lucius coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-08500550.translation: L. [Name] placed this for his well-deserving wife, having lived 60 years.
EDCS-08500550.summary: This funerary inscription records that L. [Name] placed this monument for his wife, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-08500553.text_edition: \n Silvano Silvestri sacrum Varro Titi posuit\n
EDCS-08500553.translation: Sacred to Silvanus the wild god, Varro son of Titus placed this.
EDCS-08500553.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Silvanus by Varro, son of Titus, likely a local dedication.

EDCS-08500556.text_edition: \n Herculi Augusto Caius Sempronius Urbanus procurator Augusti\n
EDCS-08500556.translation: To Hercules Augustus, Gaius Sempronius Urbanus, procurator of the Emperor, set this up.
EDCS-08500556.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Hercules Augustus by Gaius Sempronius Urbanus, an imperial procurator, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08500557.text_edition: \n Volcano miti Caius Sempronius Urbanus procurator Augusti\n
EDCS-08500557.translation: To gentle Vulcan, Gaius Sempronius Urbanus, procurator of the Emperor, set this up.
EDCS-08500557.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Vulcan by Gaius Sempronius Urbanus, an imperial procurator, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08500558.text_edition: \n Apollini Granno et Sironae Caius Sempronius Urbanus procurator Augusti\n
EDCS-08500558.translation: To Apollo Grannus and Sirona, Gaius Sempronius Urbanus, procurator of the Emperor, set this up.
EDCS-08500558.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the deities Apollo Grannus and Sirona by Gaius Sempronius Urbanus, an imperial procurator, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08500559.text_edition: \n Marti Singili et Minervae Caius Sempronius Urbanus procurator Augusti\n
EDCS-08500559.translation: To Mars Singulus and Minerva, Gaius Sempronius Urbanus, procurator of the Emperor, set this up.
EDCS-08500559.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mars Singulus and Minerva by Gaius Sempronius Urbanus, an imperial procurator, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08500561.text_edition: \n Fortunae Augustae Aelius Apollinaris procurator Augusti\n
EDCS-08500561.translation: To Fortuna Augusta, Aelius Apollinaris, procurator of the Emperor, set this up.
EDCS-08500561.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Fortuna Augusta by Aelius Apollinaris, an imperial procurator, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08500564.text_edition: \n Apollini sancto Marcus Lucceius Felix procurator Augusti nostri\n
EDCS-08500564.translation: To holy Apollo, Marcus Lucceius Felix, procurator of our Emperor, set this up.
EDCS-08500564.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Apollo by Marcus Lucceius Felix, an imperial procurator, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08500565.text_edition: \n Dianae sanctae Marcus Lucceius Felix procurator Augusti nostri\n
EDCS-08500565.translation: To holy Diana, Marcus Lucceius Felix, procurator of our Emperor, set this up.
EDCS-08500565.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Diana by Marcus Lucceius Felix, an imperial procurator, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08500567.text_edition: \n Marcus Lucceius Felix procurator Augusti nostri consecravit\n
EDCS-08500567.translation: Marcus Lucceius Felix, procurator of our Emperor, consecrated this.
EDCS-08500567.summary: This inscription records a consecration by Marcus Lucceius Felix, an imperial procurator, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08500569.text_edition: \n Deae praesentissimae Core Marcus Lucceius Felix procurator Augusti nostri et Hostilia Faustina eius\n
EDCS-08500569.translation: To the ever-present goddess Kore, Marcus Lucceius Felix, procurator of our Emperor, and his wife Hostilia Faustina, dedicated this.
EDCS-08500569.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the goddess Kore (Persephone) by Marcus Lucceius Felix, an imperial procurator, and his wife Hostilia Faustina, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08500570.text_edition: \n Numinibus praesentissimis Apollini et Bono Puero Marcus Aurelius Marcus vir egregius procurator Augusti nostri agens vice praesidis Heraclida alumnus pro salute patroni sui posuit\n
EDCS-08500570.translation: To the ever-present divine powers of Apollo and the Good Boy, Marcus Aurelius Heraclida, freedman, an outstanding man, procurator of our Emperor, acting in place of the governor, dedicated this for the health of his patron.
EDCS-08500570.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Marcus Aurelius Heraclida, a freedman and procurator, to Apollo and the 'Good Boy' for his patron's health, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08500574.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Caio Messio Quinto Traiano Decio Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate consuli II proconsuli ala I Hispanorum Campagonum Deciana civium Romanorum devota numini maiestatique eius ex quaestura sua\n
EDCS-08500574.translation: To Emperor Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Traianus Decius Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power, consul for the second time, proconsul, the I Wing of Spanish Cavalry Campagonum Deciana, Roman citizens, devoted to his divine majesty, from his quaestorship.
EDCS-08500574.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the I Wing of Spanish Cavalry to Emperor Trajan Decius, highlighting his titles and the unit's devotion, likely dating to his reign (249-251 AD).

EDCS-08500577.text_edition: \n Cohortis I Flaviae Hispanorum mmiliariae |(centuriae) Valeri Eumenis Rassini Ursionis\n
EDCS-08500577.translation: Of the I Flavian Wing of a thousand Spanish infantry, the century of Valerius Eumenes Rassinus Ursio.
EDCS-08500577.summary: This inscription identifies a century belonging to the I Flavian Spanish milliary cohort, named after Valerius Eumenes Rassinus Ursio, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08500578.text_edition: \n centuria Severini Quintus cohors I Hispanorum quingenaria\n
EDCS-08500578.translation: The century of Severinus. Quintus. The I Cohort of quinqagenary Spanish infantry.
EDCS-08500578.summary: This inscription identifies a century of the I quinqagenary Spanish cohort, possibly naming Severinus and Quintus, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08500579.text_edition: \n Cohors I Ulpia Hispanorum centuria Cai Pomponi Valer\n
EDCS-08500579.translation: Of the I Ulpian Spanish Cohort, the century of Caius Pomponius Valer[ius].
EDCS-08500579.summary: This inscription marks a century belonging to the I Ulpian Spanish Cohort, named after Caius Pomponius Valerius, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08500580.text_edition: \n Cohortis I Flaviae Hispanorum miliariae |(centuriae) Luci Turani Antestiani Domiti \n
EDCS-08500580.translation: Of the I Flavian Spanish milliary cohort, the century of Lucius Turanius Antestianus Domitius.
EDCS-08500580.summary: This inscription identifies a century of the I Flavian Spanish milliary cohort, named after Lucius Turanius Antestianus Domitius, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08500581.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a century, with most of the text lost.

EDCS-08500582.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a century, with most of the text lost.

EDCS-08500585.text_edition: \n IIAPsterti YE et mensis sic X posuit pater\n
EDCS-08500585.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription mentioning a duration of ten months and a father who erected something, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08500586.summary: This inscription is a dedication or memorial referring to Germanicus the Great, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08500588.text_edition: \n Marci Aureli Cassianus et Veteranus aediles semis\n
EDCS-08500588.translation: Marcus Aurelius Cassianus and Victorinus, aediles, half...
EDCS-08500588.summary: This inscription records the actions of Marcus Aurelius Cassianus and Victorinus, who were aediles, likely related to a building project.

EDCS-08500590.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Respectus miles cohortis I Britannicae \n
EDCS-08500590.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelius Respectus, soldier of the 1st British Cohort...
EDCS-08500590.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits for Aurelius Respectus, a soldier in the 1st British Cohort.

EDCS-08600625.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Iulius M(arci)? f(ilius) Ulpia Victorinus Sarmizegetusa librarius consularis stipendiorum V vixit annis XXIII \n
EDCS-08600625.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus Iulius Victorinus, son of Marcus, of the Ulpian tribe, from Sarmizegetusa, a consular scribe, served for 5 years, lived for 23 years...
EDCS-08600625.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Marcus Iulius Victorinus, a consular scribe from Sarmizegetusa, who died at the age of 23 after serving for 5 years.

EDCS-08600626.text_edition: \n Genio coloniae Sarmizegetusae Quintus Axius Aelianus vir egregius procurator Augusti nostri\n
EDCS-08600626.translation: To the Genius of the colony of Sarmizegetusa. Quintus Axius Aelianus, an outstanding man, procurator of our Augustus...
EDCS-08600626.summary: This inscription honors the Genius of the colony of Sarmizegetusa and mentions Quintus Axius Aelianus, an outstanding man and procurator.

EDCS-08600629.text_edition: \n Publi Corneli Coloni chelidonium opobalsamatum ad caligines Publi Corneli Coloni dioxus ad aspritudines et genas callosas Publi Corneli Coloni diapsoricum opobalsamatum ad claritatem\n
EDCS-08600629.translation: Publius Cornelius Colonus's celidonium (an ointment) with balsam for dark circles, Publius Cornelius Colonus's dioxus for roughness and calloused cheeks, and Publius Cornelius Colonus's diapsoricum with balsam for clarity...
EDCS-08600629.summary: This text is a list of medicinal products, likely for sale or advertisement, from Publius Cornelius Colonus, specifying their uses for various skin conditions.

EDCS-08900552.text_edition: \n Victuriae sic Vaternius Cassianus eques turmae E <v=N>(otum solvit \n
EDCS-08900552.translation: To Victory, thus Vaternius Cassianus, a cavalryman of the turma of Evotus, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08900552.summary: This inscription records Vaternius Cassianus, a cavalryman, fulfilling a vow to the goddess Victory.

EDCS-08900553.text_edition: \n Apollini Dianae et Leto sic ceterisque dis deabusque huiusque loci salutaribus ex imperio numinis Caius Iulius Frontonianus veteranus legionis V Macedonicae piae ex beneficiario consularis decurio coloniae Apulensis pro se et suis pecunia sua pontem exstruxit\n
EDCS-08900553.translation: To Apollo, Diana, and Leto, and to all the beneficial gods and goddesses of this place, by command of the divine will, Gaius Julius Frontonianus, a veteran of the 5th Macedonian Legion, a former principal beneficiary of the consul, and a decurion of the colony of Apulensis, for himself and his family, built a bridge at his own expense.
EDCS-08900553.summary: This inscription records Gaius Julius Frontonianus, a veteran and decurion, building a bridge at his own expense for the well-being of his family and in honor of local deities.

EDCS-08900554.text_edition: \n Matri Deum Magnae Titus Fabius Barbatus pro salute \n
EDCS-08900554.translation: To the Great Mother of Gods, Titus Fabius Barbatus, for his health...
EDCS-08900554.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Titus Fabius Barbatus to the Great Mother of Gods, likely for his well-being.

EDCS-08900555.text_edition: \n Matribus Aus Publius Aelius Secundinus eques legionis XIII geminae Antoninianae ex voto posuit libenter\n
EDCS-08900555.translation: To the Mothers. Publius Aelius Secundinus, an equestrian soldier of the 13th Gemina Antoniniana Legion, set this up as a fulfillment of his vow, gladly.
EDCS-08900555.summary: This inscription records Publius Aelius Secundinus, an equestrian soldier of the 13th Gemina Antoniniana Legion, dedicating a monument to the Mothers in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-08900557.text_edition: \n Filomelus armorum custos legionis XIII geminae vixit annos XXXII Corolla et Aurelius Chrestus et Aelia Victorina heredes eius bene merenti posuerunt\n
EDCS-08900557.translation: Filomelus, keeper of arms of the 13th Gemina Legion, lived for 32 years. His heirs, Corolla and Aurelius Chrestus and Aelia Victorina, set this up for him, who deserved well.
EDCS-08900557.summary: This funerary inscription honors Filomelus, the keeper of arms for the 13th Gemina Legion, who died at age 32, and was honored by his heirs.

EDCS-08900559.text_edition: \n M PAE Flavia ula domoa vixit annos L hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur a maceria? in fronte pedesX in agro pedes ad angulos r pedes CXX\n
EDCS-08900559.translation: Flavia Ula lived 50 years; this monument will not follow an heir. From the wall, 10 feet in front, 120 feet in the field to the corners.
EDCS-08900559.summary: This is a funerary monument specifying the dimensions of the plot and the lifespan of Flavia Ula, with a stipulation that it would not pass to an heir.

EDCS-08900562.text_edition: \n Sergius Maximus fratri pientissimo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-08900562.translation: Sergius Maximus had this made for his most devoted brother.
EDCS-08900562.summary: A funerary monument erected by Sergius Maximus for his brother.

EDCS-08900563.text_edition: \n stupendiorum sic V vixit annis XXV posuit Valerius Senecio beneficiarius legati bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-08900563.translation: After 25 years of service, Valerius Senecio, a beneficiary of the legate, erected this for one who deserved well.
EDCS-08900563.summary: This inscription records Valerius Senecio, a military official, dedicating a monument to a deserving individual after 25 years of service.

EDCS-08900566.text_edition: \n stipendiorum XV vixit annos XXXV Aelius Marcus et Aelius Agrippa heredes faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-08900566.translation: After 15 years of service, Aelius Marcus and Aelius Agrippa, heirs, had this made.
EDCS-08900566.summary: A funerary monument erected by Aelius Marcus and Aelius Agrippa, heirs, for a deceased individual who served 15 years.

EDCS-08900575.text_edition: \n Lucio Aelio Aurelio Commodo filio Imperatoris Titi Aeli Hadriani Antonini Augusti Pii patris patriae res publica municipii Hadriani Drobetae decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-08900575.translation: For Lucius Aelius Aurelius Commodus, son of Emperor Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the fatherland, the public of the municipium of Drobeta Hadriana, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08900575.summary: This is a public dedication by the town council of Drobeta Hadriana to Lucius Aelius Aurelius Commodus, son of Emperor Antoninus Pius.

EDCS-08901108.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico Flavius Mar votum solvit libens\n
EDCS-08901108.translation: Flavius Mar gladly fulfilled his vow to Silvanus Domestos.
EDCS-08901108.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Flavius Mar fulfilling a vow to the deity Silvanus Domestos.

EDCS-08901109.summary: A fragmentary inscription, likely part of a larger text, mentioning a person named Marcus and Severus.

EDCS-08901112.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus Felix Parthicus maximus pontifex maximus Britannicus maximus tribuniciae potestatis XV<I> imperator II consul IIII pater patriae proconsul fecit\n
EDCS-08901112.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus Felix, victor over Parthia, Pontifex Maximus, victor over Britain, holding tribunician power for the 16th time, Imperator for the 2nd time, Consul for the 4th time, father of the fatherland, proconsul, erected this.
EDCS-08901112.summary: This is a monumental inscription by Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius, detailing his titles and achievements, likely indicating the construction of a public building or monument.

EDCS-08901115.text_edition: \n Aelius Thema veteranus ex |(centurione) decurio coloniae LAMAT patri \n
EDCS-08901115.translation: Aelius Thema, veteran, from centurion, decurion of the colony of Lamos, for his father.
EDCS-08901115.summary: This inscription records Aelius Thema, a veteran and decurion, dedicating a monument to his father.

EDCS-08901119.text_edition: \n Aelia siN vixit annos XL Marcus Cocceius Alexander veteranus ex |(centurione) augur municipii Septimii Porolissensis coniugi \n
EDCS-08901119.translation: Aelia Si(n)... lived 40 years. Marcus Cocceius Alexander, veteran, from centurion, augur of the municipium of Septimius Porolissensis, for his wife.
EDCS-08901119.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Marcus Cocceius Alexander, a veteran and local official, to his wife Aelia Si(n).

EDCS-08901134.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the ancestral gods by Aurelius Surianus, who held the consulship twice, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08901135.translation: To the god Mercury and to es patron PPYCO.
EDCS-08901135.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mercury and PPYCO, possibly a local deity or benefactor, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08901137.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Sabinus bis IIviralis et patronus municipii Hadriani Drobetae ob honorem flamoni Cai Iuli Naesi Sabini nepotis sui cryptam vetustate dilapsam pecunia sua reformavit et exaltiavit\n
EDCS-08901137.translation: Gaius Julius Sabinus, twice chief magistrate and patron of the municipium of Hadrianus Drobetae, at his own expense rebuilt and enlarged the crypt, ruined by age, for the priesthood of Caius Julius Naesius Sabinus, his grandson.
EDCS-08901137.summary: This inscription records the rebuilding and enlargement of a crypt by Gaius Julius Sabinus in honor of his grandson's priesthood, likely dating to the Hadrianic period or shortly after.

EDCS-09001563.text_edition: \n Aurelius Cavianus ex votu sic posuit\n
EDCS-09001563.translation: Aurelius Cavianus placed this according to his vow.
EDCS-09001563.summary: This inscription records a votive offering placed by Aurelius Cavianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09200448.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Gaius Sentius Bassus tubicen legionis XIII geminae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09200448.translation: Gaius Sentius Bassus, trumpeter of the XIII Gemina legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-09200448.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Gaius Sentius Bassus, a trumpeter of the XIII Gemina legion, to Jupiter, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09200470.text_edition: \n Genio |(centuriae) Aurelius Asclepiades votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09200470.translation: Aurelius Asclepiades willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the genius of the century.
EDCS-09200470.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Aurelius Asclepiades to the genius of his military century, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09300004.text_edition: \n Pro salute Imperatoris Marci Aureli Antonini Augusti Pii Felicis deo patrio Belo numerus Palmyrenorum sagittariorum templum vi ignis consumptum pecunia sua restituerunt dedicant<e=F> Caio Iulio Septimio Castino consulari III Daciar(um) M(arco) Ulpio Victore procuratore Augusti provinciae Porolissensis curam agente Tito Flavio Saturnino |(centurione) legionis V Macedonicae piae constantis \n
EDCS-09300004.translation: For the safety of the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Pius Felix, the unit of Palmyrene archers restored at their own expense the temple consumed by fire, dedicating it under Caius Julius Septimius Castinus, consular legate of the III Dacian province, and Marcus Ulpius Victor, procurator of the province of Porolissensis, with Titus Flavius Saturninus, centurion of the V Macedonica pia constans legion, overseeing the work.
EDCS-09300004.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple to the Palmyrene archers, dedicated for the safety of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, likely in the mid-2nd century CE.

EDCS-09300505.text_edition: \n Legioni V Macedonicae piae constanti Lucius U<lp=II>(ius) Clem(ens) Caius Valerius Lupus Caius Tiberius Crescens commanuculis sic posuerunt \n
EDCS-09300505.translation: Lucius Ulpius Clemens, Gaius Valerius Lupus, and Gaius Tiberius Crescens placed these alongside the V Macedonica pia constans legion.
EDCS-09300505.summary: This inscription records the placement of something (likely military equipment or standards) by three individuals in association with the V Macedonica pia constans legion, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09300506.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico Valerius Valerianus votum susceptum libens solvit\n
EDCS-09300506.translation: Valerius Valerianus willingly fulfilled his undertaken vow to Silvanus Domestus.
EDCS-09300506.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Valerius Valerianus to the god Silvanus Domestus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09300507.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Silvano ceterisque deis deabusque conservatoribus Publius Arrius anus S v(otum)? s(olvit)? l(ibens) merito\n
EDCS-09300507.translation: Publius Arrius [...] willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest, and to Silvanus and the other guardian gods and goddesses.
EDCS-09300507.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Publius Arrius to Jupiter and various other deities, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09300508.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Claudius Valentinus vixit annis XX Valeria Valentina filio piissimo opto sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09300508.translation: To the Divine Shades. Claudius Valentinus lived 20 years. Valeria Valentina to her most devoted son, with good wishes; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09300508.summary: A funerary epitaph for Claudius Valentinus, who died at the age of 20, dedicated by his mother Valeria Valentina.

EDCS-09300511.text_edition: \n Aelia Ingenua Einis Iris hic iacet sub terra misera alita es in annis plus Fata vetarunt iam nulla est parentes mater \n
EDCS-09300511.translation: Aelia Ingenua, Iris. She lies here under the earth. You are miserable, nursed, but Fate prevented you in your years. She is no more. [Her] parents, [her] mother...
EDCS-09300511.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aelia Ingenua, lamenting her early death, possibly by her mother.

EDCS-09300532.summary: A very brief inscription possibly referring to a ferry crossing or a daily route.

EDCS-09301198.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Silius Cai filius Vellina Crispinus Aquileia |(centurio) legionis XIII geminae strator Luci Iuli Proculi legati Augusti pro praetore votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09301198.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Silius Crispinus, son of Caius, of the Vellina tribe, a camp attendant (strator) to Lucius Julius Proculus, governor with the title of Augustus, paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09301198.summary: A dedication to Jupiter by Silius Crispinus, a camp attendant to a governor, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-09301201.summary: A simple dedication to the god Silvanus.

EDCS-09301203.text_edition: \n Deo Herhiboli Aelius Nisa sacerdos posuit\n
EDCS-09301203.translation: To the god Herhibolus. Aelius Nisa, the priest, erected this.
EDCS-09301203.summary: A dedication to the god Herhibolus by the priest Aelius Nisa.

EDCS-09301204.summary: A motto or statement of pride from the Seventh Legion.

EDCS-09301206.text_edition: \n Nymphis Auggustis pro salute dominnorum n[nostrorum] dedicante Flavio Postumo consulari\n
EDCS-09301206.translation: To the August Nymphs for the safety of our lords, dedicated by Flavius Postumus, a former consul.
EDCS-09301206.summary: A dedication to the Nymphs for the safety of the emperors, made by Flavius Postumus, a former consul.

EDCS-09301208.text_edition: \n Deae Nemesi Reginae Caius Valerius Maximus pecumarius sic ex visu posuit\n
EDCS-09301208.translation: To the Queen Goddess Nemesis. Caius Valerius Maximus, a cattle dealer, set this up thus after a vision.
EDCS-09301208.summary: A votive dedication to the goddess Nemesis by Caius Valerius Maximus, a cattle dealer, prompted by a vision.

EDCS-09301233.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Herculi Magusano pro salute sua et suorum Publius Aelius Maximus a a militiis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09301233.translation: To the Unconquered God Hercules Magusanus. Publius Aelius Maximus paid his vow willingly and deservedly for the safety of himself and his family.
EDCS-09301233.summary: A votive inscription to Hercules Magusanus by Publius Aelius Maximus for his own and his family's well-being.

EDCS-09400620.translation: Legio XIII Gemina, [dedicated by] Aelius Bassus.
EDCS-09400620.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Aelius Bassus, likely a soldier, to the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.

EDCS-09400621.translation: Legio IIIX Gemina, [dedicated by] Aelius Iulius.
EDCS-09400621.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Aelius Iulius, likely a soldier, to the Third Legion Gemina (note: IIIX is a less common representation of 3).

EDCS-09400622.translation: Legio XIII Gemina, [dedicated by] Aelius Philippus.
EDCS-09400622.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Aelius Philippus, likely a soldier, to the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.

EDCS-09400623.translation: Legio IIIX Gemina, [dedicated by] Aelius Valens.
EDCS-09400623.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Aelius Valens, likely a soldier, to the Third Legion Gemina (note: IIIX is a less common representation of 3).

EDCS-09400624.translation: Legio XIII Gemina, [dedicated by] Annaeus Saturninus.
EDCS-09400624.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Annaeus Saturninus, likely a soldier, to the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.

EDCS-09400625.text_edition: \n Legio XIII gemina Antoni Aeli Ateni\n
EDCS-09400625.translation: Legio XIII Gemina, [dedicated by] Antoni Aelius Ateni.
EDCS-09400625.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Antoni Aelius Ateni, likely a soldier, to the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.

EDCS-09400628.translation: Legio IIIX Gemina, [dedicated by] Aurelius Callistratus.
EDCS-09400628.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Aurelius Callistratus, likely a soldier, to the Third Legion Gemina (note: IIIX is a less common representation of 3).

EDCS-09400630.translation: Legio XIII Gemina, [dedicated by] Flavius Heliodorus.
EDCS-09400630.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Flavius Heliodorus, likely a soldier, to the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.

EDCS-09400631.translation: Legio XIII Gemina, [dedicated by] Flavius Vitalis.
EDCS-09400631.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Flavius Vitalis, likely a soldier, to the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.

EDCS-09400632.translation: Legio XIII Gemina, [dedicated by] Iulius Deiotarus.
EDCS-09400632.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Iulius Deiotarus, likely a soldier, to the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.

EDCS-09400633.summary: This inscription likely records a soldier of Legio XIII Gemina named Julius Marcianus.

EDCS-09400634.summary: This inscription likely records a soldier of Legio IIIX Gemina named Iulius Serenus.

EDCS-09400635.summary: This inscription likely records a soldier of Legio XIII Gemina named Lucretius Aquila.

EDCS-09400636.summary: This inscription likely records a soldier of Legio IIIX Gemina named Memmius Fronto.

EDCS-09400638.summary: This inscription likely records a soldier of Legio XIII Gemina named Statius Sentianus.

EDCS-09400639.summary: This inscription likely records a soldier of Legio XIII Gemina named Ulpius Fronto.

EDCS-09401171.text_edition: \n Saluti Marcus Aurelius Apollinaris procurator Augusti cum suis\n
EDCS-09401171.translation: To Salus, Marcus Aurelius Apollinaris, procurator of the Emperor, with his associates.
EDCS-09401171.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Salus by Marcus Aurelius Apollinaris, an imperial procurator, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09401176.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Eustinae Afri vixit XXX Aurelius Iustinus |(centurio bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-09401176.translation: To the divine shades. For Eustina, who lived 30 years. Aurelius Iustinus, centurion, erected [this] for [her] as deserving.
EDCS-09401176.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Eustina, erected by her husband Aurelius Iustinus, a centurion, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09401177.text_edition: \n Silvano Domesstico Aurelius Viator scriba municipii Septimi Potaissensium\n
EDCS-09401177.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Aurelius Viator, scribe of the municipality of Septimia Potaissensium.
EDCS-09401177.summary: This is a dedication to the god Silvanus Domesticus by Aurelius Viator, a scribe from the municipality of Septimia Potaissensium, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09401180.text_edition: \n Marco Iulio Philippo Pio Felici Invicto [Augusto ------\n
EDCS-09401180.translation: To Marcus Julius Philippus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus [------
EDCS-09401180.summary: This is a dedication or honorific inscription to Emperor Marcus Julius Philippus, likely from the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-09401554.translation: Of the municipium of Potaissa, Vale.
EDCS-09401554.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely records something related to the municipium of Potaissa.

EDCS-09700296.text_edition: \n Deo Forti Phoebo Apollini Parthico Caius Cassius Vitalis |(centurio) legionis V Macedonicae piae constantis libens posuit\n
EDCS-09700296.translation: To the Strong God, Phoebus Apollo Parthicus, Gaius Cassius Vitalis, centurion of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, set this up willingly.
EDCS-09700296.summary: This is a dedication to the god Apollo made by Gaius Cassius Vitalis, a centurion of the Fifth Macedonian Legion.

EDCS-09700298.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Spediae Luci filiae Severae coniugi Luci Valeri Montani Quinti fili primi pili legionis XIII geminae Valeria Montanilla filia matri pientissimae et Tycheni libertae posuit\n
EDCS-09700298.translation: To the Divine Shades. Valeria Montanilla, daughter of Lucius Valerius Montanus, a primipilar officer of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, set this up for her most devoted mother, Spedia Severa, daughter of Lucius, and for her freedwoman Tycheni.
EDCS-09700298.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Valeria Montanilla for her mother and a freedwoman, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-09700299.translation: The Thirteenth Gemina Legion, Aurelius Menander.
EDCS-09700299.summary: This inscription simply records the name of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion and an individual named Aurelius Menander.

EDCS-09700318.text_edition: \n I legionis XXII Primigeniae to filio\n
EDCS-09700318.translation: Of the First legion of the Twenty-second Primigenia, to its son.
EDCS-09700318.summary: This inscription appears to be an dedication or dedication fragment from the First Cohort of the Twenty-second Primigenia Legion.

EDCS-09700703.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Cassius Severinus |(centurio) legionis V Macedonicae \n
EDCS-09700703.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Cassius Severinus, centurion of the Fifth Macedonian Legion.
EDCS-09700703.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Cassius Severinus, a centurion of the Fifth Macedonian Legion.

EDCS-09700705.text_edition: \n Iovi? Optimo? Maximo? Aeterno pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris subsub Primii Senecionis |(centurionis) legionis XIII geminae tabularium a solo restituit\n
EDCS-09700705.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Aeternus, for the safety of the Emperor Caesar, Primus Senecio, centurion of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, restored the record office from the ground up.
EDCS-09700705.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a record office for the health of the Emperor by Primus Senecio, a centurion of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion.

EDCS-09700706.text_edition: \n N E I Aelius Iulius Priscus Cassius Senecio Frontius Aquila Aurelius Iulianus Aelius Potens Valerius Vindex Aelius Sabinus Domitius Septimus Aelius Quimiber Iulius Ingenuus Antonius Aquilinus cohortis VII Sabinius Bithus Aelius Crispus Aurelius Terentianus Iulius Iustus S Aelius Dines Sabinius Victor Ulpius Secundus Pomp Ianuarius Maximius Cornelianus nnius Amu Iulius Severus Valerius Celsus Aurelius Batavus\n
EDCS-09700706.translation: Aelius Iulius Priscus, Cassius Senecio, Frontius Aquila, Aurelius Iulianus, Aelius Potens, Valerius Vindex, Aelius Sabinus, Domitius Septimus, Aelius Quimiber, Iulius Ingenuus, Antonius Aquilinus of the Seventh Cohort, Sabinius Bithus, Aelius Crispus, Aurelius Terentianus, Iulius Iustus, Aelius Dines, Sabinius Victor, Ulpius Secundus, Pomp(onius) Ianuarius, Maximius Cornelianus, Ammu(lius?) Iulius Severus, Valerius Celsus, Aurelius Batavus.
EDCS-09700706.summary: This lengthy inscription lists the names of numerous individuals, likely members of a military unit or civic group.

EDCS-09700709.text_edition: \n IarI Lucano veterano legionis X[III geminae ------\n
EDCS-09700709.translation: To Iar Iucanus, veteran of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion...
EDCS-09700709.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a veteran of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion.

EDCS-09700711.translation: The Thirteenth Gemina Legion, Aurelius Demetrius.
EDCS-09700711.summary: This inscription simply records the name of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion and an individual named Aurelius Demetrius.

EDCS-09700712.summary: This inscription likely records the name of Aurelius Godes, a soldier or individual associated with Legio XIII Gemina.

EDCS-09700713.summary: This inscription likely records the name of Flavius Martinus, a soldier or individual associated with Legio XIII.

EDCS-09700714.summary: This inscription records the name of Aurelius Menander, associated with the Legio XIII Gemina.

EDCS-09700715.summary: This inscription likely records the name of Lucretius Aquila, a soldier or individual associated with Legio XIII Gemina.

EDCS-09700716.summary: This inscription likely records the name of Annaei Saturnini, a soldier or individual associated with Legio XIII Gemina.

EDCS-09700717.translation: Aurelius Cono of Legio XIII Gemina.
EDCS-09700717.summary: This inscription records the name of Aurelius Cono, associated with Legio XIII Gemina.

EDCS-09700718.summary: This inscription records the name of Publius Aelius Terentius, likely a Roman citizen.

EDCS-09700722.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico sacrum Valerius Them veteranus decurio municipii votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09700722.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticus. Valerius Them, veteran decurion of the municipality, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09700722.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus Domesticus by Valerius Them, a veteran decurion of a municipality, who fulfilled his vow.

EDCS-09700723.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus us veteranus ex vixit annos LXXXV militavit Passer signifer vixit annos XXXIII Aelia vixit annos XVII Aurelius miles numeri Palmyrenorum Porolissensium vixit Aurelia Sabina et Aureliusus actarius \n
EDCS-09700723.translation: To the Divine Spirits. [---] veteran, lived 85 years, served as standard-bearer. Passer, lived 33 years. Aelia, lived 17 years. Aurelius miles, of the cohort of Palmyrenes of Porolissenses, lived [---]. Aurelia Sabina and Aurelius, scribe.
EDCS-09700723.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates several individuals, including a veteran, a standard-bearer named Passer, Aelia, Aurelius miles of the Palmyrene cohort at Porolissenses, Aurelia Sabina, and Aurelius the scribe, detailing their ages and service.

EDCS-09700725.text_edition: \n Bebeia Tyche VIS vixit annos Aelius Maenemachus |(centurio) coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-09700725.translation: Bebeia Tyche. Aelius Maenemachus, centurion, set this up for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-09700725.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Aelius Maenemachus, a centurion, for his wife Bebeia Tyche, who is noted to have lived [VI]S [years].

EDCS-09700726.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno pro salute domini nostri Marci Antoni Gordiani Pii Felicis Augusti et Sabiniae Trantquillinae Augustae Solinus Tertius? se ac suis v(otum) s(olvit) l(ibens) m(erito)\n
