EDCS-15500086.summary: This dedication records the municipality of Septimius Porolissum honoring Emperor Philippus Arabs, likely during his reign in the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-15500087.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Eufemus sic vikarius sic Peregrini vixit nnos XXXV Erastus conservo posuit\n
EDCS-15500087.translation: For Eufemus, who lived 35 years. Erastus, a fellow slave, placed this for his deserving fellow slave.
EDCS-15500087.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Eufemus and was erected by his fellow slave Erastus.

EDCS-15500089.text_edition: \n Marco Iulio Severo Philippo nobilissimo Caesari nostro res publica municipii Septimii Porolissensium\n
EDCS-15500089.translation: For Marcus Julius Severus Philippus, our most noble Caesar, the state of the municipality of Septimius Porolissum [dedicated this].
EDCS-15500089.summary: This dedication records the municipality of Septimius Porolissum honoring Marcus Julius Severus Philippus, likely a co-emperor or heir, during the reign of Philippus Arabs.

EDCS-15500092.text_edition: \n Deae Suriae Aurelius Gaianus decurio municipii Porolissensis sacerdos\n
EDCS-15500092.translation: For the goddess Syria, Aurelius Gaianus, decurion of the municipality of Porolissensis, priest.
EDCS-15500092.summary: This dedication records Aurelius Gaianus, a decurion and priest, honoring the goddess Syria.

EDCS-15500093.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus Felix Parthicus maximus pontifex maximus Brittanicus maximus tribunicia potestate XVI imperator II consul IIII pater patriae proconsul fecit\n
EDCS-15500093.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus Felix, conqueror of Parthia, Pontifex Maximus, conqueror of Britain, with tribunician power for the 16th time, Imperator for the 2nd time, Consul for the 4th time, Father of the Fatherland, Proconsul, built this.
EDCS-15500093.summary: This inscription records a public work built by Emperor Marcus Aurelius, likely during his reign between AD 161 and 180.

EDCS-15500094.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico Iulius Aemilius Felix votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15500094.translation: Julius Aemilius Felix willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Silvanus Domesticus.
EDCS-15500094.summary: This is a votive inscription by Julius Aemilius Felix dedicated to the god Silvanus Domesticus.

EDCS-15500095.text_edition: \n Fortunae Augustae Felix Augusti nostri vilicus sacrum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15500095.translation: Felix, steward of our Augustus, willingly and deservedly dedicated this sacred offering and vow to Fortuna Augusta.
EDCS-15500095.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Felix, a steward, to the imperial cult of Fortuna Augusta.

EDCS-15600958.text_edition: \n Tutor et Atticus Augustales coloniae votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-15600958.translation: The Augustales Tutor and Atticus of the colony willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-15600958.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Tutor and Atticus, members of the Augustales of a colony.

EDCS-15700116.text_edition: \n Marco Ulpio Apollinari praefecto castrorum legionis XIII geminae conscribti sic et cives Romani consistentes kannabis legionis eiusdem ex pecunia publica\n
EDCS-15700116.translation: The enrolled soldiers, as well as the Roman citizens residing in the camp of the 13th Gemina legion, from public funds, [dedicated this] to Marcus Ulpius Apollinaris, prefect of the camp of the same legion.
EDCS-15700116.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by soldiers and citizens of the 13th Gemina legion from public funds to their camp prefect, Marcus Ulpius Apollinaris.

EDCS-15800001.text_edition: \n Numini Aesculapi Caius Iulius Metrobianus coloniae Sarmizegetusae sacerdos dei eiusdem pontifex quinquennalis IIvir coloniae Apuli ex voto\n
EDCS-15800001.translation: Gaius Julius Metrobianus, priest of the same god and quinquennial duovir of the colony Apula of Sarmizegetusa, fulfilled his vow to the divine power of Aesculapius.
EDCS-15800001.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Gaius Julius Metrobianus, a priest and duovir, to the god Aesculapius in the colony of Sarmizegetusa (Apula).

EDCS-15800002.text_edition: \n Numini Serapis Gaius Iulius Metrobianus IIVIII coloniae Zarmizegetusae sacerdos dei Aesculapi po<n=T>tifex quinquennalis IIvir coloniae Apuli \n
EDCS-15800002.translation: Gaius Julius Metrobianus, priest of the god Aesculapius and quinquennial duovir of the colony Zarmizegetusa Apula, [dedicated this] to the divine power of Serapis.
EDCS-15800002.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Gaius Julius Metrobianus, a priest and duovir, to the god Serapis in the colony of Zarmizegetusa (Apula).

EDCS-15800003.text_edition: \n Numini Aesculapi et Hygiae Publius Aelius Genialis IIviralis coloniae Apulensis \n
EDCS-15800003.translation: Publius Aelius Genialis, duovir of the colony Apulensis, [dedicated this] to the divine power of Aesculapius and Hygieia.
EDCS-15800003.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Publius Aelius Genialis, a duovir, to the gods Aesculapius and Hygieia in the colony of Apulensis.

EDCS-15800004.text_edition: \n Aesculapio et Hygiae Publius Aelius Rufinus decurio municipii et patronus collegii fabrum coloniae Apulensis pro salute sua et Ulpiae Helpidiae coniugis et Aeli Urbani fili ex iusso sic fecit porticum per pedes XXXX\n
EDCS-15800004.translation: Publius Aelius Rufinus, decurion of the municipality and patron of the guild of craftsmen of the colony Apulensis, made this portico 40 feet long by order, for his own safety and that of his wife Ulpia Helpidia and his son Aelius Urbanus.
EDCS-15800004.summary: This inscription records the construction of a portico by Publius Aelius Rufinus, a decurion and patron, for his family's well-being.

EDCS-15800005.text_edition: \n Aesculapio et Hygiae Publius Aelius Syrus Augustalis municipii Septimii Apulensis et Valeria Severa coniux et Aelia Syra filia ex voto porticum per pedes XXX fecerunt\n
EDCS-15800005.translation: Publius Aelius Syrus, an Augustalis of the municipium Septimius Apulensis, and his wife Valeria Severa and their daughter Aelia Syra, made this portico 30 feet long in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800005.summary: This inscription records the construction of a portico by Publius Aelius Syrus, his wife, and daughter in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15800006.text_edition: \n Aesculapio et Hygiae sacrum Aelius Moschos votum solvit libens\n
EDCS-15800006.translation: Sacred to Aesculapius and Hygieia, Aelius Moschos willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800006.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Aelius Moschos to Aesculapius and Hygieia.

EDCS-15800007.text_edition: \n Numini Aesculapi et Hygiae pro salute dominorum nnostrorum Auggstorum Androclus ex dispensatore posuit\n
EDCS-15800007.translation: Androclus, formerly a steward, set this up to the divine power of Aesculapius and Hygieia for the welfare of our Lords Emperors.
EDCS-15800007.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Androclus to Aesculapius and Hygieia for the health of the emperors.

EDCS-15800008.text_edition: \n Deo Aesculapio et Hygiae Aurelius Aeternalis votum solvit\n
EDCS-15800008.translation: To the god Aesculapius and Hygieia, Aurelius Aeternalis willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800008.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Aurelius Aeternalis to Aesculapius and Hygieia.

EDCS-15800009.text_edition: \n Aesculapio et Hygiae pro salute sua suorumque Carpion Augusti libertus tabularius provincciae Apulensis\n
EDCS-15800009.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygieia, for his own health and that of his family, Carpion, freedman of Augustus and registrar of the province of Apulia.
EDCS-15800009.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Carpion, a freedman and registrar, to Aesculapius and Hygieia for his health and family.

EDCS-15800010.text_edition: \n Aesculapio et Hygiae Tiberius Claudius Valerianus (centurio) legionis XIII geminae et legionis I adiutricis voto posuit\n
EDCS-15800010.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygieia, Tiberius Claudius Valerianus, centurion of the Legio XIII Gemina and the Legio I Adjutrix, set this up by vow.
EDCS-15800010.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Tiberius Claudius Valerianus, a centurion, to Aesculapius and Hygieia.

EDCS-15800011.text_edition: \n Aesculapio et Hygiae Caius Pomponius Fuscus IIvir coloniae Apulensis votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-15800011.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygieia, Caius Pomponius Fuscus, duovir of the colony of Apulia, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800011.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Caius Pomponius Fuscus, a duovir, to Aesculapius and Hygieia.

EDCS-15800012.text_edition: \n Aesculapio et Hygiae Lucius Septimius Nigrinus patronus collegii fabrorum coloniae Apulensis pro salute sua et suorum posuit\n
EDCS-15800012.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygieia, Lucius Septimius Nigrinus, patron of the guild of builders of the colony of Apulia, set this up for his own health and that of his family.
EDCS-15800012.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Lucius Septimius Nigrinus, patron of a builders' guild, to Aesculapius and Hygieia for his family's health.

EDCS-15800013.text_edition: \n Numini Aesculapi et Hygiae Marcus Ulpius Valens IIII viralis municipii Septimii Apulensis pro salute sua suorumque votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-15800013.translation: To the divine power of Aesculapius and Hygieia, Marcus Ulpius Valens, one of the four chief magistrates of the municipium Septimium Apulense, willingly fulfilled his vow for his own health and that of his family.
EDCS-15800013.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Marcus Ulpius Valens, a magistrate, to Aesculapius and Hygieia for his health and family.

EDCS-15800015.text_edition: \n Auribus Aesculapi et Hygiae et Apollini et Dianae Cai Iulii Dius Farnax Irenicus fratres Augustales municipii Aureli Apuli pro salute sua posuerunt Praesente II et Condiano consulibus\n
EDCS-15800015.translation: To the ears of Aesculapius, Hygieia, Apollo, and Diana, Caius Julius Dius Farnax and Irenicus, brothers and Augustales of the municipium Aurelium Apuli, set this up for their own health, in the presence of Praesens and Condianus as consuls.
EDCS-15800015.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the brothers Caius Julius Dius Farnax and Irenicus to multiple deities for their health, dated by the consuls Praesens and Condianus.

EDCS-15800016.text_edition: \n Aesculapio et Hygiae ceterisque diis deabusque huiusque loci salutaribus Caius Iulius Frontonianus veteranus ex beneficiario consularis legionis V Macedonicae piae redditis sibi luminibus gratias agens ex viso pro se et Carteia Maxima coniuge et Iulia Frontina filia votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15800016.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygieia, and to all the other resident gods and goddesses beneficial to this place, Caius Julius Frontonianus, a veteran and formerly an orderly for a consular legate of Legio V Macedonica, giving thanks for the restoration of his sight as a result of a vision, willingly fulfilled his vow, deservedly, for himself, his wife Carteia Maxima, and his daughter Iulia Frontina.
EDCS-15800016.summary: This inscription records a votive offering of thanksgiving by Caius Julius Frontonianus, a veteran, to Aesculapius and Hygieia for the restoration of his sight, for himself, his wife, and daughter.

EDCS-15800017.text_edition: \n Virtutibus deii Aeterni Lucius Aquila Ambrosius posuit\n
EDCS-15800017.translation: Lucius Aquila Ambrosius erected this in honor of the virtues of the eternal gods.
EDCS-15800017.summary: Lucius Aquila Ambrosius erected this monument in honor of the virtues of the eternal gods.

EDCS-15800018.translation: Lucius Aquila Ambrosius for the eternal.
EDCS-15800018.summary: Lucius Aquila Ambrosius dedicated this, likely to an eternal deity.

EDCS-15800019.text_edition: \n Numini Apollini Marcus Aurelius Marinus veteranus legionis XIII geminae ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-15800019.translation: Marcus Aurelius Marinus, a veteran of the Thirteenth Gemina legion, erected this in fulfillment of a vow to the divine Apollo.
EDCS-15800019.summary: A veteran of the Thirteenth Gemina legion, Marcus Aurelius Marinus, fulfilled a vow to Apollo with this dedication.

EDCS-15800020.text_edition: \n Ex iussu dei Apollinis fontem Aeterni Ulpius Proculinus speculator legionis XIII geminae Gordianae a solo restituit\n
EDCS-15800020.translation: By order of the divine Apollo, Ulpius Proculinus, a scout of the Thirteenth Gemina Gordiana legion, restored the fountain of Aeternus from the ground up.
EDCS-15800020.summary: Ulpius Proculinus, a scout of the Thirteenth Gemina Gordiana legion, restored a fountain on the order of Apollo.

EDCS-15800021.text_edition: \n Deo Apollini praestantissimo Aurelius Vitalis pro se suisque voto libens posuit\n
EDCS-15800021.translation: Aurelius Vitalis willingly erected this as a vow for himself and his family to the most excellent god Apollo.
EDCS-15800021.summary: Aurelius Vitalis made a willing votive offering for himself and his family to Apollo.

EDCS-15800022.text_edition: \n Numini Caelesti Marcianus Augggustorum nnnostrorum verna librarius\n
EDCS-15800022.translation: Marcianus, a household slave and scribe, [dedicated this] to the Celestial deity and to our August emperors.
EDCS-15800022.summary: Marcianus, a slave and scribe, dedicated this to a Celestial deity and the reigning emperors.

EDCS-15800023.text_edition: \n Caelesti Augustae et Aesculapio Augusto et Genio Carthaginis et Genio Daciarum Olus sic Terentius Pudens Uttedianus legatus Auggustorum legionis XIII geminae legatus Auggustorum pro praetore provinciae Raetiae\n
EDCS-15800023.translation: Olus Sic Terentius Pudens Uttedianus, legate of the emperors and governor of the province of Raetia, [dedicated this] to the August Celestial goddess, to the August Asclepius, to the Genius of Carthage, and to the Genius of Dacia.
EDCS-15800023.summary: Olus Sic Terentius Pudens Uttedianus, a high-ranking official in the province of Raetia, dedicated this to several deities and the emperor.

EDCS-15800024.translation: Gaius Herennius Hermes [dedicated this] to Cautus in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800024.summary: Gaius Herennius Hermes made a votive offering to a deity named Cautus.

EDCS-15800025.text_edition: \n Daciis tribus et Genio legionis XIII geminae Marcus Caelius Iulianus tribunus laticlavius donum dedit\n
EDCS-15800025.translation: Marcus Caelius Iulianus, a tribune with the broad stripe, gave this offering to the Dacian tribes and to the Genius of the Thirteenth Gemina legion.
EDCS-15800025.summary: Marcus Caelius Iulianus, a high-ranking military officer, presented an offering to the Dacian tribes and the Genius of the Thirteenth Gemina legion.

EDCS-15800028.text_edition: \n Dianae sacrum Quintus Paetinius Senilis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15800028.translation: Sacred to Diana. Quintus Paetinius Senilis willingly fulfilled his vow, as was deserved.
EDCS-15800028.summary: Quintus Paetinius Senilis willingly and deservedly fulfilled a votive offering to Diana.

EDCS-15800029.text_edition: \n Dianae sacrum pro salute Auli Tapetii Antonini Tenax votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15800029.translation: Sacred to Diana; Tenax, fulfilling a vow for the health of Aulus Tapetius Antoninus, willingly and deservedly offered this.
EDCS-15800029.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Diana by Tenax for the health of Aulus Tapetius Antoninus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800030.text_edition: \n Dianae sacrum COMMUN Veteranus ULP miles legionis XIII geminae et C filius\n
EDCS-15800030.translation: Sacred to Diana. Commun Veteranus, a soldier of the 13th Legion Gemina, and his son C., offered this.
EDCS-15800030.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Diana by Commun Veteranus, a soldier of the 13th Legion Gemina, and his son, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800031.text_edition: \n Dianae Augustae Aelius Iulianus Augustalis coloniae Apulensis votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-15800031.translation: Sacred to Augusta Diana. Aelius Iulianus Augustalis of the colony of Apula willingly offered this votive gift.
EDCS-15800031.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Augusta Diana by Aelius Iulianus Augustalis of Apula, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800032.translation: Sacred to Diana Mellifica. Comatius Super offered this.
EDCS-15800032.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Diana Mellifica (the Honey-making Diana) by Comatius Super, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800033.text_edition: \n Dianae Reginae Caius Valerius Sarapio votum libens posuit\n
EDCS-15800033.translation: To Diana Regina. Gaius Valerius Sarapio willingly erected this.
EDCS-15800033.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument to Diana Regina by Gaius Valerius Sarapio, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800034.text_edition: \n Domino Aeterno pro salute Imperatoris Nervae Traiani Caesaris Augusti Germanici Dacici Lucius Antonius Apollinaris veteranus legionis I adiutricis piae fidelis\n
EDCS-15800034.translation: To the Eternal Lord, for the health of the Emperor Nerva Trajan Caesar Augustus Germanicus Dacicus. Lucius Antonius Apollinaris, a veteran of the 1st Adjutrix Pia Fidelis Legion, offered this.
EDCS-15800034.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Eternal Lord by Lucius Antonius Apollinaris, a veteran of the 1st Adjutrix Pia Fidelis Legion, for the health of Emperor Trajan, dating to his reign (98-117 AD).

EDCS-15800035.text_edition: \n Mestrius Martinus pictor constituit pro salute sua et suorum fanum Dominarum\n
EDCS-15800035.translation: Mestrius Martinus, a painter, established this shrine to the Goddesses for the health of himself and his family.
EDCS-15800035.summary: This inscription records the establishment of a shrine to the Dominas (Goddesses) by the painter Mestrius Martinus for the well-being of his family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800036.text_edition: \n Fortunae Augustae sacrum Publius Aelius Gemellus vir clarissimus perfecto a solo balneo consacravit\n
EDCS-15800036.translation: Sacred to Fortuna Augusta. Publius Aelius Gemellus, a most distinguished man, consecrated this bathhouse, built from the ground up.
EDCS-15800036.summary: This inscription records the consecration of a newly built bathhouse to Fortuna Augusta by Publius Aelius Gemellus, a prominent man, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800037.text_edition: \n Fortunae Augustae sacrum Publius Calpurnius Proculus legatus Auggustorum pro praetore\n
EDCS-15800037.translation: Sacred to Fortuna Augusta. Publius Calpurnius Proculus, Legate of the Augusti, proconsul.
EDCS-15800037.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Fortuna Augusta by Publius Calpurnius Proculus, a high-ranking imperial legate and proconsul, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800038.text_edition: \n Fortunae Augustae sacrum et Genio canabensium Lucius Silius Maximus veteranus legionis I adiutricis piae fidelis magistrans primus in canabis donum dedit et Silia Ianuaria et Silius Firminus\n
EDCS-15800038.translation: Sacred to Fortuna Augusta and the Genius of the camp dwellers. Lucius Silius Maximus, a veteran of the 1st Adjutrix Pia Fidelis Legion, first official in the camp, gave this gift, along with Silia Ianuaria and Silius Firminus.
EDCS-15800038.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Fortuna Augusta and the Genius of the camp dwellers by Lucius Silius Maximus, a veteran and camp official, with contributions from Silia Ianuaria and Silius Firminus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800039.text_edition: \n Fortunae bonae domesticae sacrum Quintus Paetinius Senilis votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-15800039.translation: Sacred to good domestic Fortune, Quintus Paetinius Senilis willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800039.summary: This inscription records Quintus Paetinius Senilis fulfilling a vow to the goddess Fortuna domestica, likely a private dedication.

EDCS-15800040.text_edition: \n Fortunae Publicae Vitalis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15800040.translation: Vitalis willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Public Fortune.
EDCS-15800040.summary: This inscription records Vitalis fulfilling a vow to the goddess Fortuna Publica, a public deity.

EDCS-15800041.text_edition: \n Fortunae Reduci Quintus Caecilius Laetus legatus Augusti legionis XIII geminae votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-15800041.translation: Quintus Caecilius Laetus, legate of the Emperor and of the 13th Gemina Legion, willingly fulfilled his vow to Fortune the Returner.
EDCS-15800041.summary: This inscription records Quintus Caecilius Laetus, a high-ranking military official, dedicating a vow to Fortuna Redux, the goddess of safe return.

EDCS-15800042.text_edition: \n Genio legionis XIII geminae Quintus Caecilius Laetus legatus legionis eiusdem votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-15800042.translation: Quintus Caecilius Laetus, legate of the same legion, willingly fulfilled his vow to the Genius of the 13th Gemina Legion.
EDCS-15800042.summary: This inscription records Quintus Caecilius Laetus dedicating a vow to the protective spirit (Genius) of the 13th Gemina Legion.

EDCS-15800043.text_edition: \n Soli Invicto Quintus Caecilius Laetus legatus Augusti legionis XIII geminae votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-15800043.translation: Quintus Caecilius Laetus, legate of the Emperor and of the 13th Gemina Legion, willingly fulfilled his vow to the Unconquered Sun.
EDCS-15800043.summary: This inscription records Quintus Caecilius Laetus dedicating a vow to Sol Invictus, the unconquered sun god.

EDCS-15800045.translation: Tiberius Claudius Mansuetus, optio (officer), to the Genius of the century.
EDCS-15800045.summary: This inscription records Tiberius Claudius Mansuetus, a military officer, dedicating something to the Genius of his century.

EDCS-15800046.text_edition: \n Pro salute Auggustorum Genio fabrum Marcus Aurelius Timon Augustalis coloniae Apulensis et magister collegii supra scripti pecunia sua fecit loco dato decurionum decreto\n
EDCS-15800046.translation: For the safety of the Emperors, Marcus Aurelius Timon, Augustalis of the colony of Apula and master of the aforementioned college, made this at his own expense on land granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-15800046.summary: This inscription records Marcus Aurelius Timon dedicating a monument for the safety of the Emperors, funded by himself and approved by the local council.

EDCS-15800047.text_edition: \n Genio Imperatoris Gordiani Pii Felicis invicti Augusti Petronius Polianus vir clarissimus legatus legionis XIII geminae Gordianae legatus Augusti pro praetore provinciae Belgicae\n
EDCS-15800047.translation: Petronius Polianus, a most distinguished man, legate of the 13th Gordian Gemina Legion, legate of the Emperor with proconsular power in the province of Belgica, to the Genius of Emperor Gordian Pius Felix Invictus Augustus.
EDCS-15800047.summary: This inscription records Petronius Polianus, a high-ranking imperial legate in Belgica, dedicating something to the Genius of Emperor Gordian III.

EDCS-15800048.text_edition: \n Genio loci et Fortunae? magnae Q(uintus) Appianius? Maternus pro se et Marco Vettio Bellatori ex iusso sic libens merito posuit\n
EDCS-15800048.translation: Quintus Appianius? Maternus? placed this willingly and deservedly, by order, for himself and for Marcus Vettius Bellator, to the Genius of the place and perhaps great Fortune.
EDCS-15800048.summary: This inscription records Quintus Appianius? Maternus? dedicating a monument to the Genius loci and possibly Fortuna Magna, on behalf of himself and another person.

EDCS-15800049.text_edition: \n Genio praetorii huius Marcus Valerius Longinus vir clarissimus legatus legionis XIII geminae Severianae cum suis votum solvit\n
EDCS-15800049.translation: Marcus Valerius Longinus, a most distinguished man, legate of the 13th Severian Gemina Legion, fulfilled his vow with his men to the Genius of this praetorium.
EDCS-15800049.summary: This inscription records Marcus Valerius Longinus, a high-ranking military legate, dedicating a vow to the Genius of the legion's headquarters (praetorium).

EDCS-15800050.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori Marcus Valerius Longinus vir clarissimus legatus legionis XIII geminae Severianae cum suis ex voto\n
EDCS-15800050.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Protector, Marcus Valerius Longinus, a most distinguished man, legate of the Thirteenth Gemina Severiana legion, with his own (household), from a vow.
EDCS-15800050.summary: This is an altar dedicated to Jupiter Best and Greatest by Marcus Valerius Longinus, a legionary legate, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15800051.text_edition: \n Glyconi Marcus Antonius Onesas iusso dei libens posuit\n
EDCS-15800051.translation: To Glycon, Marcus Antonius Onesas erected this willingly, by the god's command.
EDCS-15800051.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the god Glycon, dedicated by Marcus Antonius Onesas.

EDCS-15800052.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Iunoni Reginae Titus Claudius Anicetus Augustalis coloniae Sarmizegetusae templum vetustate conlapsum a solo restituit\n
EDCS-15800052.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and Juno Regina, Titus Claudius Anicetus, an Augustalis of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, restored the temple, fallen from its foundations due to age.
EDCS-15800052.summary: This is a dedication by Titus Claudius Anicetus, an official of Sarmizegetusa, who rebuilt a temple to Jupiter and Juno.

EDCS-15800053.text_edition: \n Glyconi Marcus Aurelius Theodotus iusso dei posuit\n
EDCS-15800053.translation: To Glycon, Marcus Aurelius Theodotus erected this, by the god's command.
EDCS-15800053.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the god Glycon, dedicated by Marcus Aurelius Theodotus.

EDCS-15800055.text_edition: \n Deo Herculi Iulia Maximina voti sui compos\n
EDCS-15800055.translation: To the god Hercules, Julia Maximina, fulfilling her vow.
EDCS-15800055.summary: This is a dedication to the god Hercules by Julia Maximina, who is fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-15800056.text_edition: \n Herculi Augusto sacrum Aurelius Vetustinus votum solvit\n
EDCS-15800056.translation: Sacred to Hercules Augustus. Aurelius Vetustinus paid his vow.
EDCS-15800056.summary: This inscription is a sacred dedication to Hercules Augustus, with Aurelius Vetustinus fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-15800057.text_edition: \n Herculi conservatori Geminius Aelianus beneficiarius legati votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-15800057.translation: To Hercules the Protector, Geminius Aelianus, a beneficiary of the legate, willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-15800057.summary: This inscription records Geminius Aelianus, a subordinate to a legate, dedicating an offering to Hercules the Protector.

EDCS-15800058.text_edition: \n Herculi conservatori Marcus Lucilius Threptio donum dedit amicis\n
EDCS-15800058.translation: To Hercules the Protector, Marcus Lucilius Threptio gave this gift to his friends.
EDCS-15800058.summary: This inscription records Marcus Lucilius Threptio dedicating a gift to Hercules the Protector for his friends.

EDCS-15800059.text_edition: \n Herculi Invicto pro salute Cai Iuli Metrobiani et Aeliae Bonae coniugis Adamas ex voto\n
EDCS-15800059.translation: To Hercules the Unconquered, for the health of Caius Julius Metrobianus and Aelia Bona his wife, Adamas, from a vow.
EDCS-15800059.summary: This is a votive inscription to Hercules the Unconquered, dedicated by Adamas for the well-being of Caius Julius Metrobianus and his wife.

EDCS-15800060.text_edition: \n Herculi Invicto pro salute Iuliorum Callistus eorum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15800060.translation: To Hercules the Unconquered, for the health of the Iulii, Callistus paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-15800060.summary: This inscription records Callistus dedicating an offering to Hercules the Unconquered for the health of members of the Iulii family.

EDCS-15800061.text_edition: \n Iunoni Reginae Caius Aelius Primus decurio coloniae\n
EDCS-15800061.translation: To Juno Regina, Gaius Aelius Primus, decurion of the colony, [dedicated this].
EDCS-15800061.summary: A dedication to Juno Regina by Gaius Aelius Primus, a decurion of a colony, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-15800062.text_edition: \n Iovi Maximo Crispius Saturninus beneficiarius legati voto libens posuit\n
EDCS-15800062.translation: Crispius Saturninus, an official attached to a legate, willingly set this up to Jupiter Best and Greatest in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800062.summary: A votive inscription to Jupiter Best and Greatest by Crispius Saturninus, a soldier serving as a beneficiarius to a legate, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-15800063.text_edition: \n Iovi Monitori Conservatorique [] legatus Augusti legionis XIII geminae somno monitus\n
EDCS-15800063.translation: To Jupiter the Monitor and Protector, the legate of Augustus of the Legio XIII Gemina, warned in a dream, [dedicated this].
EDCS-15800063.summary: A dedication to Jupiter as Monitor and Protector, by the legate of Legio XIII Gemina, inspired by a dream, probably from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-15800064.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Bussumaro Atpatinius Rufi votum libens solvit merito\n
EDCS-15800064.translation: Bussumarus Atpatinius Rufus willingly paid his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest as deserved.
EDCS-15800064.summary: A vow fulfillment to Jupiter Best and Greatest by Bussumarus Atpatinius Rufus, an individual, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-15800065.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Gaius miles legionis XIII geminae votum solvit\n
EDCS-15800065.translation: Publius Aelius Gaius, a soldier of Legio XIII Gemina, fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-15800065.summary: A votive fulfillment by Publius Aelius Gaius, a soldier of Legio XIII Gemina, to Jupiter Best and Greatest, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-15800066.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Publi Aelii Caesidius et Iulianus cum operariis suis voto libentes posuerunt\n
EDCS-15800066.translation: Publius Aelius Caesidius and Iulianus, with their workmen, willingly set this up to Jupiter Best and Greatest in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800066.summary: A collective votive dedication to Jupiter Best and Greatest by Publius Aelius Caesidius and Iulianus, along with their workmen, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-15800067.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Aelius Fidus pro suis votum solvit\n
EDCS-15800067.translation: Lucius Aelius Fidus fulfilled his vow for himself and his family to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-15800067.summary: A votive fulfillment by Lucius Aelius Fidus for himself and his family to Jupiter Best and Greatest, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-15800068.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Avonius Tertullus veteranus legionis XIII geminae [Ale]xandr[ianae] votum solvit\n
EDCS-15800068.translation: Gaius Avonius Tertullus, a veteran of Legio XIII Gemina Alexandriana, fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-15800068.summary: A vow fulfillment to Jupiter Best and Greatest by Gaius Avonius Tertullus, a veteran of Legio XIII Gemina Alexandriana, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-15800069.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Aurelius Crescentinus immunis legionis XIII geminae Antoninianae pro se et suos posuit\n
EDCS-15800069.translation: Marcus Aurelius Crescentinus, a privileged soldier of Legio XIII Gemina Antoniniana, set this up for himself and his dependents in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800069.summary: A votive dedication by Marcus Aurelius Crescentinus, an immunis of Legio XIII Gemina Antoniniana, for himself and his family, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-15800070.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo S--- Caecilius Saturninus et Iulius Rufus beneficiarii consulares votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-15800070.translation: S--- Caecilius Saturninus and Iulius Rufus, consular officials, willingly and deservedly paid their vows.
EDCS-15800070.summary: A collective vow fulfillment to Jupiter Best and Greatest by S--- Caecilius Saturninus and Iulius Rufus, beneficiarii consulares, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-15800071.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Sextus mmaps Clemens beneficiarius consularis\n
EDCS-15800071.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Sextus Memaps Clemens, aide-de-camp to the consular governor.
EDCS-15800071.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter by Sextus Memaps Clemens, an aide-de-camp, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-15800072.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Cornelius Primus veteranus legionis XIII geminae quot votum fecit pro se et suorumque posuit\n
EDCS-15800072.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Cornelius Primus, a veteran of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, made this offering for himself and his family, having fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800072.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter made by Cornelius Primus, a veteran of Legio XIII Gemina, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800073.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Flavius Silvanus et Titus Flavius Iustinus milites legionis XIII geminae votum solverunt libentes merito Victorino consulari\n
EDCS-15800073.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Titus Flavius Silvanus and Titus Flavius Iustinus, soldiers of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vow to Victorinus, the consular governor.
EDCS-15800073.summary: This inscription records soldiers Titus Flavius Silvanus and Titus Flavius Iustinus of Legio XIII Gemina fulfilling a vow to their consular governor, Victorinus, likely during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800074.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute imperii et collegii fabrum Tiberius Iulius Bubalus ex decuria XI donum dedit\n
EDCS-15800074.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, for the safety of the empire and of the guild of craftsmen, Tiberius Iulius Bubalus from the eleventh century gave this gift.
EDCS-15800074.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter by Tiberius Iulius Bubalus for the welfare of the empire and a guild of craftsmen, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800075.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Iulius Celer Isauria |(centurio) legionis IIII Scythicae et XVI Flaviae firmae et XIII geminae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15800075.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Caius Iulius Celer, a centurion of the Fourth Scythica Legion, the Sixteenth Flavia Firma, and the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800075.summary: This inscription records Caius Iulius Celer, a centurion serving in three legions (Legio IV Scythica, Legio XVI Flavia Firma, and Legio XIII Gemina), fulfilling a vow to Jupiter, likely during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800076.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iulius Primus |(centurio) legionis XIII geminae votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-15800076.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Iulius Primus, a centurion of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, gladly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800076.summary: This inscription records Iulius Primus, a centurion of Legio XIII Gemina, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter with joy, likely during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800077.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iustia Secundina ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-15800077.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Iustia Secundina set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800077.summary: This inscription records Iustia Secundina dedicating a monument to Jupiter in fulfillment of a vow, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-15800078.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marius Cerdo votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15800078.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marius Cerdo gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800078.summary: This inscription records Marius Cerdo fulfilling a vow to Jupiter, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-15800079.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Omulius Crescens votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15800079.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Omulius Crescens gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800079.summary: This inscription records Omulius Crescens fulfilling a vow to Jupiter, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-15800080.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Marcus Postumius Tertius\n
EDCS-15800080.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Postumius Tertius.
EDCS-15800080.summary: This inscription records Marcus Postumius Tertius dedicating something sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-15800081.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Sempronius Valentinus beneficiarius legati votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-15800081.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Sempronius Valentinus, aide to the legate, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800081.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by Sempronius Valentinus, an aide to a legate, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-15800082.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Sentius Anicetus decurio coloniae Sarmizegetusae patronus collegii fabrum primus municipii Septimii Apulensis votum solvit libens merito Auggustis nnostris Imperatoribus Antonino II et [Geta] consulibus (ante diem) X Kalendas Iunias luna XVIII die Iovis\n
EDCS-15800082.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Caius Sentius Anicetus, decurion of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, patron of the guild of smiths of the municipality of Septimius Apulensis, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to our August Emperors, Antoninus II and Geta, consuls, on the 10th day before the Kalends of June, the 18th day of the moon, on a Thursday.
EDCS-15800082.summary: This votive inscription to Jupiter was made by Caius Sentius Anicetus, a prominent local official and patron of a guild, and is precisely dated to June 22, 209 CE.

EDCS-15800083.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Septimius Pius miles legionis XIII geminae ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-15800083.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Septimius Pius, a soldier of the Legion XIII Gemina, set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800083.summary: This votive inscription to Jupiter was set up by Septimius Pius, a soldier of the Legion XIII Gemina, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-15800084.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Terentius Iuvenalis NV \n
EDCS-15800084.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Terentius Iuvenalis.
EDCS-15800084.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter by Terentius Iuvenalis, with the latter part of his name or title illegible, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-15800085.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Gaius Valerius Sarapio a Carpis liberatus pro salute sua et suorum voto libens posuit\n
EDCS-15800085.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Gaius Valerius Sarapio, freed from Carpis, willingly set this up in fulfillment of a vow for his own safety and that of his family.
EDCS-15800085.summary: This votive inscription to Jupiter was made by Gaius Valerius Sarapio, who was freed from Carpis, for his own welfare and that of his family, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-15800086.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute CII Caius Valerius Secundus donum dedit\n
EDCS-15800086.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, for the safety of 102 people, Gaius Valerius Secundus gave this offering.
EDCS-15800086.summary: This inscription records an offering made to Jupiter by Gaius Valerius Secundus for the well-being of 102 individuals, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-15800087.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Lucius Veratius Speratus beneficiarius praefecti legionis XIII geminae\n
EDCS-15800087.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest, Lucius Veratius Speratus, aide to the prefect of Legion XIII Gemina.
EDCS-15800087.summary: This inscription records Lucius Veratius Speratus, an aide to the prefect of Legion XIII Gemina, dedicating something to Jupiter, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-15800088.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo ex visu Sex(us) Virius - f(ilius) Postumus votum solvit\n
EDCS-15800088.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Sex(us) Virius Postumus, son of [---], willingly fulfilled his vow after a divine vision.
EDCS-15800088.summary: This votive inscription to Jupiter was made by Sexus Virius Postumus after a divine vision, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-15800089.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Victorinus legionis XIII geminae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15800089.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Victorinus of Legion XIII Gemina willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800089.summary: This votive inscription to Jupiter was made by Victorinus, a soldier of Legion XIII Gemina, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-15800090.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo T AMUS Vindex benef(iciarius) consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15800090.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, T. Amus Vindex, aide to the consular governor, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800090.summary: This votive inscription to Jupiter was made by T. Amus Vindex, an aide to a consular governor, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-15800091.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo ceterisque diis deabusque immortalibus Titus Iulius Gaianus decurio coloniae Sarmizegetusae pro salute sua suorumque omnium posuit\n
EDCS-15800091.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, and to all other immortal gods and goddesses, Titus Julius Gaianus, a decurion of the colony of Sarmizegetusa, erected this for the safety of himself and all his family.
EDCS-15800091.summary: An offering dedicated to Jupiter and other deities by Titus Julius Gaianus, a decurion of Sarmizegetusa, for his well-being and that of his family.

EDCS-15800092.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et consessui deorum dearumque pro salute imperii Romani et virtute legionis XIII geminae sub Marco Statio Prisco consule designato demonstrantibus ipsis aquas aperiendas per Lucium Aurelium Trophimum ponentem signum Iovis et aram pecunia sua fecit\n
EDCS-15800092.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to the assembly of gods and goddesses, for the safety of the Roman Empire and the valor of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, under the consul-designate Marcus Stasius Priscus, demonstrating the need for opening the waters, Lucius Aurelius Trophimus, who erected the statue of Jupiter and the altar, made this at his own expense.
EDCS-15800092.summary: A dedication to Jupiter and other gods, made by Lucius Aurelius Trophimus at his own expense, for the safety of the Roman Empire and the Thirteenth Legion, during the consulship of Marcus Stasius Priscus.

EDCS-15800093.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo ceteris dis deabusque us Pus(?) legatus Augusti legionis XIII geminae votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-15800093.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to all other gods and goddesses, he, Pus(?), legate of the Emperor of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800093.summary: A vow fulfillment to Jupiter and other deities by Pus(?), a legate of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.

EDCS-15800094.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et ceteris diis deabusque immortalibus et Daciae pro salute domini nostri Marci Aurelii Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti nostri Caius Aurelius Sigillius tribunus legionis XIII geminae Antoninianae pridie Nonas Apriles Laeto II et Cerialae consulibus\n
EDCS-15800094.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, and to all other immortal gods and goddesses, and to Dacia, for the safety of our lord Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, Caius Aurelius Sigillius, tribune of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina Antoniniana, on the day before the Nones of April, with Laetus II and Cerialis as consuls.
EDCS-15800094.summary: A dedication to Jupiter, other deities, and Dacia by Caius Aurelius Sigillius, a tribune of the Thirteenth Legion, for the safety of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, during the consulship of Laetus II and Cerialis.

EDCS-15800095.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo dis et deabus Tiberius Claudius Rufus flamen coloniae\n
EDCS-15800095.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, and to the gods and goddesses, Tiberius Claudius Rufus, flamen of the colony.
EDCS-15800095.summary: An offering to Jupiter and other deities by Tiberius Claudius Rufus, flamen of the colony.

EDCS-15800096.text_edition: \n Libero Patri Tiberius Claudius Rufus decurio coloniae et flamen municipii Apulensis\n
EDCS-15800096.translation: To Liber Pater, Tiberius Claudius Rufus, decurion of the colony and flamen of the municipality of Apulum.
EDCS-15800096.summary: An inscription honoring Liber Pater by Tiberius Claudius Rufus, identified as both a decurion and a flamen in Apulum.

EDCS-15800097.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo ceterisque diis Caius? Pescennius Niger? consularis\n
EDCS-15800097.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to all other gods, Caius(?) Pescennius Niger(?), consular governor.
EDCS-15800097.summary: A dedication to Jupiter and other gods by Caius(?) Pescennius Niger(?), a former consular governor.

EDCS-15800098.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Pomponius Victorinus veteranus ex duplario legionis XIII geminae voto libens posuit\n
EDCS-15800098.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to Juno, Pomponius Victorinus, a veteran formerly a duplicarius of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, willingly erected this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800098.summary: A votive offering to Jupiter and Juno by Pomponius Victorinus, a veteran of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.

EDCS-15800099.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Titus Aurelius Narcissus negotiator pro se et suorum libens merito votum solvit\n
EDCS-15800099.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to Juno Regina, Titus Aurelius Narcissus, a merchant, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and his family.
EDCS-15800099.summary: A vow fulfillment to Jupiter and Juno Regina by Titus Aurelius Narcissus, a merchant, for himself and his family.

EDCS-15800100.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Iunoni Reginae pro salute Imperatoris Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Augusti et Iuliae Augustae matris Augusti Marcus Ulpius Mucianus miles legionis XIII geminae horologiarium templum a solo ex voto fecit Falcone et Claro consulibus\n
EDCS-15800100.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to Juno Regina, for the safety of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus and Julia Augusta, mother of the Emperor, Marcus Ulpius Mucianus, a soldier of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, built a temple with a clock from the ground up according to his vow, during the consulship of Falcon and Clarus.
EDCS-15800100.summary: Marcus Ulpius Mucianus, a soldier of the Thirteenth Legion, built a temple with a clock from the ground up as a votive offering for the safety of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus and Julia Augusta, during the consulship of Falcon and Clarus.

EDCS-15800101.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Minervae Lucius Annius Italicus Honoratus legatus Augusti legionis XIII geminae Antoninianae praefectus aerarii militaris sodalis Hadrianalis cum Gavidia Torquata sua et Anniis Italico et Honorato et Italica filiis\n
EDCS-15800101.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Juno Regina, and Minerva. Lucius Annius Italicus Honoratus, legate of the emperor of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion Antoniniana, prefect of the military treasury, and member of the Hadrianales sodality, with his wife Gavidia Torquata and his sons Annius Italicus, Annius Honoratus, and Annius Italica.
EDCS-15800101.summary: This is a dedication to the Capitoline Triad by Lucius Annius Italicus Honoratus, a high-ranking military official, and his family.

EDCS-15800102.text_edition: \n Victoriae Antonini Augusti Lucius Annius Italicus Honoratus legatus Augusti legionis XIII geminae Antoninianae praefectus aerarii militaris sodalis Hadrianali cum Gavidia Torquata sua et Anniis Italico et Honorato et Italica filiis\n
EDCS-15800102.translation: To Victoria of Antoninus Augustus. Lucius Annius Italicus Honoratus, legate of the emperor of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion Antoniniana, prefect of the military treasury, and member of the Hadrianales sodality, with his wife Gavidia Torquata and his sons Annius Italicus, Annius Honoratus, and Annius Italica.
EDCS-15800102.summary: This is a dedication to Victoria by Lucius Annius Italicus Honoratus, a high-ranking military official, and his family.

EDCS-15800104.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Caerellius Sabinus legatus Augusti legionis XIII geminae et Fufidia Pollitta eius voto\n
EDCS-15800104.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Gaius Caerellius Sabinus, legate of the emperor of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, and his wife Fufidia Pollitta, fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-15800104.summary: This is a votive offering to Jupiter by Gaius Caerellius Sabinus, a Roman legate, and his wife.

EDCS-15800105.text_edition: \n Iunoni Reginae Populoniae deae patriae Caius Caerellius Sabinus legatus Augusti legionis XIII geminae et Fufidia Pollitta eius voto\n
EDCS-15800105.translation: To Juno Regina Populonia, the native goddess. Gaius Caerellius Sabinus, legate of the emperor of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, and his wife Fufidia Pollitta, fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-15800105.summary: This is a votive offering to Juno Regina Populonia by Gaius Caerellius Sabinus, a Roman legate, and his wife.

EDCS-15800106.text_edition: \n Minervae Iovis consiliorum participi Caius Caerellius Sabinus legatus Augusti legionis XIII geminae et Fufidia Pollitta eius voto\n
EDCS-15800106.translation: To Minerva, participant in Jupiter's counsels. Gaius Caerellius Sabinus, legate of the emperor of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, and his wife Fufidia Pollitta, fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-15800106.summary: This is a votive offering to Minerva by Gaius Caerellius Sabinus, a Roman legate, and his wife.

EDCS-15800107.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Minervae Quintus Cornelius Silvanus |(centurio) legionis V Macedonicae piae constantis votum solvit\n
EDCS-15800107.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Juno Regina, and Minerva. Quintus Cornelius Silvanus, centurion of the Fifth Macedonian Legion Pia Constans, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800107.summary: This is a votive offering to the Capitoline Triad by Quintus Cornelius Silvanus, a centurion of the Fifth Macedonian Legion.

EDCS-15800108.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Minervae veterani legionis XIII geminae missi honesta missione per Iulium Bassum legatum Augusti pro praetore Idibus Decembribus Pontiano et Atiliano consulibus milites facti \n
EDCS-15800108.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Juno Regina, and Minerva. Veterans of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, honorably discharged and enrolled as soldiers on the Ides of December in the consulship of Pontianus and Atilanus, through Julius Bassus, legate of the emperor pro praetore.
EDCS-15800108.summary: This inscription records a vow made by veterans of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion on the occasion of their honorable discharge and enrollment, under the legateship of Julius Bassus.

EDCS-15800109.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Minervae et Aesculapio domino Septimius Asclepius Hermes libertus numinis Aesculapi habens ornamenta decurionalia coloniae Apulensis et Augustalis coloniae eiusdem voto posuit\n
EDCS-15800109.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Juno, Minerva, and Aesculapius the lord. Septimius Asclepius Hermes, freedman of the divine Aesculapius, holding decurional insignia of the colony of Apula and of its colony Augustalis, set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800109.summary: This is a votive offering to several deities by Septimius Asclepius Hermes, a freedman with local civic honors in the colony of Apula.

EDCS-15800110.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Marti Lucius Arrius Probus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15800110.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to Mars. Lucius Arrius Probus, a beneficiary of the consular legate, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800110.summary: This is a votive offering to Jupiter and Mars by Lucius Arrius Probus, who held a specific privileged position under a consular legate.

EDCS-15800111.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Dis Penatibus Scaurianus\n
EDCS-15800111.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to the Household Gods. Scaurianus.
EDCS-15800111.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter and the Penates by Scaurianus.

EDCS-15800112.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aeterno Titus Flavius Flavianus Augustalis municipii Septimii Apulensis et decurio collegii fabrum una cum Aelia Vindia coniuge sua quod pro se et Flaviis Restuto Flavino Ianuario Flaviano filiis suis vovetant votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-15800112.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, the Eternal. Titus Flavius Flavianus Augustalis of the municipium of Septimius Apulensis and a decurion of the guild of craftsmen, together with his wife Aelia Vindia, because they had vowed for themselves and their sons Flavius Restutus, Flavius Ianuarius, and Flavius, have gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-15800112.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to Jupiter by Titus Flavius Flavianus, an official and decurion, and his wife Aelia Vindia, for themselves and their sons, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800113.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aeterno Caius Iulius Valentinus IIIIvir primus annualis municipii Septimii Apulensis et patronus collegii fabrum municipii supra scripti ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-15800113.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, the Eternal. Caius Julius Valentinus, first annual chief magistrate of the municipium of Septimius Apulensis and patron of the aforementioned guild of craftsmen, set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800113.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to Jupiter by Caius Julius Valentinus, a high-ranking official and patron of a guild, for the municipium of Septimius Apulensis, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800114.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori Lucius Antonius Secundus Augustalis coloniae Apulensis in his aedibus natus cum Celsina filia votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15800114.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, the Preserver. Lucius Antonius Secundus Augustalis of the colony of Apulensis, born in these buildings, together with his daughter Celsina, has gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15800114.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to Jupiter by Lucius Antonius Secundus, an Augustalis of the colony of Apulensis, and his daughter Celsina, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800115.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori Callistus Augusti nostri verna dispensator et Cornelia eius pro se et suis\n
EDCS-15800115.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, the Preserver. Callistus, a servant (verna) and steward (dispensator) of our Emperor, and Cornelia his wife, for themselves and their family.
EDCS-15800115.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter by Callistus, an imperial servant and steward, and his wife Cornelia, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800116.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo divo fulgurali sacrum Iuliano et Crispino consulibus consularis\n
EDCS-15800116.translation: Sacred to Jupiter, Best and Greatest, the Thunder-God. In the consulship of Iulianus and Crispinus.
EDCS-15800116.summary: This inscription is a sacred dedication to Jupiter, the Thunder-God, dated by the consuls Iulianus and Crispinus, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800117.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Statori Caius Valerius Valerianus ex visu posuit\n
EDCS-15800117.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, the Stayer. Caius Valerius Valerianus set this up as a result of a vision.
EDCS-15800117.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter, the Stayer, by Caius Valerius Valerianus, who set it up after a divine vision, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800118.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Taviano et dis deabusque pro salute et victoria domini nostri sanctissimi Tavianus Augusti libertus sub procurator aurariarum votum solvit animo libenter\n
EDCS-15800118.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Tavius and the gods and goddesses. For the safety and victory of our most sacred lord Tavianus, freedman of Augustus, procurator of the gold mines, has gladly fulfilled his vow with a willing spirit.
EDCS-15800118.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to Jupiter and other deities by Tavianus, an imperial freedman and procurator, for the safety and victory of the Emperor, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800119.text_edition: \n Iovi Statori Quintus Aburnius Caedicianus legatus Augusti\n
EDCS-15800119.translation: To Jupiter, the Stayer. Quintus Aburnius Caedicianus, legate of Augustus.
EDCS-15800119.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter, the Stayer, by Quintus Aburnius Caedicianus, a legate of Augustus, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800121.text_edition: \n Libero Patri Publius Aelius Fronto veteranus ex beneficiario consularis et Aelia Frontonia coniux eius pro salute sua suorumque omnium \n
EDCS-15800121.translation: To Liber Pater. Publius Aelius Fronto, a veteran, formerly a deputy to a consular governor, and his wife Aelia Frontonia, for their health and that of all their family.
EDCS-15800121.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Liber Pater by Publius Aelius Fronto, a veteran, and his wife Aelia Frontonia, for their health and that of their family, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800122.text_edition: \n Libero Patri sacrum pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Commodi Antonini Augusti Pii patris patriae Lucius Calvisius Luci filius Velina Secundus Falerione primus pilus legionis XIII geminae donum dedit sub Vespronio Candido cons(sulari) dedicante Caerellio Sabino legato\n
EDCS-15800122.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater. For the health of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the country. Lucius Calvisius Secundus, son of Lucius, of the Velina tribe, primus pilus of the Legio XIII Gemina, gave this offering, under the supervision of Vespronius Candidus, consular governor, with Caerellius Sabinus acting as legate.
EDCS-15800122.summary: This inscription records a sacred offering to Liber Pater by Lucius Calvisius Secundus, a primus pilus of Legio XIII Gemina, for the health of Emperor Commodus, dated by consular officials during the reign of Commodus (AD 177-192).

EDCS-15800123.text_edition: \n Libero Patri et Liberae Claudius Atteius Celer veteranus legionis XIII geminae INI decurio canabensium cum suis votum libens solvit loco dato decurionum decreto\n
EDCS-15800123.translation: To Liber Pater and Libera, Claudius Atteius Celer, veteran of the XIII Gemina legion, decurion of the camp followers, with his family, gladly fulfilled a vow on land granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-15800123.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Liber Pater and Libera by a veteran and decurion of the camp followers in the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-15800124.text_edition: \n Libero Patri et Liberae Caius Maximius Iulianus optio praetorii legionis V Macedonicae visu monitus pro salute sua et suorum libens posuit\n
EDCS-15800124.translation: To Liber Pater and Libera, Caius Maximius Iulianus, optio of the Praetorian legion V Macedonica, compelled by a vision, gladly dedicated this for his own safety and that of his family.
EDCS-15800124.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Liber Pater and Libera made by an optio of the Praetorian Guard, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15800125.text_edition: \n Libero Patri et Liberae ius tus CF --- eq(uo) publico Marcus Aurelius Comatius Super antistes\n
EDCS-15800125.translation: To Liber Pater and Libera, Marcus Aurelius Comatius Super, antistes of the equestrian order, with his son, [restored name].
EDCS-15800125.summary: This fragmentary inscription records a dedication to Liber Pater and Libera by Marcus Aurelius Comatius Super, an antistes of equestrian rank, likely in the late 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15800126.text_edition: \n Pro salute sua et suorum Marcus Aurelius Comatius Super decurio antistes Marcus Comatius Exsuperatus Petronia Celerina mater Herennia Heuresis eius filiorum Superiani Exsuperantiani Superstitis Superes sic cryptam cum porticibus et apparatorio et exedra pecunia sua fecerunt libenter\n
EDCS-15800126.translation: For their own health and that of their families, Marcus Aurelius Comatius Super, decurion and antistes, Marcus Comatius Exsuperatus, and Petronia Celerina, mother, Herennia Heuresis, their sons Superianus, Exsuperantius, and Superstes, and Superes gladly built this crypt with its porticoes, an assembly area, and an exedra with their own money.
EDCS-15800126.summary: This inscription records the construction of a crypt and associated features by Marcus Aurelius Comatius Super and his family for their well-being, likely in the late 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15800127.text_edition: \n Lunae Luciferae Caius Iulius Varianus magister donum dedit\n
EDCS-15800127.translation: To Luna Lucifera, Caius Julius Varianus, magister, gave this gift.
EDCS-15800127.summary: This inscription records a gift dedicated to Luna Lucifera by Caius Julius Varianus, a magister, likely in the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-15800128.text_edition: \n Marti et Victoriae Publius Aelius Crescens duplicarius posuit\n
EDCS-15800128.translation: To Mars and Victory, Publius Aelius Crescens, a duplicarius, dedicated this.
EDCS-15800128.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mars and Victory by Publius Aelius Crescens, a duplicarius (a soldier with double pay), likely in the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-15800129.text_edition: \n Marti et Victoriae Publius Aelius Crescens duplicarius posuit\n
EDCS-15800129.translation: To Mars and Victory, Publius Aelius Crescens, a duplicarius, dedicated this.
EDCS-15800129.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mars and Victory by Publius Aelius Crescens, a duplicarius (a soldier with double pay), likely in the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-15800130.text_edition: \n Pro salute Auggustorum Marti conservatori Publius Aelius Rufinus quod adiutor officii rationum vovit cornicularius praefecti kastrorum legionis XIII geminae voto libens posuit\n
EDCS-15800130.translation: For the safety of the Emperors, to Mars the Preserver, Publius Aelius Rufinus, adjutant of the office of accounts and cornicularius of the prefect of the camp of the XIII Gemina legion, gladly dedicated this vowed offering.
EDCS-15800130.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Mars the Preserver made by Publius Aelius Rufinus, an adjutant and cornicularius, for the safety of the Emperors, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-15800131.text_edition: \n Magnae Deum Matri sacrum pro se et suis Gaius Statius Pollio veteranus legionis XIII geminae\n
EDCS-15800131.translation: Sacred to the Great Mother of the Gods, for himself and his family, Gaius Statius Pollio, veteran of the XIII Gemina legion.
EDCS-15800131.summary: This inscription records a sacred dedication to the Great Mother of the Gods by Gaius Statius Pollio, a veteran of the XIII Gemina legion, likely in the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-15800132.text_edition: \n Magnae Deum Matri Umbricia Maxima pro se suisque votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15800132.translation: Umbricia Maxima fulfilled her vow gladly and deservedly to the Great Mother of the Gods for herself and her family.
EDCS-15800132.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to the Great Mother of the Gods by Umbricia Maxima for herself and her family, likely in the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-15800133.text_edition: \n Invicto Mythirae sic Chrestion votum solvit\n
EDCS-15800133.translation: Chrestion fulfilled his vow to the unconquered Mithras.
EDCS-15800133.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Mithras by Chrestion, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800134.text_edition: \n Deo Mercurio Caius -imius Veranus voto libens posuit\n
EDCS-15800134.translation: Caius -imius Veranus willingly set up this offering to the god Mercury.
EDCS-15800134.summary: This is a dedication to Mercury by Caius -imius Veranus, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800135.text_edition: \n Minervae Publi Aeli Antipater Iulianus Genialis equo publico decuriones coloniae Apuli Q\n
EDCS-15800135.translation: Publius Aelius Antipater Iulianus Genialis, a Decurion of the colony of Apulia, [dedicated this] to Minerva, with the support of the knights.
EDCS-15800135.summary: This dedication to Minerva was made by Publius Aelius Antipater Iulianus Genialis, a decurion in the colony of Apulia, indicating a local civic and equestrian dedication, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800136.text_edition: \n Minervae Tiberius Iulius Novianus librarius legati posuit\n
EDCS-15800136.translation: Tiberius Iulius Novianus, scribe of the legate, set this up for Minerva.
EDCS-15800136.summary: This is a dedication to Minerva by Tiberius Iulius Novianus, a scribe serving a legate, suggesting a military or administrative context from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800137.text_edition: \n Minervae sanctae Celerinius Valerianus cornicularius consularis\n
EDCS-15800137.translation: Celerinius Valerianus, a cornicularius (a type of military officer) of the consular legate, [dedicated this] to holy Minerva.
EDCS-15800137.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Minerva by Celerinius Valerianus, a cornicularius serving a consular legate, indicating a military dedication likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800139.text_edition: \n Deo Soli Hieribolo Aurelius Bassinus decurio coloniae Aequensis sacerdos numinum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15800139.translation: Aurelius Bassinus, a decurion of the colony of Aequa, priest of the gods, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the god Sol Hieribolus.
EDCS-15800139.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Sol Hieribolus by Aurelius Bassinus, a decurion and priest from the colony of Aequa, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800140.text_edition: \n Deo Soli Invicto Mithrae pro salute et incolumitate Marci Aureli Thimothei et Aureli Maximi votum nuncupavit solvitque Euthices eorum libertus\n
EDCS-15800140.translation: Euthices, their freedman, made and fulfilled a vow to the unconquered god Sol Mithras for the health and safety of Marcus Aurelius Thimotheus and Aurelius Maximus.
EDCS-15800140.summary: This inscription records a vow made by a freedman, Euthices, to the god Sol Mithras for the well-being of his patrons Marcus Aurelius Thimotheus and Aurelius Maximus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800141.text_edition: \n Deo Soli Invicto Mithrae pro salute (et) incotlumitate Marci Aureli Timotei et Aureli Maximi votum nuncupavit solvitque Euthyces eorum libertus retulit\n
EDCS-15800141.translation: Euthyces, their freedman, made and fulfilled a vow, and set it up, to the unconquered god Sol Mithras for the health and safety of Marcus Aurelius Timotei and Aurelius Maximi.
EDCS-15800141.summary: This inscription records a vow made by a freedman, Euthyces, to the god Sol Mithras for the well-being of his patrons Marcus Aurelius Timotei and Aurelius Maximi, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800142.text_edition: \n Soli Invicto aedem restituit Caius Caerellius Sabinus legatus Augusti legionis XIII geminae\n
EDCS-15800142.translation: Caius Caerellius Sabinus, legate of the emperor and of the XIII Gemina legion, restored the temple to the unconquered Sol.
EDCS-15800142.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple to Sol Invictus by Caius Caerellius Sabinus, a legate in command of the Legio XIII Gemina, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800143.text_edition: \n Invicto Mythrae sic Dioscorus Marci votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15800143.translation: Dioscorus, son of Marcus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the unconquered Mithras.
EDCS-15800143.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Mithras by Dioscorus Marci, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800144.text_edition: \n invicto pro salute imperii populique Romani et ordinis coloniae Apulensis Caius Iulius Valens haruspex coloniae supra scriptae et antises huiusque loci voto libens posuit\n
EDCS-15800144.translation: To the Unconquered [god] for the safety of the empire and the Roman people and the council of the colony of Apulia, Gaius Julius Valens, diviner of the aforementioned colony and priest of this place, gladly set this up as a vow.
EDCS-15800144.summary: This dedication to an unnamed unconquered deity was set up by Gaius Julius Valens, a local diviner and priest, for the welfare of the empire and the colony of Apulia.

EDCS-15800145.text_edition: \n Veneri Victrici pro salute imperii et senatus populique Romani et ordinis coloniae Apulensis Caius Iulius Valens haruspex coloniae supra scriptae et antistes huiusce loci voto libens posuit\n
EDCS-15800145.translation: To Venus the Victorious for the safety of the empire and the senate and the Roman people and the council of the colony of Apulia, Gaius Julius Valens, diviner of the aforementioned colony and priest of this place, gladly set this up as a vow.
EDCS-15800145.summary: This dedication to Venus Victrix was set up by Gaius Julius Valens, a local diviner and priest, for the welfare of the empire and the colony of Apulia.

EDCS-15800148.text_edition: \n Soli Quintus Marcius Victor Felix Maximillianus legatus Auggustorum legionis XIII geminae et Pullaiena Caeliana clarissima femina eius et Publius Marcius Victor Maximillianus clarissimus puer filius voto\n
EDCS-15800148.translation: To Sol, Quintus Marcius Victor Felix Maximillianus, legate of the Augusti of the XIII Gemina legion, and Pullaina Caeliana, his most illustrious wife, and Publius Marcius Victor Maximillianus, their most illustrious son, [dedicate this] as a vow.
EDCS-15800148.summary: This dedication to the god Sol was made as a vow by Quintus Marcius Victor Felix Maximillianus, a legionary legate, his wife, and his son, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-15800149.text_edition: \n Invicto Mithrae Lucius Octavius Gratus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15800149.translation: To the Unconquered Mithras, Lucius Octavius Gratus gladly paid his vow, as deserved.
EDCS-15800149.summary: Lucius Octavius Gratus fulfilled a vow to the Unconquered Mithras, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-15800150.text_edition: \n Deo invicto Mithrae Euhemerus ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-15800150.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Euhemerus set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800150.summary: Euhemerus dedicated an offering to the Unconquered God Mithras in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15800151.text_edition: \n Soli Invicto Mithrae Turranius Marcellinus et Antonius Senecio Iunior conductores armamentarii ex voto posuerunt\n
EDCS-15800151.translation: To Sol Invictus Mithras, Turranius Marcellinus and Antonius Senecio the Younger, contractors of the armory, set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800151.summary: Turranius Marcellinus and Antonius Senecio the Younger, as contractors of an armory, dedicated an offering to Sol Invictus Mithras as a fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15800152.text_edition: \n Soli Invicto Mithrae Marcus Valerius Maximianus legatus Augusti votum solvit\n
EDCS-15800152.translation: To Sol Invictus Mithras, Marcus Valerius Maximianus, legate of the emperor, paid his vow.
EDCS-15800152.summary: Marcus Valerius Maximianus, a legionary legate, fulfilled a vow to Sol Invictus Mithras.

EDCS-15800153.text_edition: \n S(oli) Invicto Mithrae signum Secundinus ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-15800153.translation: To Sol Invictus Mithras, Secundinus set up this image in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800153.summary: Secundinus dedicated an image to Sol Invictus Mithras as a fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15800154.text_edition: \n Deae Nemesi Aurelius Marius optio signiferorum legionis XIII geminae cum Severia Secundina coniuge pro salute sua et Marianae Bonosae et Mariniani liberti\n
EDCS-15800154.translation: To the Goddess Nemesis, Aurelius Marius, optio of the standard-bearers of the XIII Gemina legion, with his wife Severia Secundina, for the safety of themselves and Mariana Bonosa and their freedman Mariniana.
EDCS-15800154.summary: Aurelius Marius, an optio in the XIII Gemina legion, along with his wife and family members, dedicated this to the Goddess Nemesis for their safety.

EDCS-15800155.text_edition: \n Deae Nemesi sive Fortunae Pistorius Rugianus vir clarissimus legatus legionis XIII geminae Gordianae\n
EDCS-15800155.translation: To the Goddess Nemesis, also called Fortune, Pistorius Rugianus, most distinguished man and legate of the XIII Gemina Gordiana legion, [dedicated this].
EDCS-15800155.summary: Pistorius Rugianus, a distinguished legate of the XIII Gemina Gordiana legion, dedicated this to the Goddess Nemesis, also known as Fortuna.

EDCS-15800156.translation: To Nemesis, who hears prayers most readily, Claudius Marcellus Augustalis.
EDCS-15800156.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the goddess Nemesis by Claudius Marcellus Augustalis, likely a private offering.

EDCS-15800157.text_edition: \n Numinibus Auggustorum Severi et Antonini et Getae Caesaris et deae Dianae Caius Iulius Maximinus legatus Auggustorum pro praetore\n
EDCS-15800157.translation: To the Divine Powers of the Emperors Severus and Antoninus, and of Caesar Geta, and to the goddess Diana, Caius Iulius Maximinus, imperial legate with proconsular power.
EDCS-15800157.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the divine powers of Emperors Severus and Antoninus, Geta Caesar, and Diana by Caius Iulius Maximinus, an imperial legate, likely from the Severan period.

EDCS-15800159.text_edition: \n Pro salute domini nostri sanctissimi Antonini Pii Augusti Nymphis novis sacrum Rufrius Sulpicianus legatus legionis XIII geminae Antoninianae\n
EDCS-15800159.translation: For the health of our most sacred lord Antoninus Pius Augustus, to the Nymphs, Rufrius Sulpicianus, legate of the Thirteenth Gemina Antoniniana Legion.
EDCS-15800159.summary: This inscription is a dedication made for the health of Emperor Antoninus Pius to the Nymphs by Rufrius Sulpicianus, a legionary legate, likely from the Antonine period.

EDCS-15800160.summary: This is a dedication to the deity known as Bono Puer Phosphorus, likely a syncretic or local god.

EDCS-15800161.text_edition: \n Bono Puero Aurelius Chrestus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15800161.translation: Aurelius Chrestus willingly fulfilled his vow to the Good Boy, as deserved.
EDCS-15800161.summary: This inscription records Aurelius Chrestus fulfilling a vow to Bono Puer, likely a deity or a revered individual.

EDCS-15800162.text_edition: \n Bono Puero ex voto ARO DIVS Alexandri posuit\n
EDCS-15800162.translation: Arodius Alexandri set this up to the Good Boy, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800162.summary: This inscription records Arodius Alexandri setting up a dedication to Bono Puer in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-15800163.text_edition: \n Bono Deo Puero Phosphoro Titus Flavius Italicus primus IIIIvir municipii Aureli Apulensis cum Statilia Lucia coniuge et suis ex voto\n
EDCS-15800163.translation: To the Good Boy Phosphorus, Titus Flavius Italicus, first of the four magistrates of the municipality of Aurelia Apulensis, with his wife Statilia Lucia and his family, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-15800163.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Bono Puer Phosphorus made by Titus Flavius Italicus, a local magistrate, his wife, and family in the municipality of Aurelia Apulensis.

EDCS-15800164.text_edition: \n Deo Bono Puero Phosphoro Apollini Pythio Titus Flavius Titus et Titus Flavius Philetus pro salute sua suorumque\n
EDCS-15800164.translation: To the Good Boy Phosphorus, Apollo the Pythian, Titus Flavius Titus and Titus Flavius Philetus, for their health and that of their families.
EDCS-15800164.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Bono Puer Phosphorus and Apollo Pythius by Titus Flavius Titus and Titus Flavius Philetus for the health of themselves and their families.

EDCS-15800165.text_edition: \n Iulia Secunda Puero Phosphoro D O M donum dedit dedicavitque\n
EDCS-15800165.translation: Iulia Secunda gave and dedicated this gift to the Good Boy Phosphorus, to the greatest gods.
EDCS-15800165.summary: This inscription records Iulia Secunda giving and dedicating a gift to Bono Puer Phosphorus and the greatest gods.

EDCS-15800166.text_edition: \n Bono Puero Phosphoro Laellia Curillia pro salute sua suorumque votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15800166.translation: To the Good Boy Phosphorus, Laellia Curillia willingly fulfilled her vow for her health and that of her family, as deserved.
EDCS-15800166.summary: This inscription records Laellia Curillia fulfilling a vow to Bono Puer Phosphorus for her health and that of her family.

EDCS-15800167.text_edition: \n Deo Bono Phosphoro Apollini Pythio Decimus Iulius Rusonius custos armorum pro salute sua et suorum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15800167.translation: To the good god Apollo, Pythian, god of dawn, Decimus Iulius Rusonius, keeper of arms, freely and deservedly fulfilled his vow for the safety of himself and his family.
EDCS-15800167.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Apollo by Decimus Iulius Rusonius, likely a military official, for his and his family's well-being, probably dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-15800168.text_edition: \n Priepo Pantheo PPublii Aelii Ursio et Antonianus aediles coloniae Apulensis dicaverunt Severo et Quintiano consulibus\n
EDCS-15800168.translation: P. Aelius Ursio and Antonianus, aediles of the colony of Apula, dedicated this to Priapus Pantheus, during the consulship of Severus and Quintianus.
EDCS-15800168.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument to Priapus by two aediles of the colony of Apula, dating to the consulship of Severus and Quintianus, probably in the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-15800169.text_edition: \n Quadriviis sacrum Flavia Pulchra pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15800169.translation: Sacred to the Four Crossroads. Flavia Pulchra freely and deservedly fulfilled her vow for herself and her family.
EDCS-15800169.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the spirits of the crossroads by Flavia Pulchra for herself and her family, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-15800170.text_edition: \n Silvano Publius Aelius Publi filius Fabianus augur pontifex IIvirir decurio coloniae Sarmizegetusae decurio coloniae Napocae augur coloniae eiusdem decurio municipii Apulensis votum solvit libens merito\n
