EDCS-27200003.translation: To the unconquered Hercules for the health of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus.
EDCS-27200003.summary: This is a dedication to Hercules Invictus for the health of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, likely dating to his reign (161-180 AD).

EDCS-27200004.text_edition: \n Herculi invicto Quintus Ennius Ianuarius beneficiarius consularis legionis V Macedonicae ponendum curavit\n
EDCS-27200004.translation: Quintus Ennius Ianuarius, a beneficiarius of the consular legate of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, arranged for this to be set up to the unconquered Hercules.
EDCS-27200004.summary: This inscription records a private dedication to Hercules Invictus by Quintus Ennius Ianuarius, a soldier of the Legio V Macedonica, likely from the late 2nd century AD.

EDCS-27200005.text_edition: \n Invic<t>o Aurelius Montanus miles legionis V Macedonicae <p=I>(iae) constantis felicis libens posuit \n
EDCS-27200005.translation: Aurelius Montanus, a soldier of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, 'The Constant, Lucky One', willingly set this up to the unconquered one.
EDCS-27200005.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Aurelius Montanus, a soldier of the Legio V Macedonica, to an unconquered deity, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200006.text_edition: \n I V P Aurelius Florus votum solvit\n
EDCS-27200006.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Aurelius Florus, probably dating to the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200007.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelius Lucifer votum libens posuit\n
EDCS-27200007.translation: Aurelius Lucifer willingly set this up as a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-27200007.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Aurelius Lucifer to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200008.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute domini nostri Marci Aureli Antonini Imperatoris Aurelius Rusticus <l=C>(ibens) animo votum posuit \n
EDCS-27200008.translation: For the health of our lord Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, Aurelius Rusticus, with a willing and good heart, set up this vow.
EDCS-27200008.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Aurelius Rusticus for the health of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, likely from his reign (161-180 AD).

EDCS-27200009.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelius Sedatus immunis librarius votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-27200009.translation: Aurelius Sedatus, an exempt scribe, willingly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-27200009.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Aurelius Sedatus, a scribe, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200010.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelius Valentinus praefectus equitum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-27200010.translation: Aurelius Valentinus, prefect of the cavalry, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-27200010.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Aurelius Valentinus, a prefect of cavalry, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200011.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iucundius Iuvenalis cornicularius legati votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-27200011.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Iucundius Iuvenalis, a *cornicularius* (an officer's aide) to the legate, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27200011.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Iucundius Iuvenalis, a *cornicularius*.

EDCS-27200012.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Manlius quaestor votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-27200012.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Manlius the quaestor gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27200012.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Manlius, a quaestor.

EDCS-27200013.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo D REB IAS miles legionis I Italicae \n
EDCS-27200013.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, D[---] REB IAS, a soldier of the First Italian Legion.
EDCS-27200013.summary: This inscription names a soldier of the First Italian Legion dedicating something to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-27200014.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Silvano Publius S C\n
EDCS-27200014.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to Silvanus, Publius [dedicates this] by decree of the Senate.
EDCS-27200014.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Silvanus by Publius.

EDCS-27200015.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Valerius Romanus Aurelius Felix votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-27200015.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Valerius Romanus and Aurelius Felix gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vows.
EDCS-27200015.summary: This inscription records a joint votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Valerius Romanus and Aurelius Felix.

EDCS-27200016.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et diis deabusque et Genio loci Optatus praefectus legionis V Macedonicae constantis votum libens solvit merito\n
EDCS-27200016.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, and to all the gods and goddesses, and to the Genius of the place, Optatus, prefect of the steadfast Fifth Macedonian Legion, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27200016.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Optatus, prefect of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and other deities.

EDCS-27200017.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Aurelius Victor beneficiarius ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-27200017.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and Juno, Aurelius Victor, a *beneficiarius* (a soldier with special duties), set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-27200017.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno by Aurelius Victor, a *beneficiarius*.

EDCS-27200018.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Paterno Aurelius Ulpius adiutor officii corniculariorum ex voto libens merito posuit\n
EDCS-27200018.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Paterno Aurelius Ulpius, an assistant in the office of the *cornicularii*, set this up gladly and deservedly in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-27200018.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Paterno Aurelius Ulpius, an administrator for *cornicularii*.

EDCS-27200019.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Statori item Depulsori Iulius Maximianus tribunus militum votum solvit pro sua suorumque salute\n
EDCS-27200019.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Stayer, and likewise the Repeller, Iulius Maximianus, a military tribune, fulfilled his vow for the safety of himself and his family.
EDCS-27200019.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Iulius Maximianus, a military tribune, to Jupiter under the epithets Stator and Depulsor for the welfare of his family.

EDCS-27200020.translation: To Liber Pater, Rom[---] from Lucca.
EDCS-27200020.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Liber Pater by an individual from Lucca.

EDCS-27200021.text_edition: \n Marti amico et consentienti sacrum Hermias dedicavit idemque vovit\n
EDCS-27200021.translation: Sacred to Mars the friend and consenting; Hermias dedicated it and also made a vow.
EDCS-27200021.summary: A dedication to Mars by Hermias, likely a private votive offering, dated possibly to the Imperial period.

EDCS-27200022.text_edition: \n Mercurio consentienti sacrum Hermias dedicavit idemque vovit\n
EDCS-27200022.translation: Sacred to Mercury the consenting; Hermias dedicated it and also made a vow.
EDCS-27200022.summary: A dedication to Mercury by Hermias, likely a private votive offering, dated possibly to the Imperial period.

EDCS-27200023.text_edition: \n Aelius Maximus miles legionis V Macedonicae votum solvit libens posuit\n
EDCS-27200023.translation: Aelius Maximus, a soldier of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, gladly fulfilled his vow and set this up.
EDCS-27200023.summary: A military dedication by Aelius Maximus of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-27200024.translation: Iulius Iulianus set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-27200024.summary: A private votive inscription by Iulius Iulianus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-27200026.text_edition: \n Deae Nemesi Ulpius Valerius miles legionis V Macedonicae piae Antoninianae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-27200026.translation: To the Goddess Nemesis, Ulpius Valerius, a soldier of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, Pious Antoninianae, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27200026.summary: A military dedication to the goddess Nemesis by Ulpius Valerius of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, possibly dating to the Antonine period.

EDCS-27200027.text_edition: \n Silvano sacrum Valerius Celsus flamen municipii votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-27200027.translation: Sacred to Silvanus, Valerius Celsus, priest of the municipality, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27200027.summary: A dedication to Silvanus by Valerius Celsus, a municipal priest, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-27200028.text_edition: \n SER Aurelius Pat legionis V Macedonicae ex voto \n
EDCS-27200028.translation: Servius Aurelius Pat of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-27200028.summary: A military inscription by Aurelius Pat of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, likely a votive, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-27200029.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate III imperator VII consul II proconsul pater patriae legioni V Macedonicae piae <c=P>(onstantis) dono dedit dedicante Publio Septimio Geta legato Augusti pro praetore curam agente Tiberio Claudio Claudiano legato Augusti \n
EDCS-27200029.translation: The Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the third time, imperator for the seventh time, consul for the second time, proconsul, father of the country, gave this gift to the Fifth Macedonian Legion, the Constant one, with Publius Septimius Geta, legate of the emperor with praetorian power, overseeing, and Tiberius Claudius Claudianus, legate of the emperor, in charge.
EDCS-27200029.summary: An imperial dedication by Emperor Septimius Severus to the Fifth Macedonian Legion, dated to his third tribunician power (195 AD) or shortly thereafter, listing imperial officials.

EDCS-27200030.text_edition: \n nnos VIII Aelius Vitalis vixit nnos VIII Aelius Primus lt;t=P>ubicen] legionis supra scriptae et Viria Quarta filiis bene merentibus posuerunt \n
EDCS-27200030.translation: Aelius Vitalis lived 8 years; Aelius Primus lived [---] years; and Viria Quarta set this up for their well-deserving sons, Aelius Vitalis and Aelius Primus of the aforementioned legion.
EDCS-27200030.summary: A funerary monument set up by Viria Quarta for her sons Aelius Vitalis and Aelius Primus, one of whom was a soldier in the Fifth Legion, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-27200031.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelius Tiiadmes Palmura sic vixit annos VIII Surillio vixit annos XXV Rufina vixit annos XX Aelius Bolhas Bannaei veteranus ex numero Palmurenorum sic et Aelia Domestica coniux aeius posuerant filiae pientissimae et <d=C>ulcissimae et liberto et menesteriis sic bene merentibus \n
EDCS-27200031.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aelius Tiiadmes Palmura lived 8 years; Surillio lived 25 years; Rufina lived 20 years. Aelius Bolhas, a veteran from the Palmurensian group, and his wife Aelia Domestica had set this up for their most dutiful and sweetest daughters, and for their freedman and freedwomen, who deserved well.
EDCS-27200031.summary: A family tomb inscription for Aelius Tiiadmes, Surillio, Rufina, Aelius Bolhas, and Aelia Domestica, likely from the Roman military settlement of Palmura, dated to the Imperial period.

EDCS-27200032.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Celsus vixit annos LX Aurelius Bassus vixit annos L Aurelius Celsinianus miles cohortis I p p patri et avunculo pientissimis ob merita posuit\n
EDCS-27200032.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Celsus lived 60 years. Aurelius Bassus lived 50 years. Aurelius Celsinianus, a soldier of the I auxiliary cohort, erected this for his most dutiful father and uncle, for their merits.
EDCS-27200032.summary: A tombstone for Aurelius Celsinianus' father and uncle, erected by Celsinianus, a soldier in the I auxiliary cohort, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200033.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Victor librarius legionis V Macedonicae vixit annos XXXIIII et Valeria Valentina POI vixit annum Ulpia Flaviana coniugi posuit\n
EDCS-27200033.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Victor, a scribe of the V Macedonian legion, lived 34 years, and Valeria Valentina. Ulpia Flaviana erected this for her husband.
EDCS-27200033.summary: A tombstone for Aurelius Victor, a legionary scribe, and his wife Valeria Valentina, erected by Ulpia Flaviana, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200034.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Statio Prisciano nnorum VIII Marcus Statius Priscianus primus pilus pater faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-27200034.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Statins Priscianus, 8 years old. Marcus Statius Priscianus, a centurion of the first rank (primus pilus), had this made.
EDCS-27200034.summary: A tombstone for a young Marcus Statius Priscianus, erected by his father of the same name and rank, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200035.text_edition: \n leia o vixit annos III menses T VAL DEC N M natis piissimis \n
EDCS-27200035.translation: To the spirits of the departed. [Name] lived 3 years and [months]. [Name] and [Name] erected this for their most dutiful children.
EDCS-27200035.summary: A tombstone for at least two young children, whose parents erected the monument, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200036.text_edition: \n AII Aurelius dus magister Augustalis Napocae Aureliae Felicissimae sorori bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-27200036.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Dus, Augustalis magister, erected this for Aurelia Felicissima, his well-deserving sister.
EDCS-27200036.summary: A tombstone for Aurelia Felicissima, erected by her brother Aurelius Dus, who held the position of Augustalis magister, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200038.text_edition: \n ] Flavius Hiero filius AD Antonin municipii Septimi Potaissae item Ulpia Sabina coniux eius et filii item Claudia Sabina itemcubla item Claudius Maximianus filiorum et suo nomine sua vota restituerunt\n
EDCS-27200038.translation: [---] Flavius Hiero, son of Antonin, of the municipality of Septimi [---], also Ulpia Sabina, his wife, and their sons, also Claudia Sabina, [---], and Claudius Maximianus, in the name of the sons and of himself, restored their vows.
EDCS-27200038.summary: A dedication recording the restoration of vows by Flavius Hiero, his wife, sons, and other family members, likely related to a municipal or religious building, dating possibly to the Antonine period.

EDCS-27200039.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Crito vixit nnos IIII Aurelius Zosimianus vixit annum ex Moesia superiore Aurelius Zosimus natibus\n
EDCS-27200039.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Crito lived 4 years. Aurelius Zosimianus lived [duration] from Upper Moesia. Aurelius Zosimus, for his parents.
EDCS-27200039.summary: A tombstone for Aurelius Crito and Aurelius Zosimianus, likely children, erected by Aurelius Zosimus for his parents, mentioning a connection to Upper Moesia, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200040.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelius Sabinus vixit annos XIII Aelia Tertina vixit annos VI Aelia Meletina vixit annos LX Aelius Ingenuus et Aelia Pudentilla \n
EDCS-27200040.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aelius Sabinus lived 13 years. Aelia Tertina lived 6 years. Aelia Meletina lived 60 years. Aelius Ingenuus and Aelia Pudentilla [erected this, likely for the deceased].
EDCS-27200040.summary: A tombstone commemorating four individuals: Aelius Sabinus, Aelia Tertina, Aelia Meletina, and likely Aelius Ingenuus and Aelia Pudentilla as the dedicator(s), likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200041.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelia Publicia vixit annos XXXX Aurelius Germanus coniugi posuit\n
EDCS-27200041.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aelia Publicia lived 40 years. Aurelius Germanus erected this for his wife.
EDCS-27200041.summary: A tombstone for Aelia Publicia, erected by her husband Aurelius Germanus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200042.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aia Nandonis vixit annis LXXX Andrada Bituvantis vixit annis LXXX Bricena vixit annis XL Iusta vixit annis XXX Bedarus vixit XII post obitum ei Herculanus libertus patronae bene merenti\n
EDCS-27200042.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aia daughter of Nandon lived 80 years. Andrada daughter of Bituvantis lived 80 years. Bricena lived 40 years. Iusta lived 30 years. Bedarus lived 12 years. Herculanus, freedman, for his well-deserving patroness after her death.
EDCS-27200042.summary: A tombstone with multiple burials, including named women and Bedarus, with a final dedication by Herculanus, freedman, for his patroness, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200043.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Aulucmus vixit diem I menses VI annos XXXV Aurelii Basianus et Dasius bene merenti et sibi memoriam faciendam curaverunt\n
EDCS-27200043.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aurelius Aulucmus lived for 1 day, 6 months, and 35 years. Aurelii Basianus and Dasius had this memorial made for him, as deserving well, and for themselves.
EDCS-27200043.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life span of Aurelius Aulucmus and is a memorial set up by Aurelii Basianus and Dasius, likely family members.

EDCS-27200044.text_edition: \n Aurelius Bonosus vixit nnos V menses V Aurelii Viatorinus et Magna filio\n
EDCS-27200044.translation: Aurelius Bonosus lived for 5 years, 5 months. Aurelii Viatorinus and Magna [set this up] for their son.
EDCS-27200044.summary: This funerary epitaph records the short life of Aurelius Bonosus, set up by his parents, Aurelii Viatorinus and Magna.

EDCS-27200045.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Epicatius vixit nnis LII Aurelius Viator vi[x](it) vixit annis X Aurelius Corbulo vixit annis XXII\n
EDCS-27200045.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aurelius Epicatius lived for 52 years. Aurelius Viator lived for 10 years. Aurelius Corbulo lived for 22 years.
EDCS-27200045.summary: This funerary epitaph records the ages at death of Aurelius Epicatius, Aurelius Viator, and Aurelius Corbulo, likely family members.

EDCS-27200046.text_edition: \n vixit annos XXXX Maxima coniux bene merenti\n
EDCS-27200046.translation: lived 40 years. Maxima, his wife, [set this up] for him, as deserving well.
EDCS-27200046.summary: This funerary epitaph records the age at death of an unnamed man and states that his wife, Maxima, erected the monument.

EDCS-27200048.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that names at least two individuals with the cognomen Ulpius, likely a dedication or a funerary record.

EDCS-27200049.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ulpius <P=F>roculus vixit annos LX Ul<p=B>(i) PLLI vixit annos L MOC IVS VIXV vixit annos XXXVII L NA vixit annos XV HP IT-- v(ixit)? a(nnos) XXX FVIPM vixit annos X nus vixit annos VII annos II \n
EDCS-27200049.translation: To the Divine Shades. Ulpius Proculus lived for 60 years. Ulpius [son of?] PLLI lived for 50 years. MOCIVS lived for 37 years. L NA lived for 15 years. HP IT... [lived?] for 30 years. FVIPM lived for 10 years. [---]nus lived for 7 years, [---] lived for 2 years.
EDCS-27200049.summary: This extensive funerary epitaph lists the ages at death of several individuals, all presumably related or part of the same burial, identified by the gentilician name Ulpius and other names.

EDCS-27200050.text_edition: \n AREO IOR IS vixit annos XXX conserva posuit bene mere<nt=TN>i \n
EDCS-27200050.translation: [In the name of the gods?] AREO IOR IS lived for 30 years. conserva erected [this] for her, as deserving well.
EDCS-27200050.summary: This funerary epitaph records the age at death of AREO IOR IS and states that 'conserva' (a female slave or freedwoman) erected the monument.

EDCS-27200051.text_edition: \n lus VE LO FIL AN patrono pro pietate\n
EDCS-27200051.translation: ... FIL AN for his patron out of dutiful regard.
EDCS-27200051.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to be a dedication or dedication of a monument to a patron by someone named FIL AN, motivated by piety.

EDCS-27200058.text_edition: \n Apollini Atilius Celsianus decurio votum solvit libens merito Apollini\n
EDCS-27200058.translation: To Apollo. Atilius Celsianus, a decurio, paid his vow to Apollo freely and deservedly.
EDCS-27200058.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Atilius Celsianus, a decurio (local councillor), fulfilling a vow to the god Apollo.

EDCS-27200059.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription that begins with the name Aelius Setus and indicates it was likely a funerary epitaph.

EDCS-27200061.text_edition: \n Aurelius Cto tesserarius legionis V Macedonicae vixit nnos XL Aelia Candida \n
EDCS-27200061.translation: Aurelius, a tesserarius of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, lived 40 years. Aelia Candida [erected this].
EDCS-27200061.summary: This is a tombstone for Aurelius, a soldier of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-27200062.text_edition: \n Marcellina posuit coniugi et marito suo bene merito \n
EDCS-27200062.translation: Marcellina set this up for her deserving husband and spouse.
EDCS-27200062.summary: This is a tombstone set up by Marcellina for her deceased husband, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27200066.text_edition: \n Nymphis Augustis pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Titi Aeli Hadriani Antonini Augusti Pii patris patriae Marcus Statius Priscus legatus Augusti pro praetore votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-27200066.translation: Marcus Statius Priscus, imperial legate with praetorian authority, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the August Deities for the health of the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the country.
EDCS-27200066.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Marcus Statius Priscus, a Roman governor, to the Imperial Deities for the health of Emperor Antoninus Pius.

EDCS-27200067.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Imperatoris collegium aurariarum Lucius Calpurnius donum dedit\n
EDCS-27200067.translation: The guild of leather workers gave a gift to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the health of the Emperor; Lucius Calpurnius gave it.
EDCS-27200067.summary: This is a votive inscription by the guild of leather workers (collegium aurariarum) and Lucius Calpurnius to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the emperor's health, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27200068.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo ceterisque dis consentibus Marcus Opellius Adiutor IIvir coloniae\n
EDCS-27200068.translation: Marcus Opellius Adiutor, duovir of the colony, [dedicated this] to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and all the consenting gods.
EDCS-27200068.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and other gods by Marcus Opellius Adiutor, a high-ranking official (duovir) in a Roman colony, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27200069.text_edition: \n Publio Furio Saturnino consuli Publius Aelius Maximianus decurio coloniae\n
EDCS-27200069.translation: Publius Aelius Maximianus, decurion of the colony, [dedicated this] to Publius Furio Saturninus, consul.
EDCS-27200069.summary: This inscription records Publius Aelius Maximianus, a decurion in a Roman colony, honoring Publius Furio Saturninus, who held the consulship, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27200070.text_edition: \n In honorem domus divinae Adrastiae collegium utriculariorum\n
EDCS-27200070.translation: In honor of the divine house, the guild of boatmen [dedicated this] to Adrastia.
EDCS-27200070.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the guild of boatmen (collegium utriculariorum) to the goddess Adrastia in honor of the imperial family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27200071.text_edition: \n Dianae Augustae sacrum Titus Annius Annianus domo Tipasa praefectus cohortis IIII Hispanorum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-27200071.translation: Sacred to Diana Augusta. Titus Annius Annianus, from Tipasa, prefect of the Fourth Cohort of Spaniards, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27200071.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Diana Augusta by Titus Annius Annianus, a prefect of a Spanish cohort, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27200072.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Iulius Iulianus praefectus cohortis IIII Hispanorum equitatae domo Roma votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-27200072.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Caius Iulius Iulianus, prefect of the Fourth Cohort of Spaniards, cavalry, from Rome, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27200072.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Caius Iulius Iulianus, a cavalry prefect of the Fourth Cohort of Spaniards from Rome, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27200073.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Vettius Severus praefectus cohortis IIII? Hisp(anorum) equitatae Antoninianae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-27200073.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Titus Vettius Severus, prefect of the Fourth? Spanish Cavalry Cohort Antoniniana, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27200073.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Titus Vettius Severus, a prefect of a Spanish cavalry cohort, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27200076.text_edition: \n Pro salute victoria Imperatoris domini nostri Marci Aureli Severi Alexandri Pii Felicis III posuit libens\n
EDCS-27200076.translation: For the health and victory of our lord Emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, he gladly erected this.
EDCS-27200076.summary: This inscription records a dedication for the well-being and victory of Emperor Alexander Severus, erected by an unspecified individual in gratitude.

EDCS-27200077.text_edition: \n Aesculapio et Hygiae Tiberius Claudius Donatus merito\n
EDCS-27200077.translation: Tiberius Claudius Donatus, in recognition of services, (dedicated this) to Aesculapius and Hygieia.
EDCS-27200077.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the gods Aesculapius and Hygieia by Tiberius Claudius Donatus for meritorious service.

EDCS-27200080.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto\n
EDCS-27200080.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus.
EDCS-27200080.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla).

EDCS-27200081.text_edition: \n Deo Mithrae Honoratus praefectus cohortis III Gallorum dono dedit\n
EDCS-27200081.translation: Honoratus, prefect of the III Cohort of Gauls, dedicated this as a gift to the god Mithras.
EDCS-27200081.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the god Mithras by Honoratus, the prefect of the III Cohort of Gauls.

EDCS-27200082.text_edition: \n ------] Caius Suria ri filius aetatis EINIM voto posuit bene merenti\n
EDCS-27200082.translation: [...] Gaius Suria, son of Ri, at the age of [...], erected this in fulfillment of a vow to the well-deserving one.
EDCS-27200082.summary: This incomplete inscription records the erection of a monument by Gaius Suria, son of Ri, fulfilling a vow to an unspecified, well-deserving recipient.

EDCS-27200085.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Licinia mater vixit AM MVS\n
EDCS-27200085.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Licinia lived M years [...]
EDCS-27200085.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Licinia, recording her lifespan and likely her burial place.

EDCS-27200087.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Suadullus TITVR S vixit annos LXXXXI fecit filius patri bene merenti\n
EDCS-27200087.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Suadullus [lived?] [...] TITVR S lived 91 years. His son made this for his deserving father.
EDCS-27200087.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Suadullus at the age of 91 and was erected by his son.

EDCS-27200089.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Prima Macri filia annorum L Secund \n
EDCS-27200089.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Prima, daughter of Macr[i], aged 50 years. Secund [...]
EDCS-27200089.summary: This incomplete funerary inscription commemorates Prima, daughter of Macr[i], who lived to be 50 years old.

EDCS-27200090.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ulpio Sabino decurioni coloniae Aureliae Napocensium vixit annos L Ulpia Sabina marito Ulpii Maximus Manus patri \n
EDCS-27200090.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ulpius Sabinus, decurion of the colony of Aurelia Napocensium, lived 50 years. Ulpia Sabina (made this) for her husband Ulpius Maximus Manus, (her) father.
EDCS-27200090.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Ulpius Sabinus, a decurion of Aurelia Napocensium, at the age of 50, and was erected by his wife and son.

EDCS-27200091.text_edition: \n Deo Saromando Demetrius Antoni votum libens posuit\n
EDCS-27200091.translation: Demetrius Antoni gladly erected this vow to the god Saromandus.
EDCS-27200091.summary: This inscription records a voluntary vow fulfillment to the god Saromandus by Demetrius Antoni.

EDCS-27200093.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cotu Successi filia cives Norica vixit annos LV Claudius Latinus posuit coniugi bene merenti hic sita est\n
EDCS-27200093.translation: To the departed spirits. Cotus, daughter of Successus, a Roman citizen of Noricum, lived 55 years. Claudius Latinus erected this for his deserving wife, buried here.
EDCS-27200093.summary: A tombstone for Cotus, a Norican citizen, erected by her husband Claudius Latinus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27200095.text_edition: \n Iovi Soli Invicto Deo Genitori rupe nato Lucius Aelius Hylas XX libertus pro salute et Horientis sic fili sui et Apuleia eius signum numinis cum absidata ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-27200095.translation: To Jupiter Sol Invictus, the Father God, born of the rock. Lucius Aelius Hylas, a freedman of the XX legion, for his own health and that of his son Horientis and his wife Apuleia, set up this image of divinity with an apse, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-27200095.summary: A votive inscription to Jupiter Sol Invictus by Lucius Aelius Hylas, dedicated for his health and that of his family, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27200096.text_edition: \n Iuliae Mamaeae Augustae matri sanctissimi Imperatoris Caesaris Severi Alexandri Augusti et castrorum senatusque ala Frontoniana Alexandriana ex quaestura sua dedicante Iasdio Domitiano legato Augusti pro praetore\n
EDCS-27200096.translation: To Julia Mamaea Augusta, mother of the most holy Emperor Caesar Severus Alexander Augustus, the ala Frontoniana Alexandriana dedicated from her quaestorship, with Iasdius Domitianus, governor, presiding.
EDCS-27200096.summary: A dedication by the ala Frontoniana Alexandriana to Julia Mamaea Augusta, mother of Emperor Severus Alexander, likely dating to the reign of Severus Alexander (222-235 CE).

EDCS-27200098.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aelius Quadratus eques a<l=I>ae Fronton<i=T>anae T VAIANI \n
EDCS-27200098.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aelius Quadratus, a cavalryman of the ala Frontoniana, [dedicated this?].
EDCS-27200098.summary: A dedication for Aelius Quadratus, a cavalryman of the ala Frontoniana, likely a tombstone or memorial from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27200099.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aponius eques alae Frontonianae vixit annos L Aponia et Laelius patri posuerunt bene merenti\n
EDCS-27200099.translation: To the departed spirits. Aponius, a cavalryman of the ala Frontoniana, lived 50 years. Aponia and Laelius erected this for their deserving father.
EDCS-27200099.summary: A tombstone for Aponius, a cavalryman of the ala Frontoniana, erected by his children Aponia and Laelius, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27200101.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Iustinus eques numeri Palmyrenorum Porolissensium vixit nnos XL Aurelius Maximus frater et heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-27200101.translation: To the departed spirits. Aurelius Iustinus, a cavalryman of the numerus Palmyrenorum Porolissensium, lived 40 years. Aurelius Maximus, his brother and heir, took care to have this made.
EDCS-27200101.summary: A tombstone for Aurelius Iustinus, a cavalryman of the Palmyrene cohort at Porolissum, erected by his brother and heir, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27200102.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Themaes librarius alae Frontonianae vixit annis L et Aelia Iulia coniu<x=G> et Aelia Pupula vixit annis II Aurelius Thementianus filius parentibus bene merentibus posuit \n
EDCS-27200102.translation: To the departed spirits. Aurelius Themaes, a scribe of the ala Frontoniana, lived 50 years, and Aelia Iulia, his wife, and Aelia Pupula lived 2 years. Aurelius Thementianus, their son, erected this for his deserving parents.
EDCS-27200102.summary: A tombstone for Aurelius Themaes and Aelia Iulia, and their infant daughter Aelia Pupula, erected by their son Aurelius Thementianus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27200103.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Bonosa vixit annos XLII Valerius Valerianus veteranus ex decurione alae Frontonianae coniugi pientissimae bene merenti faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-27200103.translation: To the departed spirits. Bonosa lived 42 years. Valerius Valerianus, a veteran, formerly a decurion of the ala Frontoniana, arranged for this to be made for his most devoted and deserving wife.
EDCS-27200103.summary: A tombstone for Bonosa, erected by her veteran husband Valerius Valerianus, formerly a decurion of the ala Frontoniana, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27200104.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cocceius Iulius eques alae Frontonianae stipendiorum VII vixit annos XXVII Primus heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-27200104.translation: To the departed spirits. Cocceius Iulius, a cavalryman of the ala Frontoniana, served 7 years, lived 27 years. Primus, his heir, took care to have this made.
EDCS-27200104.summary: A tombstone for Cocceius Iulius, a cavalryman of the ala Frontoniana, erected by his heir Primus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27200105.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cittius Ioivai eques alae Frontonianae stipendiorum XVI vixit annos XXXVI Iustus eques alae eiusdem heres et frater faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-27200105.translation: To the departed spirits. Cittius Ioivai, a cavalryman of the ala Frontoniana, served 16 years, lived 36 years. Iustus, a cavalryman of the same unit, his heir and brother, took care to have this made.
EDCS-27200105.summary: A tombstone for Cittius Ioivai, a cavalryman of the ala Frontoniana, erected by his brother and heir Iustus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27200106.text_edition: \n Iulia Mansueta vixit annos XIII Valerius Valerianus veteranus ex decurione alae Frontonianae \n
EDCS-27200106.translation: Iulia Mansueta lived 13 years. Valerius Valerianus, a veteran formerly a decurion of the Frontonian cavalry wing, [set this up].
EDCS-27200106.summary: This is a tombstone for Iulia Mansueta, with her father, a veteran decurion of the Frontonian cavalry wing, identified as the dedicator.

EDCS-27200107.text_edition: \n eques alae Frontonianae hic situs est vixit annis XXXX Mucapuis coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-27200107.translation: Mucapuis, a cavalryman of the Frontonian cavalry wing, lies here; he lived 40 years. He placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-27200107.summary: This tombstone records the death of Mucapuis, a cavalryman of the Frontonian cavalry wing, and his wife.

EDCS-27200109.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caius Valerius Vitalis veteranus ex decurione alae Frontonianae vixit nnis L et Valerius Vitalianus filius huius vixit annis II et mensibus VII Valerius Laetillus decurio alae supra scriptae Valerius Saturninus duplicarius decuriae eiusdem parentibus fratrique pientissimis bene merentibus posuerunt\n
EDCS-27200109.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Caius Valerius Vitalis, a veteran formerly a decurion of the Frontonian cavalry wing, lived 50 years, and his son Valerius Vitalianus lived 2 years and 7 months. Valerius Laetillus, decurion of the aforementioned cavalry wing, and Valerius Saturninus, duplicarius of his same unit, erected this for their most dutiful parents and brother, who were well-deserving.
EDCS-27200109.summary: This tombstone commemorates Caius Valerius Vitalis, his son Valerianus, and another son, with the inscription set up by Valerius Laetillus and Valerius Saturninus, likely relatives.

EDCS-27200110.text_edition: \n Ius veteranus vixit annis LX Aurelius anus filius M \n
EDCS-27200110.translation: Ius the veteran lived 60 years. Aurelius... son of M...
EDCS-27200110.summary: This fragmentary tombstone records the lifespan of a veteran named Ius and names Aurelius as his son.

EDCS-27200113.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelius Mercurius vixit nnis XXXX Aelia Rufilla vixit annis VIIII Aelius Publius vixit nnis VI Nennia Ianuaria \n
EDCS-27200113.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aelius Mercurius lived 40 years. Aelia Rufilla lived 9 years. Aelius Publius lived 6 years. Nennia Ianuaria...
EDCS-27200113.summary: This is a fragmented tombstone recording the lives of several individuals, likely family members, dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed.

EDCS-27200115.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Calpurnia Maurina vixit annos XL Aelius Respectus coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-27200115.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Calpurnia Maurina lived 40 years. Aelius Respectus placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-27200115.summary: This tombstone records the death of Calpurnia Maurina and was erected by her husband, Aelius Respectus.

EDCS-27200116.text_edition: \n ------] Iovinus vixit nnos II menses II Aurelius Castor posuit\n
EDCS-27200116.translation: ...Iovinus lived 2 years and 2 months. Aurelius Castor placed [this].
EDCS-27200116.summary: This fragmentary tombstone records the short life of a child named Iovinus, with Aurelius Castor identified as the dedicator.

EDCS-27200119.summary: This very brief inscription likely commemorates two individuals, Amarantus and Hediste, possibly on a tombstone or other marker.

EDCS-27200127.summary: This is a highly fragmented inscription, possibly part of a longer text or name, with illegible sections.

EDCS-27200131.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Aurelius Moenenus consularis legionis VIII Augustae ISR ANS pro salute sua suorumque omnium votum libens merito\n
EDCS-27200131.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Marcus Aurelius Moenenus, a consular legate of the Eighth Augustan Legion, willingly and deservedly made this vow for his own safety and that of all his family.
EDCS-27200131.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by Marcus Aurelius Moenenus, a consular legate of the Eighth Augustan Legion, for his and his family's well-being.

EDCS-27200132.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et dis deabus omnibus Valerius Vibius Valerianus beneficiarius consularis legionis XIII geminae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-27200132.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, and to all the gods and goddesses, Valerius Vibius Valerianus, an officer of a consular legate of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27200132.summary: This is a votive inscription from a soldier to Jupiter and all gods, dating to the period when the Thirteenth Legion Gemina was active.

EDCS-27200133.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo ceterisque dis Publius Aelius Sextilianus et Caius Speratus one et Caius OVII Lposuerunt\n
EDCS-27200133.translation: Publius Aelius Sextilianus and Caius Speratus and Caius OVII L erected this to Jupiter, Best and Greatest, and the other gods.
EDCS-27200133.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter and other gods by two individuals, likely soldiers or officials given the context of other inscriptions.

EDCS-27200134.text_edition: \n Deae Nemesi sacrarium vetustate dilapsum a solo restituit Cassius Erotianus beneficiarius consularis agens in munere stationis \n
EDCS-27200134.translation: Cassius Erotianus, an officer of a consular legate, acting in the office of the station, restored from the ground up a sanctuary to the goddess Nemesis that had fallen into ruin through age.
EDCS-27200134.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a sanctuary to the goddess Nemesis by Cassius Erotianus, an official serving in a military post, likely in the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-27200135.text_edition: \n Deae Nemesi Caius Iulius Maximus beneficiarius consularis legionis V Macedonicae fecit\n
EDCS-27200135.translation: Caius Iulius Maximus, an officer of a consular legate of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, made this for the goddess Nemesis.
EDCS-27200135.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Nemesis by Caius Iulius Maximus, a soldier in the Fifth Macedonian Legion, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-27200136.text_edition: \n Deae Nemesi Reginae Marcus Valerius Valentinus beneficiarius consularis miles legionis XIII geminae Gordianae aedilis coloniae Napocae agens sub si<g=C>(nis) Samum cum regione Ans votum libens merito posuit Imperatore domino nostro Marco Antonio Gordiano Augusto et Aviola consulibus X \n
EDCS-27200136.translation: Marcus Valerius Valentinus, an officer of a consular legate, a soldier of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion Gordiana, acting as aedile of the colony of Napoca, serving under Samus with the region of Ans, gladly and deservedly set up this vow during the consulship of our lord Emperor Marcus Antonius Gordianus Augustus and Aviola.
EDCS-27200136.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Marcus Valerius Valentinus, a soldier and local official in the colony of Napoca, dedicated to the goddess Nemesis Regina and dating to the reign of Emperor Gordian III.

EDCS-27200137.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno pro salute domini nostri Augusti pontifex municipii Septimii Porolissensium et Antiochianus PSAE \n
EDCS-27200137.translation: For Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, for the safety of our lord Augustus, the pontiff of the municipality of Septimia Porolissensium and Antiochianus PSAE.
EDCS-27200137.summary: This inscription is a prayer for the safety of the emperor, dedicated to Jupiter Dolichenus by a local religious official in the municipality of Porolissum, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-27200139.text_edition: \n Genio scholaes sic ordinatorum suc sic curagentibus Lucio Cilio Aeliano et Tiberio Aurelio Ro principibus posuerunt\n
EDCS-27200139.translation: Those serving as principes, Lucius Cilius Aelianus and Tiberius Aurelius Ro, placed this for the genius of the collegium of ordinati and those administering it.
EDCS-27200139.summary: This inscription records the dedication of something by two leaders (principes) to the protecting spirit of a collegium (guild or association) of officials.

EDCS-27200140.text_edition: \n o vixit nnos LX et Aureli- Aelian bene m(erent---?) ---\n
EDCS-27200140.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription commemorating a person named Aelian who lived 60 years, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-27200141.text_edition: \n Herculi sancto Marcus Veracilius Verus legatus Augusti pro praetore curante nnio Maximo praefecto equitum\n
EDCS-27200141.translation: To the holy Hercules, Marcus Veracilius Verus, legate of the emperor with praetorian power, with Annianus Maximus, prefect of cavalry, overseeing.
EDCS-27200141.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Hercules by a high-ranking imperial legate, Marcus Veracilius Verus, with the participation of a cavalry prefect, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-27200142.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Daphnus.
EDCS-27200142.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Daphnus and the Di Manes (spirits of the dead), likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-27200145.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XX imperator II consul IIII pater patriae amphitheatrum vetustate dilapsum denuo fecit curante Tiberio Claudio Quintiliano procuratore suo\n
EDCS-27200145.translation: Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 20th time, imperator for the 2nd time, consul for the 4th time, father of the fatherland, rebuilt the amphitheater, which had fallen into ruin from age, under the supervision of his procurator Tiberius Claudius Quintilianus.
EDCS-27200145.summary: An inscription recording Emperor Antoninus Pius's rebuilding of an amphitheater, dated to his 20th tribunician power, likely around 156-157 CE.

EDCS-27200146.text_edition: \n annos X filio vixit annos V Salmas Rami ex numero Palmyrenorum coniugi et filio bene merentibus\n
EDCS-27200146.translation: Salmas Rami lived for 10 years for her son, and for 5 years for her husband; for her deserving husband and son, from the number of Palmyrenes.
EDCS-27200146.summary: A funerary inscription for Salmas Rami, commemorating her life and her relationship to her husband and son, likely from the Roman East.

EDCS-27200147.text_edition: \n Tiberius Annius Marcellus et Aurelius Manneus et Mestrinius Firminus et Aurelii Victor et Pacutianus et Maximus dederunt Aurelius Manneus princeps posuit\n
EDCS-27200147.translation: Tiberius Annius Marcellus, Aurelius Manneus, Mestrinius Firminus, and Aurelii Victor, Pacutianus, and Maximus dedicated this; Aurelius Manneus, the leader, erected it.
EDCS-27200147.summary: A dedication of a monument by a group of individuals, with Aurelius Manneus identified as the leader who erected it, likely a public or private commission.

EDCS-27200148.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cai Campani Vitalis |(centurionis) cohortis I Batavorum |(milliariae) stipendiorum VIIII vixit annos XXVII Florius Virilis veteranus ex decurione nepoti pientissimo ponendum curavit\n
EDCS-27200148.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Caius Campanius Vitalis, a centurion of the I Cohort of Batavians, with 9 years of service, lived for 27 years. Florius Virilis, a veteran, formerly a decurion, had this erected for his most dutiful grandson.
EDCS-27200148.summary: A tombstone for Caius Campanius Vitalis, a centurion of the Batavian cohort, who died at 27, erected by his veteran grandfather.

EDCS-27200149.text_edition: \n SIL vixit annos XLVI Arrun<t=I>ius Lucilianus filius vixit annos XVIII Arrun<t=I>ius Latinus filius vixit annos V Ulpia Amadusa coniunx bene merenti posuerunt \n
EDCS-27200149.translation: SIL lived for 46 years. Arrun<t=I>ius Lucilianus, his son, lived for 18 years. Arrun<t=I>ius Latinus, his son, lived for 5 years. Ulpia Amadusa, his wife, set this up for the well-deserving [husband and sons].
EDCS-27200149.summary: A funerary inscription commemorating Ulpia Amadusa and her two sons, Arrun<t=I>ius Lucilianus and Arrun<t=I>ius Latinus, and her husband (indicated by 'SIL'), who died at ages 46, 18, and 5.

EDCS-27200150.text_edition: \n E BAT o vixit annos IIII et OTVM EI CASTIA OH VICINIDEM FELO ES QVANTINE \n
EDCS-27200150.translation: E BAT O lived for 4 years and OTVM EI CASTIA OH VICINIDEM FELO ES QVANTINE [possibly a dedication or epitaph with fragmented names and ages].
EDCS-27200150.summary: A fragmented inscription, possibly an epitaph, recording a short lifespan and potentially names or dedicatory phrases, origin uncertain.

EDCS-27200152.text_edition: \n Dis Inferis Manibus Aelio Tatoni decurioni cohortis II Hispanorum vixit annos XL Aelius Iulianus socer et heres Ae<l=T>(ia) Silvana coniux bene merenti faciendum curaverunt \n
EDCS-27200152.translation: To the Infernal Divine Spirits. Aelius Iulianus, his father-in-law, and Ae<l=T>(ia) Silvana, his wife, had this made for the well-deserving Aelius Tatoni, decurion of the II Cohort of Spaniards, who lived for 40 years.
EDCS-27200152.summary: A tombstone for Aelius Tatoni, a decurion of the II Spanish Cohort, who died at 40, erected by his father-in-law and wife.

EDCS-27200154.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aeliae Maximimiae F vixit annis LXVI Aelius Proculus coniugi bene merenti\n
EDCS-27200154.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aelia Maximinia lived for 66 years. Aelius Proculus for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-27200154.summary: A funerary inscription for Aelia Maximinia, who died at 66, erected by her husband Aelius Proculus.

EDCS-27200155.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Aurelius Dassius vixit annis LXXX Aurelius Vi \n
EDCS-27200155.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus Aurelius Dassius lived for 80 years. Aurelius Vi...
EDCS-27200155.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcus Aurelius Dassius, who died at 80, with the text cut off, likely erected by Aurelius Vi[...].

EDCS-27200157.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titi Aeli Vet Titi Aeli Pauli veterani ex decurione fili vixit nnos XLII Valerius Festivus pupillus bene merenti posuit hic situs est\n
EDCS-27200157.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Tit(i) Aeli Pauli, veteran and formerly decurion, son, lived for 42 years. Valerius Festivus, the ward, set this up for the well-deserving [father]. He lies here.
EDCS-27200157.summary: A tombstone for Titus Aelius Paulus, a veteran and former decurion, who died at 42, erected by his ward Valerius Festivus.

EDCS-27200158.text_edition: \n Aurelius Fabius signifer alae Silianae vixit nnos XXVIIII menses XI Aurelius Reburrus veteranus et Fabia filio pientissimo posuerunt SET et sibi\n
EDCS-27200158.translation: Aurelius Fabius, standard-bearer of the Ala Gallica, lived 29 years and 11 months. Aurelius Reburrus, a veteran, and Fabia erected this for their most devoted son and for themselves.
EDCS-27200158.summary: This funerary monument records the life and death of Aurelius Fabius, a standard-bearer, and was erected by his parents.

EDCS-27200159.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Carinus cornicularius a<l=I>(a)<e=I> Silianae vixit annis Aurelia Serconiux pientissimo coniugi posuit bene merenti \n
EDCS-27200159.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Carinus, a signaller of the Ala Gallica, lived [his life]. Aurelia Serconiux placed this for her most devoted husband, who deserved well.
EDCS-27200159.summary: This tombstone commemorates Aurelius Carinus, a signaller, and was erected by his wife Aurelia Serconiux.

EDCS-27200160.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus inius Verecundus veteranus ex duplicario vixit annos LIII Aurelia Sabina coniugi \n
EDCS-27200160.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Verecundus, a veteran and former second-in-command, lived 53 years. Aurelia Sabina placed this for her husband.
EDCS-27200160.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Verecundus, a veteran, and was erected by his wife Aurelia Sabina.

EDCS-27200161.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ulpius Saturninus eques al(ae) Thraecum sic vixit annos XXXV Ulpius Valentinus fratri bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-27200161.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Ulpius Saturninus, an equestrian of the Thracian cavalry, lived 35 years. Ulpius Valentinus erected this for his brother, who deserved well.
EDCS-27200161.summary: This tombstone was erected by Ulpius Valentinus for his deceased brother, Ulpius Saturninus, a cavalryman.

EDCS-27200162.text_edition: \n Aurelius Mucapor vixit annos <L=I>XXX Aurelius Gemellinus vixit annos Aurelia Re vixit annos \n
EDCS-27200162.translation: Aurelius Mucapor lived 30 years. Aurelius Gemellinus lived [his life]. Aurelia Re lived [her life].
EDCS-27200162.summary: This fragmented inscription records the lifespans of three individuals: Aurelius Mucapor, Aurelius Gemellinus, and Aurelia Re.

EDCS-27200163.text_edition: \n Fortunae Augustae Caius Aurelius Atilianus procurator Augusti\n
EDCS-27200163.translation: To Imperial Fortune. Caius Aurelius Atilianus, procurator of the Emperor.
EDCS-27200163.summary: This dedication to the goddess Fortuna Augusta was made by Caius Aurelius Atilianus, an imperial procurator.

EDCS-27200164.text_edition: \n Fortunae Augustae Ulpius Masculinus veteranus ex signifero legionis V Macedonicae et <de=ED>curio coloniae Napocae pro salute sua et suorum posuit dedicata Kalendis Septembribus Cilone II et Libone consulibus \n
EDCS-27200164.translation: To Imperial Fortune. Ulpius Masculinus, a veteran and former standard-bearer of the Legio V Macedonica, and a decurion of the colony of Napoca, erected this for his own safety and that of his family. Dedicated on the Kalends of September, during the consulship of Cilo (II) and Libo.
EDCS-27200164.summary: This dedication to Fortuna Augusta was made by Ulpius Masculinus, a veteran and local official, for the well-being of himself and his family, dated to 97 AD.

EDCS-27200165.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo ex visu Publius Aelius Maximus\n
EDCS-27200165.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, in accordance with a vision. Publius Aelius Maximus.
EDCS-27200165.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Publius Aelius Maximus, apparently prompted by a divine vision.

EDCS-27200166.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Publicius Antonius Probus procurator Auggustorum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-27200166.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Caius Publicius Antonius Probus, procurator of the Emperors, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-27200166.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Caius Publicius Antonius Probus, an imperial procurator, recording the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-27200167.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Valerius Catullinus procurator Augusti\n
EDCS-27200167.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Valerius Catullinus, procurator of the Emperor.
EDCS-27200167.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus made by Valerius Catullinus, an imperial procurator.

EDCS-27200168.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Minervae pro salute ddominorum nnostrorum Marci Antoni Gordiani Pii Felicis Augusti et Sabiniae Tranquillinae Augustae Lucius Laelius Terentianus decurio coloniae aedilis praefectus\n
EDCS-27200168.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Juno Regina, and Minerva, for the health of our lords Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus and Sabinia Tranquillina Augusta, Lucius Laelius Terentianus, decurion of the colony, aedile, prefect, [dedicated this].
EDCS-27200168.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter, Juno, and Minerva for the health of the emperors Gordian III and Tranquillina, dedicated by a local official, Lucius Laelius Terentianus, likely during the reign of Gordian III (238-244 AD).

EDCS-27200169.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Eruseno sic Aurelius Alexander votum solvit libens\n
EDCS-27200169.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Erusenus Aurelius Alexander willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27200169.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Best and Greatest by Aurelius Alexander, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200170.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Taviano pro salute Imperatoris Antonini et Marci Aureli Caesaris Galatae consistentes municipio pos<u=I>erunt \n
EDCS-27200170.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest Taviano, the residents of the municipality erected [this] for the health of the Emperor Antoninus and Marcus Aurelius Caesar Galata.
EDCS-27200170.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument to Jupiter Best and Greatest Taviano for the health of emperors Antoninus Pius and Marcus Aurelius, erected by the citizens of a municipality, likely in the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-27200171.translation: Maessianus willingly fulfilled his vow to Mercury.
EDCS-27200171.summary: This is a simple votive inscription by Maessianus to the god Mercury, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-27200172.text_edition: \n Numini Augusti Marcus Ulpius Caecilius Bassianus Callisth maggistri Augustalis sic coloniae Napocae donum dederunt\n
EDCS-27200172.translation: Marcus Ulpius Caecilius Bassianus Callisthus, magistrates of the Augustalis, and the colony of Napoca gave this gift to the divine power of the Emperor.
EDCS-27200172.summary: This inscription records a gift to the numen of the emperor by local magistrates and the colony of Napoca, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200173.text_edition: \n Deo Silvano pro salute Aeli Iuliani Securus posuit\n
EDCS-27200173.translation: Securus set this up for the god Silvanus for the health of Aelius Iulianus.
EDCS-27200173.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Silvanus by Securus for the health of Aelius Iulianus, probably dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200174.text_edition: \n Veneri Victrici Aurelius Umbrianus veteranus ex decurione votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-27200174.translation: Aurelius Umbrianus, a veteran formerly a decurion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Venus Victrix.
EDCS-27200174.summary: This is a votive inscription to Venus Victrix by Aurelius Umbrianus, a veteran and former decurion, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200175.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Lucio Aelio Aurelio Commodo Pio Felici Augusto Sarmatico Germanico maximo Brittannico ob honorem decurionatus Aeli Constantis procuratoris eius et Iuli Pacatiani quondam procuratoris Titus Fl(avius) IanuariusR coloniae et Titus Flavius Germanus decurio coloniae quod decuriones alares promiserunt pecunia sua posuerunt locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-27200175.translation: Titus Flavius Ianuarius of the colony and Titus Flavius Germanus, a decurion of the colony, erected this with their own money, as the alar decurions had promised, for the honor of Aelius Constantis, his procurator, and Iulius Pacatianus, formerly procurator, on a site granted by decree of the decurions, to the Emperor Caesar Lucius Aelius Aurelius Commodus Pius Felix Augustus Sarmaticus Germanicus Maximus Britannicus.
EDCS-27200175.summary: This is a complex dedication to Commodus by two local officials, Titus Flavius Ianuarius and Titus Flavius Germanus, commemorating the honors granted to the emperor's procurators, likely erected around 175-180 AD.

EDCS-27200176.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelia Aurelia vixit nnis XVII Aurelius Marinianus filius eius vixit anno I mensibus IIII Aurelius Babus veteranus pater posuit sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-27200176.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Aurelia lived 17 years. Aurelius Marinianus, her son, lived 1 year and 4 months. Aurelius Babus, the veteran father, set this up. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-27200176.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelia and her infant son Aurelius Marinianus, set up by their father and husband, Aurelius Babus, a veteran, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200177.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Traiano vixit annos XXVIII filio et Aelio Libero alumno vixit annos III Publius Aelius Dacianus decurio et aedilis coloniae faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-27200177.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Publius Aelius Traianus, who lived 28 years, and Aelius Liber, his adopted son, who lived 3 years. Publius Aelius Dacianus, decurion and aedile of the colony, saw to its construction.
EDCS-27200177.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Aelius Traianus and his adopted son Aelius Liber, erected by Publius Aelius Dacianus, a local official, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27200178.text_edition: \n iae O DEC et Aurelius anus A \n
EDCS-27200178.translation: To Dec... and Aurelia anus A.
EDCS-27200178.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication, likely to a deity, with the names of a dedicant and a woman.

EDCS-27200179.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Aurelius Papia Augustalis coloniae Napocae vixit annis LX Aurelia Bona coniux marito perficiendum curavit\n
EDCS-27200179.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Aurelius Papia Augustalis of the colony of Napoca lived for 60 years. Aurelia Bona, his wife, had this constructed for her husband.
EDCS-27200179.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Aurelius Papia Augustalis, erected by his wife Aurelia Bona, from the colony of Napoca.

EDCS-27200180.text_edition: \n Severo et Quintiano consulibus nomina Asianorum Germanus sprirarchaes Tattaro Hermes Dizo Gaius Hyius Zoilus Valerius Zoilianus Crescens Eptala Ulpius Mucianus Carus magister Surius Tzinto Longinianus Ulpianus Iulius Hermescus Nepos Agrippinus Valerianus Curinius Rev Co Epipodia mater Valentina Augusta Asclepiodote Tiberina Maximina Tzinta Iustina Cornificia Longina Cornelia Vera Hilara Graeca Rufina Victorina Paula Zoilianus scripsit\n
EDCS-27200180.translation: In the consulship of Severus and Quintianus. The names of the Asians: Germanus, sprirarchaes. Tattaro, Hermes, Dizo, Gaius, Hyius, Zoilus, Valerius Zoilianus, Crescens, Eptala, Ulpius Mucianus, Carus, the magistrate Surius, Tzinto, Longinianus, Ulpianus, Iulius Hermescus, Nepos, Agrippinus, Valerianus, Curinius, Rev, Co, Epipodia, mother Valentina, Augusta, Asclepiodote, Tiberina, Maximina, Tzinta, Iustina, Cornificia Longina, Cornelia Vera, Hilara, Graeca, Rufina, Victorina, Paula. Zoilianus wrote.
EDCS-27200180.summary: This is a list of names, likely associated with an ethnic or professional group (Asians), recorded during the consulship of Severus and Quintianus.

EDCS-27200183.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelia Sura vixit annos XXX et Aelius Saturninus vixit annos III et Aelius Valentinus vixit annos III Aelius Siro filiis et coniugi pientissimis\n
EDCS-27200183.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aelia Sura lived for 30 years, and Aelius Saturninus lived for 3 years, and Aelius Valentinus lived for 3 years. Aelius Siro [dedicated this] to his most dutiful sons and wife.
EDCS-27200183.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aelia Sura, Aelius Saturninus, and Aelius Valentinus, dedicated by Aelius Siro to his sons and wife.

EDCS-27300009.summary: This inscription likely records a soldier, Aelius Deiotarus, associated with the Legio XIII Gemina.

EDCS-27300068.text_edition: \n Ammius Crescens Silvano votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-27300068.translation: Ammius Crescens willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Silvanus.
EDCS-27300068.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Ammius Crescens fulfilling a vow to the god Silvanus.

EDCS-27300083.text_edition: \n Fortunae Reduci Lari Viali Romae Aeternae Quintus Axius Aelianus vir egregius procurator Auggustorum Ioni\n
EDCS-27300083.translation: To Fortuna Redux, to the Eternal City of Rome. Quintus Axius Aelianus, an outstanding man, procurator of the Emperors, [dedicated this] at Ionia.
EDCS-27300083.summary: This is a dedication to Fortuna Redux and Rome by Quintus Axius Aelianus, an imperial procurator, possibly made at Ionia.

EDCS-27300084.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cai Iuli Sabini militis legionis XIII geminae librarius sic a rationibus vixit annos XXX Caius Iulius Valens IIvir iure dicundo et Cominia Florentina parentes infelicissimi\n
EDCS-27300084.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Of Caius Iulius Sabinus, soldier of Legio XIII Gemina, scribe of accounts. He lived for 30 years. Caius Iulius Valens, duovir with judicial authority, and Cominia Florentina, his most unhappy parents.
EDCS-27300084.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Sabinus, a soldier and scribe of Legio XIII Gemina, erected by his parents who held civic office.

EDCS-27300087.summary: This is a tombstone or memorial simply naming Olympia Faustina.

EDCS-27300090.summary: This is a brief ownership mark or dedication, likely indicating property belonging to Quintus Aponius Bassus.

EDCS-27300091.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely recording the name of Quintus Cornelius Cornelianus, possibly a dedication or ownership mark.

EDCS-27300092.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely recording the name of Lucius Domitius Primanus, possibly a dedication or ownership mark.

EDCS-27300094.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Hadriani filio divi Traiani Parthici nepoti divi Nervae pronepoti Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate VIII imperatori II consuli IIII patri patriae res publica municipii Hadriani Drobetensium decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-27300094.translation: To Emperor Caesar, son of the divine Hadrian, grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of the divine Nerva, Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, chief priest, in his eighth year of tribunician power, twice supreme commander, twice consul, father of the fatherland; by decree of the town council, on the proposal of the town council, the municipality of Hadrian's Drobetae erected this.
EDCS-27300094.summary: This is a formal dedication to Emperor Antoninus Pius by the municipality of Hadrian's Drobetae, dated to his eighth tribunician power, indicating a significant public building or monument.

EDCS-27300095.text_edition: \n Matri Deum Magnae Iulia Materna T votum solvit libens merito porticum impendio suo fecit\n
EDCS-27300095.translation: Iulia Materna, devoted and deserving, fulfilled her vow to the Great Mother of the Gods and built the portico at her own expense.
EDCS-27300095.summary: This inscription records a private vow fulfilled by Iulia Materna, who built a portico at her own expense dedicated to the Magna Mater (Mother of the Gods).

EDCS-27300096.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caius Valerius Victorinus veteranus legionis V Macedonicae ex beneficiario tribuni vixit annos LV Caius Valerius Mercurialis generi suo et Aurelia Calliroe coniugi bene merenti posuerunt\n
EDCS-27300096.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Caius Valerius Victorinus, a veteran of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, formerly a subordinate of a tribune, lived 55 years. Caius Valerius Mercurialis, his son-in-law, and Aurelia Calliroe, his deserving wife, erected this monument to him.
EDCS-27300096.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by a son-in-law and wife for Caius Valerius Victorinus, a veteran, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27300097.text_edition: \n IAC interfecta a latronibus et vindicata Ulcudius Baedari et Sutta Epicadi parentes pientissimae filiae titulum posuerunt Dis Inferis Manibus Ulcudius Baedari vixit annos L\n
EDCS-27300097.translation: IAC, killed by bandits and avenged. Ulcudius Baedari and Sutta Epicadi erected this monument to their most devoted daughter. Ulcudius Baedari lived 50 years.
EDCS-27300097.summary: This is a funerary monument for a daughter named IAC, who was killed by bandits, erected by her parents, and also records the life span of one of the parents.

EDCS-27300098.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Antonia Calliste vixit nnos LX Diogenes Moschi coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-27300098.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Antonia Calliste lived 60 years. Diogenes Moschi erected this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-27300098.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonia Calliste, erected by her husband Diogenes Moschi, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27300099.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Vario Maroni vixit annis XLVI\n
EDCS-27300099.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Vario Maro lived 46 years.
EDCS-27300099.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph recording the name and age of Vario Maro.

EDCS-27300100.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo defensori et tutatori Caius Iulius Sennianus cum Iulia Rufina coniuge votum libens posuit\n
EDCS-27300100.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, protector and guardian. Caius Iulius Sennianus, with his wife Iulia Rufina, gladly set up this vow.
EDCS-27300100.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Caius Iulius Sennianus and his wife Iulia Rufina.

EDCS-27300103.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Claudia Amba vixit annos XX fecit in Dacia nnos V Claudius Montanus immunis ex numero Surorum sagittariorum pater eius bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-27300103.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Claudia Amba lived 20 years. Claudius Montanus, a tax-exempt soldier from the Sura archers, who served 5 years in Dacia, erected this for his deserving daughter.
EDCS-27300103.summary: This is a funerary monument for Claudia Amba, erected by her father Claudius Montanus, an archer serving in Dacia, likely from the Roman military presence in the province.

EDCS-27300104.translation: To the Divine Spirits, Antonius Nicanoris.
EDCS-27300104.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Antonius Nicanoris, likely a tombstone dedication.

EDCS-27300105.text_edition: \n Pliciae Var Capitonis Ulpius Pulcher <ge=CI>ner bene merenti \n
EDCS-27300105.translation: Ulpius Pulcher to his well-deserving Plicia Varia, daughter of Capito.
EDCS-27300105.summary: This epitaph was set up by Ulpius Pulcher for his deserving wife, Plicia Varia, daughter of Capito.

EDCS-27300106.text_edition: \n Suleviis Montanis Titus Iulius lapidarius votum libens posuit\n
EDCS-27300106.translation: Titus Iulius the stonecutter willingly set up a vow to the Sulevian gods.
EDCS-27300106.summary: This is a votive inscription by Titus Iulius, a stonecutter, dedicated to the Sulevian gods.

EDCS-27300107.text_edition: \n Aurelius Ciiunus |(centurio) legionis V Macedonicae vixit nnos LIX posuerunt filiae<s> Aurelia Ascia et Aurelia Marcellina patri pientissimo titulum \n
EDCS-27300107.translation: Aurelius Ciiunus, a centurion of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, lived 59 years. His daughters Aurelia Ascia and Aurelia Marcellina erected this monument to their most devoted father.
EDCS-27300107.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Ciiunus, a centurion in the Fifth Macedonian Legion, erected by his daughters.

EDCS-27300108.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Calanus vixit annos LI et militavit XXV \n
EDCS-27300108.translation: To the Divine Spirits, Aurelius Calanus lived 51 years and served 25 years.
EDCS-27300108.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan and military service of Aurelius Calanus.

EDCS-27300109.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titus F Titi <f=T>(ilius) Sera stipendiorum III vixit annos XXX \n
EDCS-27300109.translation: To the Divine Spirits, Titus F. Sera, son of Titus, served 3 years and lived 30 years.
EDCS-27300109.summary: This funerary inscription records the short life and military service of Titus F. Sera.

EDCS-27300110.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Sicinius Lucanus veteranus legionis XIII geminae mater pientissima posuit\n
EDCS-27300110.translation: To the Divine Spirits, Sicinius Lucanus, a veteran of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion. His most devoted mother set this up.
EDCS-27300110.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sicinius Lucanus, a veteran of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, erected by his mother.

EDCS-27300111.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valerius Longinus veteranus numeri Campestrorum vixit nnos XXXXVIII Aurelia Pirusi virginio bene merenti posuit M\n
EDCS-27300111.translation: To the Divine Spirits, Valerius Longinus, a veteran of the Field Troop, lived 48 years. Aurelia Pirusi set this up for her deserving freedman.
EDCS-27300111.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of veteran Valerius Longinus, set up by his freedwoman Aurelia Pirusi.

EDCS-27300112.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelia Secundina vixit annos XIIII et Aelia Crispina vixit annos IIII Aelii Secundus et Deccia parentes filiis pientissimis et Aelius Consius maritus\n
EDCS-27300112.translation: To the Divine Spirits, Aelia Secundina lived 14 years and Aelia Crispina lived 4 years. Aelius Secundus and Deccia, parents, [erected this] for their most devoted sons, and Aelius Consius, husband [erected this].
EDCS-27300112.summary: This is a funerary inscription for two young girls, Aelia Secundina and Aelia Crispina, erected by their parents and husband/brother.

EDCS-27300113.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelia Valeria vixit nnos LX et Tiberius Claudius Festus vixit annos XX et Aelius Valerius vixit annos XVI Aelius Saturninus Aelia Valentina liberti patronae bene meritae posuerunt\n
EDCS-27300113.translation: To the Divine Spirits, Aelia Valeria lived 60 years and Tiberius Claudius Festus lived 20 years and Aelius Valerius lived 16 years. Aelius Saturninus and Aelia Valentina set this up for their deserving freedwoman patroness.
EDCS-27300113.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Aelia Valeria, Tiberius Claudius Festus, and Aelius Valerius, set up by freedmen.

EDCS-27300114.text_edition: \n P Ulpianus prob coniugi bene merenti\n
EDCS-27300114.summary: A private epitaph, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, recording a husband's dedication to his wife.

EDCS-27300115.text_edition: \n canius vixit annis XXXV Aflicta Caesia coniugi bene merito posuit\n
EDCS-27300115.translation: Canius lived 35 years. Afflicta Caesia placed this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-27300115.summary: A private epitaph, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, recording a wife's dedication to her deceased husband and his age at death.

EDCS-27300120.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus pontifex maximus tr(ibunicia) potestate XII consul V imperator VI pater patriae fecit per cohortem I Flaviam Ulpiam Hispanorum milliariam civium Romanorum equitatam a Potaissa Napocae milia passuum X\n
EDCS-27300120.translation: Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajanus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 12th time, consul for the 5th time, Imperator for the 6th time, father of the country, made [this road] through Cohors I Flavia Ulpia Hispana milliaria, citizen Romans, cavalry, for 10 miles from Potaissa to Napoca.
EDCS-27300120.summary: A milestone or road inscription from the reign of Trajan (98-117 CE), marking a road built by a Roman auxiliary cohort.

EDCS-27800803.text_edition: \n Marti V pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris divi fili Nervae Traiani Germanici Dacici pontificis maximi tribunicia potestate XII consuli V cohors II Hispanorum\n
EDCS-27800803.translation: To Mars. For the safety of the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Nerva Trajanus Germanicus Dacicus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 12th time, consul for the 5th time, Cohors II Hispana.
EDCS-27800803.summary: A dedication to Mars, likely from the reign of Trajan (98-117 CE), by Cohors II Hispana for the emperor's safety.

EDCS-27800804.text_edition: \n tius signifer alae I Frontonianae Tungrorum coniugi piissimae bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-27800804.translation: Tius, standard-bearer of Ala I Frontoniana Tungrorum, placed this for his most dutiful, well-deserving wife.
EDCS-27800804.summary: A private epitaph, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, recording a soldier's dedication to his wife.

EDCS-27800805.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Severus et P Surus M miles ex Marcia Donata heres coniugi bene merenti P posuit\n
EDCS-27800805.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Severus and P. Surus, soldiers, placed this for Marcia Donata, heir, to her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-27800805.summary: A private epitaph, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, recording a dedication to a deceased husband by his wife and possibly another soldier.

EDCS-27800807.summary: A very fragmentary inscription, possibly a tombstone, mentioning two individuals, 'Alexander' and his son, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27800809.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo cohors IIII Hispanorum equitata cui praeest Publius Didius Italicus praefectus domi sic Mauretania Caesarensi votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-27800809.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Cohors IIII Hispana, cavalry, which is commanded by Publius Didius Italicus, prefect, paid its vow willingly and deservedly from Mauretania Caesarensis.
EDCS-27800809.summary: A votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, dedicated by an auxiliary cohort and its commander.

EDCS-27800810.text_edition: \n Aeterno sacrum Aurelia Galla coniux Aureli Decimi imaginiferi M\n
EDCS-27800810.translation: Sacred to the Eternal. Aurelia Galla, wife of Aurelius Decimus, standard-bearer.
EDCS-27800810.summary: A private dedication, likely from the Roman Imperial period, made by a wife to an eternal deity, mentioning her husband.

EDCS-27800811.text_edition: \n Dianae sacrum Publius <C=G>aesonius Celer votum solvit libens merito \n
EDCS-27800811.translation: Sacred to Diana. Publius Caesolius Celer paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-27800811.summary: A votive inscription to Diana, likely from the Roman Imperial period, recording a personal vow fulfilled.

EDCS-27800812.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aulus Tapetius Amethystus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-27800812.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Aulus Tapetius Amethystus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27800812.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by Aulus Tapetius Amethystus.

EDCS-27800813.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aulus Tapetius Epius\n
EDCS-27800813.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Aulus Tapetius Epius.
EDCS-27800813.summary: A dedication to Jupiter, likely a votive offering, by Aulus Tapetius Epius.

EDCS-27800815.text_edition: \n Obilae et Herculi Marcus D P decurio coloniae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-27800815.translation: To Obilas and Hercules, Marcus D[omitius] P[ertinax?], decurion of the colony, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27800815.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Obilas and Hercules by Marcus D. P., a decurion of the colony.

EDCS-27800816.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quintus Valerius Macrinus veteranus legionis V Macedonicae missicius praefecti decurio? coloniae Dacicae Sarmizegetusae \n
EDCS-27800816.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Quintus Valerius Macrinus, veteran of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, discharged by the prefect, perhaps a decurion of the Dacian colony of Sarmizegetusa.
EDCS-27800816.summary: A tombstone inscription for Quintus Valerius Macrinus, a veteran of the Fifth Macedonian Legion and potentially a decurion in Sarmizegetusa.

EDCS-27800818.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Maturus veteranus cohortis Commagenorum vixit annos L Aurelius Primanus vixit annos XVIIII Aurelius Surus milis sic numeri Maurorum Miciensium vixit annos XX Aurelia Eustina vixit annos BS XVIII Aurelia Surilla mater pro pietate fecit bene merentibus\n
EDCS-27800818.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: Aurelius Maturus, veteran of the Cohors Commagenorum, lived 50 years; Aurelius Primanus, lived 17 years; Aurelius Surus, soldier of the Miciensian Moors, lived 20 years; Aurelia Eustina, lived 18 years. Aurelia Surilla, their mother, made this for her dutifully beloved children.
EDCS-27800818.summary: A tombstone inscription for Aurelius Maturus and his family members, erected by their mother Aurelia Surilla, indicating their ages at death.
