EDCS-29000024.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Philumene, recording her age and burial, and dedicated by her husband Philetus to his deserving wife.

EDCS-29100046.text_edition: \n Sabino et Anullino consulibus dis deabusq(ue) immortalibus pro salute domini nostri in honorem nocturnorum Marcus Aurelius Frontonianus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-29100046.translation: In the consulship of Sabinus and Anullinus, for the health of our lord, Marcus Aurelius Frontonianus fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly to the immortal gods and goddesses, in honor of the Nocturni.
EDCS-29100046.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Marcus Aurelius Frontonianus to the gods and goddesses for the health of the emperor, during the consulship of Sabinus and Anullinus.

EDCS-29100047.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo M N Lucretianus praefectus alae II Pannoniorum votum solvit libens\n
EDCS-29100047.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Lucretianus, prefect of the II Ala of Pannonians, fulfilled his vow willingly.
EDCS-29100047.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucretianus, prefect of the ala II Pannoniorum, who fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-29100048.text_edition: \n Invicto Mithrae Surus votum solvit libens\n
EDCS-29100048.translation: Surus gladly fulfilled his vow to the unconquered Mithras.
EDCS-29100048.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Mithras by Surus, likely dating from the imperial period.

EDCS-29100051.text_edition: \n Deanae Augustae Caius Catullinius Adamantus et Caius Catullinius Achilleus\n
EDCS-29100051.translation: Gaius Catullinius Adamantus and Gaius Catullinius Achilleus dedicated this to the Augustan Dea.
EDCS-29100051.summary: This is a dedication to the Dea Augusta by two individuals named Gaius Catullinius, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-29100052.translation: Restituta dedicated this to Aesculapius and Hygieia.
EDCS-29100052.summary: This is a dedication to the gods of healing, Aesculapius and Hygieia, by Restituta, probably from the imperial period.

EDCS-29100053.text_edition: \n IMD N Ulpius Manus An Marcus Ip Septimus Marcus Iulius M Iulius Vinc Iulius P \n
EDCS-29100053.translation: Ulpius Manus, freedman of the Divine Emperor, and Marcus, son of Septimus Marcus, and Iulius Vinc, son of Marcus Iulius, and P. Iulius [dedi...].
EDCS-29100053.summary: This inscription appears to record a dedication or honorific text involving multiple individuals, including Ulpius Manus, a freedman of the emperor, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-29100055.translation: Ignus erected this for Surilla, who deserved well of him.
EDCS-29100055.summary: This is a funerary or commemorative inscription set up by Ignus for Surilla, likely dating from the imperial period.

EDCS-29100056.translation: Rufinus of Stenna gave this gift to Silvanus.
EDCS-29100056.summary: This is a dedication of a gift to the god Silvanus by Rufinus of Stenna, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-29100057.text_edition: \n Herculi et Silvano vexillatio legionis XIII geminae Antoninianae Aurelio Arimo votum merito posuit immuni V I\n
EDCS-29100057.translation: The detachment of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina Antoniniana set up this votive offering to Hercules and Silvanus for Aurelius Arimus, who deserved well and was exempt.
EDCS-29100057.summary: This is a votive inscription by a military detachment of the XIII Gemina legion to Hercules and Silvanus for a soldier named Aurelius Arimus, likely from the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-29100058.summary: This is a dedication to the deities Liber Pater and Libera, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-29100059.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publius Aelius Ianuarius vixit annos LX et Publia Aelia Dionisia sic vixit annos XXXXV Publius Aelius Germanus vixit annos XXV Publius Aelius Dionisius vixit annos XVIII Publius Aelius Cassianus parentibus bene merentibus posuit\n
EDCS-29100059.translation: Publius Aelius Ianuarius lived for 60 years, and Publia Aelia Dionisia lived for 45 years, Publius Aelius Germanus lived for 25 years, Publius Aelius Dionisius lived for 18 years; Publius Aelius Cassianus set this up for his well-deserving parents.
EDCS-29100059.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Publius Aelius Cassianus for his parents and siblings, recording their ages at death, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-29100060.text_edition: \n vixit nnos militavit annos XV Aulusanus heres bene merenti posuit arbitrante Ingenui\n
EDCS-29100060.translation: [He/She] lived... years, served for 15 years; Aulus [his/her son/heir] set this up for the deserving [his/her father/mother], under the supervision of Ingenuius.
EDCS-29100060.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording years of life and military service, set up by an heir under the supervision of another individual, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-29100061.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Dionysius veteranus cohortis II Flaviae Commagenorum vixit annos Ulpia Demetria et -- faciendum curav---\n
EDCS-29100061.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Dionysius, a veteran of the Second Cohort of Flavian Commageni, lived... Ulpia Demetria and -- had this made.
EDCS-29100061.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Dionysius, a veteran, with a dedication also mentioning Ulpia Demetria, likely his wife or relative.

EDCS-29100063.text_edition: \n Libero Patri et Liberae Matri Secundinus votum libens merito\n
EDCS-29100063.translation: To Liber Pater and Libera Mater, Secundinus willingly paid his vow, deservedly.
EDCS-29100063.summary: This is a votive dedication to the deities Liber Pater and Libera Mater by Secundinus.

EDCS-29100064.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Iulio Philippo Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate II consuli patri patriae proconsuli numerus singularium Britannicorum Philippiani devoti numini maiestatique eius\n
EDCS-29100064.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus Pius Felix Augustus, supreme pontiff, in his second tribunician power, consul, father of the fatherland, proconsul, the unit of Philippian bodyguards, devoted to his divine majesty and authority.
EDCS-29100064.summary: This is an official inscription recording the devotion of the Philippian bodyguards unit to Emperor Philip the Arab.

EDCS-29100065.text_edition: \n Dianae sacrum pro salute Cai Arri Antonini legati Augusti pro praetore Marcus Verius Superstes |(centurio) legionis V Macedonicae praepositus numeri Germanicianorum votum solvit\n
EDCS-29100065.translation: Sacred to Diana. For the health of Caius Arrius Antoninus, imperial legate with proconsular authority, Marcus Verius Superstes, centurion of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, prefect of the Germanician cohort, paid his vow.
EDCS-29100065.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating a monument to Diana for the health of the legate Arrius Antoninus, by Marcus Verius Superstes, a centurion and prefect.

EDCS-29100066.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iovivipulsori Grattius Paternus decurio coloniae posuit\n
EDCS-29100066.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Jupiter the Protector. Grattius Paternus, a decurion of the colony, erected this.
EDCS-29100066.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Jupiter Protector, erected by Grattius Paternus, a decurion.

EDCS-29100067.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Appennino Marcus Aurelius Valentinus decurio coloniae votum libens solvit merito\n
EDCS-29100067.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Protector of the Apennines. Marcus Aurelius Valentinus, a decurion of the colony, willingly paid his vow, deservedly.
EDCS-29100067.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus of the Apennines by Marcus Aurelius Valentinus, a decurion.

EDCS-29100068.text_edition: \n Marti Lucius Grattius decurio coloniae posuit\n
EDCS-29100068.translation: To Mars. Lucius Grattius, a decurion of the colony, erected this.
EDCS-29100068.summary: This is a dedication to Mars erected by Lucius Grattius, a decurion of the colony.

EDCS-29100071.text_edition: \n Deo Malagbeli Titus Flavius Aper scriba coloniae ex voto\n
EDCS-29100071.translation: To the god Malagbel. Titus Flavius Aper, scribe of the colony, according to his vow.
EDCS-29100071.summary: This is a votive dedication to the god Malagbel by Titus Flavius Aper, the colony's scribe.

EDCS-29100072.text_edition: \n Soli Invicto Mithrae Cornelius Cornelianus ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-29100072.translation: To Sol Invictus Mithras. Cornelius Cornelianus erected this according to his vow.
EDCS-29100072.summary: This is a votive dedication to Sol Invictus Mithras by Cornelius Cornelianus.

EDCS-29100074.translation: Papiria Zoe erected this according to her vow.
EDCS-29100074.summary: This is a votive dedication by Papiria Zoe.

EDCS-29100078.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publius Aelius Theimes veteranus ex |(centurione) cohortis I Vindelicorum et IIviralis coloniae Dacicae Sarmizegetusae vixit nnos LXXXVIII et Publius Aelius Bericio vixit nnos XV et Publius Aelius Zabdibol vixit nnos XI et Aelia Phamenis vixit an[nos ------\n
EDCS-29100078.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Publius Aelius Theimes, a veteran formerly a centurion of the I Cohort of Vindelici and also a *duumvir* of the colony Dacica Sarmizegetusa, lived 88 years, and Publius Aelius Bericio lived 15 years, and Publius Aelius Zabdibol lived 11 years, and Aelia Phamenis lived [-----] years.
EDCS-29100078.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lives of multiple members of the Aelius family, including a veteran and local official, in the Dacian colony of Sarmizegetusa.

EDCS-29100079.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Cassio Marcioni Augustali coloniae vixit annos LXX et Cassiae Cassianae vixit annos XVIIII Cassia Rufina coniugi et filiae piissimis\n
EDCS-29100079.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Cassius Marcion, Augustalis of the colony, lived 70 years, and Cassia Cassiana lived 19 years. Cassia Rufina set this up for her most devoted wife and daughter.
EDCS-29100079.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Cassia Rufina to her deceased wife and daughter, mentioning their ages and Lucius Cassius Marcion, an Augustalis of the colony.

EDCS-29100081.text_edition: \n Caio Valerio Cai filio Papiria Valeriano IIviro coloniae Sarmizegetusae metropolis et decurioni coloniae Apulensis equiti Romano Valerii Niceforus sic et Protus liberti patrono dignissimo locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-29100081.translation: To Gaius Valerius Valerianus, son of Gaius, of the Papiria tribe, *duumvir* of the metropolis colony Sarmizegetusa and decurion of the colony Apulensis, a Roman knight. Valerii Niceforus and Protus, his freedmen, granted this site to their most worthy patron by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-29100081.summary: A dedication recording the grant of a burial plot by freedmen to their patron, Gaius Valerius Valerianus, who held prominent civic and equestrian positions in Sarmizegetusa and Apulum.

EDCS-29100082.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ulpio Chelidoni vixit annos XLVI Lucius Cassius \n
EDCS-29100082.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Ulpius Chelidon lived 46 years. Lucius Cassius...
EDCS-29100082.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Ulpius Chelidon, with the dedicant Lucius Cassius.

EDCS-29100085.text_edition: \n Dis Inferis Manibus sacrum Septimius Bitus et Septimia Valentina vixerunt annis LIIII posuerunt Septimius Bassus et Aurelius Valens patribus bene merentibus\n
EDCS-29100085.translation: Sacred to the Infernal Gods, the Spirits. Septimius Bitus and Septimia Valentina lived 54 years. Septimius Bassus and Aurelius Valens set this up for their well-deserving fathers.
EDCS-29100085.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Septimius Bassus and Aurelius Valens for their fathers, Septimius Bitus and Septimia Valentina, who lived to 54 years.

EDCS-29100086.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aelius Philip...
EDCS-29100086.summary: A very fragmentary funerary epitaph mentioning Aelius Philip.

EDCS-29100106.summary: An inscription that appears to be a label or record related to a unit, possibly mentioning the name Herculanus and a count of 'singulares'.

EDCS-29100116.summary: A simple dedication or inscription naming Theotimus, son of Stephanos.

EDCS-29100117.summary: A simple dedication or inscription naming Attenia Cyttonis.

EDCS-29100118.text_edition: \n Legionis V Macedonicae piae fidelis pondus I examinatum iussu dddominorum nostrorum Augustorum Valerius Sabinus optio legionis supra scriptae dono dedit\n
EDCS-29100118.translation: Of the V Macedonian Legion, pious and faithful. One pound weight, examined by order of our Lords the Augusti. Valerius Sabinus, optio of the aforementioned legion, donated.
EDCS-29100118.summary: A dedication by an optio, Valerius Sabinus, of the V Macedonian Legion, recording the examination and donation of one pound weight under imperial authority.

EDCS-29200184.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Marcus Aelius Antoninus decurio coloniae Napocae iuridicus municipii Porolissensis\n
EDCS-29200184.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, Marcus Aelius Antoninus, decurion of the colony of Napoca and legal official of the municipium of Porolissensis.
EDCS-29200184.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus by Marcus Aelius Antoninus, a local official in Roman Dacia, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29200185.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Titintius beneficiarius legati votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-29200185.translation: Titintius, a *beneficiarius* of the legate, willingly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus as deserved.
EDCS-29200185.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Dolichenus by Titintius, a soldier serving as a *beneficiarius*, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29200186.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Probus centurio legionis et Apollonia eius votum solverunt\n
EDCS-29200186.translation: Probus, a centurion of the legion, and Apollonia, his wife, fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-29200186.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Probus, a centurion, and his wife Apollonia to Jupiter Dolichenus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29601678.text_edition: \n Deae Nemesi Reginae Caecilius Antoninus ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-29601678.translation: Caecilius Antoninus set this up in fulfillment of a vow to the Royal Goddess Nemesis.
EDCS-29601678.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the goddess Nemesis by Caecilius Antoninus, likely from Roman Dacia.

EDCS-29601679.text_edition: \n Hilarus pro Alexandro fratre deae Nemesi quem periculo liberavit\n
EDCS-29601679.translation: Hilarus, for his brother Alexander, [dedicated this] to the goddess Nemesis, whom she freed from danger.
EDCS-29601679.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Hilarus to the goddess Nemesis on behalf of his brother Alexander, whom the goddess saved from peril.

EDCS-29601680.text_edition: \n Nemesi Augustae Titus Varenius Gallicanus decurio collegii fabrorum coloniae Sarmizegetusae metropolis ter magister ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-29601680.translation: To Nemesis Augusta, Titus Varenius Gallicanus, decurion of the guild of builders of the metropolis colony of Sarmizegetusa, and three times master of the guild, set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-29601680.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Nemesis Augusta by Titus Varenius Gallicanus, a prominent guild official in the Roman colony of Sarmizegetusa, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29601681.translation: To the god Silvanus, Naevius D[id this].
EDCS-29601681.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription recording a dedication to the god Silvanus by Naevius D[...].

EDCS-29601684.text_edition: \n Marcus Procilius Theodorus IIvir coloniae quinquennalis decurio ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-29601684.translation: Marcus Procilius Theodorus, *duumvir* and quinquennial *decurio* of the colony, set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-29601684.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Marcus Procilius Theodorus, a high-ranking local magistrate (*duumvir*) in a Roman colony, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29601688.translation: [... A]mabilis willingly set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-29601688.summary: This fragmentary inscription records a votive offering by a person named Amabilis.

EDCS-29601691.text_edition: \n Imperatore Caesare divi Traiani Parthici filio divi Nervae nepote Traiano Hadriano Augusto pontifice maximo tribunicia potestate XXIII consule III patre patriae et Imperatore Tito Aelio Caesare Antonino Traiani Hadriani Augusti filio divi Traiani Parthici nepote divi Nervae pronepote tribunicia potestate numerus burgariorum et veredariorum Daciae inferioris sub Flavio Constante procuratore Augusti\n
EDCS-29601691.translation: Under Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, chief priest, in his 23rd year of tribunician power, consul for the 3rd time, father of the country; and Emperor Titus Aelius Caesar Antoninus, son of Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, in his tribunician power; [this was set up by] the group of *burgarii* and *veredarii* of Lower Dacia, under Flavius Constans, procurator of the Emperor.
EDCS-29601691.summary: This lengthy inscription records the imperial titulatures of Hadrian and Antoninus Pius, and mentions the *burgarii* (road maintenance workers) and *veredarii* (courier horsemen) of Dacia Inferior under the procurator Flavius Constans, likely dating to 140 CE.

EDCS-29601692.text_edition: \n Imperatore Caesare Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio tribunicia potestate III consule III castra numerus burgariorum et veredariorum quod anguste tenderet duplicato valli pede et inpositis turribus ampliavit per Aquilam Fidum procuratorem Augusti\n
EDCS-29601692.translation: Under Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, holding tribunician power for the third time and consul for the third time, Aquila Fidus, procurator of the Emperor, enlarged the camp of the burgarii and veredarii, which was narrow, by doubling the width of the rampart and adding towers.
EDCS-29601692.summary: This inscription records an army camp expansion undertaken by the procurator Aquila Fidus under Emperor Antoninus Pius.

EDCS-29601693.text_edition: \n Nymphis Hylas vicesimarius ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-29601693.translation: Hylas, a tax collector, set this up in fulfillment of a vow to the Nymphs.
EDCS-29601693.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Nymphs by Hylas, a tax collector.

EDCS-29900102.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ulpia Claudia vixit annos XXXV Ulpius Festus vixit annos XVIII Aelius Certus signifer cohortis I Batavorum coniugi pientissimae faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-29900102.translation: To the Spirits of the departed. Ulpia Claudia lived 35 years. Ulpius Festus lived 18 years. Aelius Certus, standard-bearer of the I Cohort of Batavians, arranged for this to be made for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-29900102.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ulpia Claudia, with a mention of Ulpius Festus and erected by Aelius Certus, a standard-bearer.

EDCS-30100823.text_edition: \n Minervae Victrici pro salute nnorum nnostrorum Auggustorum Imperatoris Severi et Antonini Caesaris Aelius Sabinus librarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30100823.translation: To Minerva Victrix, for the health of our Emperors Severus and Antoninus Caesar. Aelius Sabinus, an imperial scribe, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30100823.summary: This is a votive inscription to Minerva Victrix, dedicated by Aelius Sabinus, a scribe, for the welfare of Emperors Severus and Antoninus.

EDCS-30100824.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelius Borafas Zabdiboli miles ex numero Palmyrenorum vixit annos et Valeria C et Zabdibolus FNRVM FNVFR \n
EDCS-30100824.translation: To the Spirits of the departed. Aelius Borafas, a soldier from the Palmyrene cohort, lived [age missing]. Valeria C [age missing] and Zabdibolus FNRVM FNVFR [relationship and names unclear].
EDCS-30100824.summary: This incomplete funerary inscription commemorates Aelius Borafas, a soldier from the Palmyrenes, and includes partially legible names of others.

EDCS-30100826.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Liccaius Vinentis miles cohortis III campestris beneficiarius tribuni militavit annis XIX vixit annis XL Linda Severus heredes bene merenti posuerunt\n
EDCS-30100826.translation: To the Spirits of the departed. Liccaius Vinentis, a soldier of the III Cohort Campestris, a special duty soldier under a military tribune, served for 19 years and lived for 40 years. Linda Severus, his heirs, set this up for him as deserving.
EDCS-30100826.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and service of Liccaius Vinentis, a soldier, and was erected by his heirs.

EDCS-30100827.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Valerius Marci filius Longinus medicus legionis VII Claudiae ornatus ornamentis decurionalibus a splendidissimo ordine municipii Hadriani Drobetensium vixit annos XXIII Marcus Victorius anio et Victoria Gemina filio pientissimo posuerunt\n
EDCS-30100827.translation: To the Spirits of the departed. Marcus Valerius Longinus, son of Marcus, a physician of the Legio VII Claudia, honored with decorations by the most distinguished council of the municipality of Hadriani Drobetensium, lived 23 years. Marcus Victorius and Victoria Gemina set this up for their most devoted son.
EDCS-30100827.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Marcus Valerius Longinus, a physician decorated for service, who lived 23 years, and was erected by his parents.

EDCS-30100844.text_edition: \n Lucius Marius Valens patronus collegii fabrum\n
EDCS-30100844.translation: Lucius Marius Valens, patron of the guild of artisans.
EDCS-30100844.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Marius Valens, honored as patron of a guild of artisans.

EDCS-30100845.summary: This is a dedication to Marcus Procilius Nicetas.

EDCS-30100846.summary: This incomplete inscription simply names Aelius Vitalis.

EDCS-30100852.text_edition: \n Nemesi sacrum Nepos centurio numeri Palmyrenorum\n
EDCS-30100852.translation: Sacred to Nemesis. Nepos, a centurion of the Palmyrene cohort.
EDCS-30100852.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Nemesis by Nepos, a centurion of the Palmyrene cohort, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-30100853.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico ex voto posuit EPITOT\n
EDCS-30100853.translation: Epitot erected this in fulfillment of a vow to Silvanus Domesticus.
EDCS-30100853.summary: This is a votive dedication to Silvanus Domesticus by Epitot, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-30100854.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio Daciarum Marcus Caelius Iulianus tribunus laticlavius legionis XIII geminae donodono\n
EDCS-30100854.translation: Marcus Caelius Iulianus, a broad-striped tribune of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, dedicated this offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of Dacia.
EDCS-30100854.summary: This is a dedication by Marcus Caelius Iulianus, a high-ranking military officer, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of Dacia, likely dating to the Roman occupation of Dacia.

EDCS-30100856.summary: This is a simple inscription recording that Theotimus made something, likely a monument or offering, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-30100859.text_edition: \n Mercurio Plator Implei sic votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-30100859.translation: Plator Implei gladly and joyfully fulfilled his vow to Mercury.
EDCS-30100859.summary: This is a votive inscription by Plator Implei to Mercury, expressing joy in fulfilling his vow, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-30100860.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Platius.
EDCS-30100860.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Platius, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-30100861.translation: Platius Turi fulfilled his vow to Depulsorius Jupiter.
EDCS-30100861.summary: This is a votive inscription by Platius Turi to a specific epithet of Jupiter, Depulsorius, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-30100862.text_edition: \n Beucus Daeici aram Veneri votum solvit\n
EDCS-30100862.translation: Beucus Daeici erected an altar to Venus and fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30100862.summary: This is a votive inscription by Beucus Daeici, recording the erection of an altar to Venus and the fulfillment of a vow, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-30100864.text_edition: \n Mercurio sacrum Verzo Platoris posuit donum dedit\n
EDCS-30100864.translation: Sacred to Mercury. Verzo Platoris set this up and gave it as a gift.
EDCS-30100864.summary: This is a dedication to Mercury by Verzo Platoris, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-30100865.text_edition: \n Apollini sacrum Plator Implai donum dedit\n
EDCS-30100865.translation: Sacred to Apollo. Plator Implai gave this as a gift.
EDCS-30100865.summary: This is a dedication to Apollo by Plator Implai, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-30100866.translation: Platius Dasantis to the Genius of the Sardians, a gift.
EDCS-30100866.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Platius Dasantis to the Genius of the Sardians, likely a cultic figure associated with the community.

EDCS-30100867.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dasas Loni collegii Sardiatensium donum dedit\n
EDCS-30100867.translation: Dasas Loni gave this gift to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, of the college of the Sardians.
EDCS-30100867.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Dasas Loni, a member of the college of Sardians, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-30100870.text_edition: \n Aelius Quintus Dii Genio collegi(i) Sardia<t=I>arum \n
EDCS-30100870.translation: Aelius Quintus to the Genius of the college of the Sardians.
EDCS-30100870.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Aelius Quintus to the Genius of the Sardian college.

EDCS-30100871.text_edition: \n Fortunae Augustae sacrum Plator Sar votum solvit libens\n
EDCS-30100871.translation: Sacred to Augusta Fortuna. Plator Sar willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30100871.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Plator Sar to Augusta Fortuna.

EDCS-30100872.text_edition: \n Asclepio Marcus Ulpius Clemens et Aelius Mes ex voto posuerunt\n
EDCS-30100872.translation: Marcus Ulpius Clemens and Aelius Mes set this up in fulfillment of a vow to Asclepius.
EDCS-30100872.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Marcus Ulpius Clemens and Aelius Mes to Asclepius.

EDCS-30100873.text_edition: \n Mercurio sacrum IRAT Beucus Sutti votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-30100873.translation: Sacred to Mercury. Beucus Sutti willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30100873.summary: This inscription records the voluntary fulfillment of a vow by Beucus Sutti to Mercury.

EDCS-30100874.text_edition: \n Apollini sacrum Verso Dasantis qui Veidavius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30100874.translation: Sacred to Apollo. Verso Dasantis, who is also called Veidavius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30100874.summary: This inscription records the voluntary fulfillment of a vow by Verso Dasantis (Veidavius) to Apollo.

EDCS-30100875.text_edition: \n Silvano ex voto Dexter et Martialis fratr<e=I>s \n
EDCS-30100875.translation: To Silvanus, from a vow, Dexter and Martialis his brother.
EDCS-30100875.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Silvanus by Dexter and his brother Martialis.

EDCS-30100876.text_edition: \n Libero et Liberae ex voto Beucus Dasantis\n
EDCS-30100876.translation: To Liber and Libera, from a vow, Beucus Dasantis.
EDCS-30100876.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Liber and Libera by Beucus Dasantis.

EDCS-30100877.text_edition: \n Terrae Matri ex voto Dasius Sta qui et Durius\n
EDCS-30100877.translation: To Mother Terra, from a vow, Dasius Sta, who is also called Durius.
EDCS-30100877.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Mother Terra by Dasius Sta (Durius).

EDCS-30100878.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo ex voto Panes Stagilis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30100878.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, in fulfillment of a vow, Panes Stagilis willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-30100878.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter by Panes Stagilis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30100880.text_edition: \n Apto Delmatarum sacrum Purtus et Germanus votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-30100880.translation: Sacred to Aptos of the Delmatae. Purtus and Germanus willingly and deservedly paid their vow.
EDCS-30100880.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the deity Aptos, dedicated by Purtus and Germanus of the Delmatae tribe, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30100881.text_edition: \n Apollini Piruneno sacrum Macrianus Surionis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30100881.translation: Sacred to Apollo Pirunenos. Macrianus Surionis willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-30100881.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Apollo Pirunenos by Macrianus Surionis, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30100882.text_edition: \n Dibus Artavis Bato Secundi votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30100882.translation: To the gods Artavis. Bato Secundi willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-30100882.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the gods Artavis by Bato Secundi, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30100883.text_edition: \n Ianus Dasas Verzonis et Nevato Implai\n
EDCS-30100883.translation: To Janus Dasas Verzonis and Nevato Implai.
EDCS-30100883.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Janus, potentially mentioning two individuals, Dasas Verzonis and Nevato Implai, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30100884.text_edition: \n Iano Gemino Lonius Tizius Celsi votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30100884.translation: To Janus Geminus. Lonius Tizius Celsi willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-30100884.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Janus Geminus by Lonius Tizius Celsi, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30100885.text_edition: \n Libero Patri Suttis Panentis filius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30100885.translation: To Liber Pater. Suttis, son of Panentis, willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-30100885.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Liber Pater by Suttis, son of Panentis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30100887.text_edition: \n Nimphis sic Aelius Mes collegii Sardiatensium votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30100887.translation: To the Nymphs. Aelius Mes of the college of Sardiatensians willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-30100887.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the Nymphs by Aelius Mes, a member of the college of Sardiatensians, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30100888.text_edition: \n Terrae Matri sacrum Siurioio Sumeletis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30100888.translation: Sacred to Mother Earth. Siurioio Sumeletis willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-30100888.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Mother Earth by Siurioio Sumeletis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30100889.text_edition: \n Dianae Augustae sacrum pro salute et incolumitate Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Augusti et Iuliae Augustae matris Augusti castrorum senatus ac patriae Caius Valerius Valentinus tribunus legionis XIIII geminae Antoninianae item tribunus numeri singularium Brittannicianorum\n
EDCS-30100889.translation: Sacred to Diana Augusta for the health and safety of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus and Julia Augusta, mother of the emperor, of the camp, the senate, and the fatherland, by Gaius Valerius Valentinus, tribune of the Legio XIIII Gemina Antoniniana, and also tribune of the elite cohort of Brittanniciani.
EDCS-30100889.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Diana Augusta for the well-being of the imperial family and the state, made by Gaius Valerius Valentinus, a military officer of high rank, during the reign of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (likely 161-180 AD).

EDCS-30200690.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelia Plotina annis vixit XXXXXX Aurelius Saturninus mil<e=I>s legionis \n
EDCS-30200690.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Aurelia Plotina lived 50 years. Aurelius Saturninus, a soldier of the legion...
EDCS-30200690.summary: A funerary inscription recording the age of Aurelia Plotina and the affiliation of Aurelius Saturninus, likely her husband, as a soldier.

EDCS-30200692.text_edition: \n Deo Apollini Augusto Caius Iulius Lysias |(centurio) legionis XIII <g=C>eminae Severianae iussu numinis eius porticum pecunia sua fecit \n
EDCS-30200692.translation: To the divine Apollo Augustus. Caius Iulius Lysias, a centurion of the XIII Gemina Severiana legion, built the portico at his own expense by order of the god's spirit.
EDCS-30200692.summary: A dedication to Apollo Augustus by a centurion of the XIII Gemina Severiana legion, who funded the construction of a portico.

EDCS-30200694.text_edition: \n Badonibus Reginis Sextia Augustina ex voto\n
EDCS-30200694.translation: To the Badonenses Regini. Sextia Augustina, fulfilling her vow.
EDCS-30200694.summary: A votive inscription to the deities Badones and Regina by Sextia Augustina.

EDCS-30200695.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iulius Chrysampelus voto libens posuit\n
EDCS-30200695.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Iulius Chrysampelus willingly set this up, fulfilling his vow.
EDCS-30200695.summary: A votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Iulius Chrysampelus.

EDCS-30200696.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Octavia Digna pro salute suis et suorum votum libens solvit merito\n
EDCS-30200696.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Octavia Digna willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow for the safety of her family and their relatives.
EDCS-30200696.summary: A votive inscription by Octavia Digna to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the well-being of her family.

EDCS-30200697.text_edition: \n Nemesi deae Terentius Marcianus beneficiarius templum a novo fecit ex viso votum solvit\n
EDCS-30200697.translation: To the goddess Nemesis. Terentius Marcianus, a beneficiary, built the temple anew from a vision, fulfilling his vow.
EDCS-30200697.summary: A dedication to the goddess Nemesis by Terentius Marcianus, who built a temple after a vision.

EDCS-30200699.text_edition: \n Lucius Aurelius Marinus veteranus legionis XIII geminae Antoninianae pro salute sua suorumque ex visu posuit\n
EDCS-30200699.translation: Lucius Aurelius Marinus, a veteran of the XIII Gemina Antoniniana legion, set this up from a vision for his own safety and that of his family.
EDCS-30200699.summary: A dedication by Lucius Aurelius Marinus, a veteran of the XIII Gemina legion, for his and his family's well-being.

EDCS-30200700.text_edition: \n LIVI hastatus legionis XIII geminae et Marcus Ulpius Restitutus |(centurio) exercitator equitum votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-30200700.translation: LIVIUS Hastatus, a soldier of the XIII Gemina legion, and Marcus Ulpius Restitutus, a centurion trainer of cavalry, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-30200700.summary: A joint votive inscription by Livius Hastatus and Marcus Ulpius Restitutus, a soldier and a centurion trainer, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-30200704.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VIIII imperator XI et Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus tribunicia potestate III muros caespiticios castrorum cohortis I Aureliae Brittonum |(miliariae) Antoninianae vetustate dilapsos lapide eos restituerunt per Octavium Iulianum legatum ipsorum pro praetore\n
EDCS-30200704.translation: Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, Pontifex Maximus, with the seventh tribunician power, Imperator for the eleventh time, and Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, with the third tribunician power, restored the turf ramparts of the camp of the I Aureliae Brittonum Cohort (miliariae) Antoniniana, which had collapsed from age, with stone, through Octavius Iulianus, their governor and propraetor.
EDCS-30200704.summary: An inscription detailing the restoration of a military camp's ramparts by Emperors Septimius Severus and Caracalla, acting through the governor Octavius Iulianus, likely from the period of Severus's reign (c. 205-210 AD).

EDCS-30200705.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gaius Crispinus Gai filius Claudia Firmus Aspendo veteranus legionis I Italicae Aureliae Severae coniugi vixit annos XXVI et nepotibus Aureliae Primillae suae bene maerentibus sic posuit\n
EDCS-30200705.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Gaius Crispinus, son of Gaius, and Claudia Firmus of Aspendo, a veteran of the I Italica Aurelia Severa legion, with his wife, lived 26 years, and for his grandchildren of Aurelia Primilla, his beloved, he set this up with sorrow.
EDCS-30200705.summary: A funerary inscription commemorating Gaius Crispinus, a veteran of the I Italica legion, his wife Claudia Firmus, and their grandchildren, likely dating to the Severan period.

EDCS-30300645.translation: Aelius Iulius of the Thirteenth Twin Legion.
EDCS-30300645.summary: This inscription records the name of Aelius Iulius, likely a soldier of the Legio XIII Gemina, possibly on a military diploma or tombstone.

EDCS-30300652.summary: This inscription records the name of Marcus Opellius Adiutor, likely an owner or dedicator, possibly on a tombstone or property marker.

EDCS-30300711.translation: Domitius Rufus, decurion, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30300711.summary: This inscription records Domitius Rufus, a decurion, fulfilling a vow, likely in relation to a religious offering or monument.

EDCS-30500562.summary: This inscription records the name of Gaius Iulius Valerius, possibly a property owner or dedicator, potentially on a boundary stone or building inscription.

EDCS-30500568.summary: This inscription records the name of Claudius Domitius Euarestus, possibly an owner or dedicator, likely on a tombstone or private monument.

EDCS-30500610.translation: Aurelius Apollonius of the Thirteenth Twin Legion.
EDCS-30500610.summary: This inscription records the name of Aurelius Apollonius, likely a soldier of the Legio XIII Gemina, possibly on a military diploma or tombstone.

EDCS-30500616.summary: This inscription records the name of Gaius Iulius Valens, possibly a property owner or dedicator, potentially on a boundary stone or building inscription.

EDCS-30500618.summary: This inscription records the name of Gaius Iulius Valerius, possibly a property owner or dedicator, potentially on a boundary stone or building inscription.

EDCS-30500620.translation: Of Gaius Velius Rufus, procurator of the Emperor.
EDCS-30500620.summary: This inscription records Gaius Velius Rufus, an imperial procurator, likely associated with an official building or monument from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-30500621.summary: This inscription records the name of Gaius Spedius Valens, possibly a property owner or dedicator, potentially on a boundary stone or building inscription.

EDCS-30500625.summary: This is a dedication to Publius Aelius Valens, likely for a tomb or a shrine.

EDCS-30500626.summary: This inscription likely records a dedication or a payment made by Amabilis for the public good.

EDCS-30500627.summary: This inscription likely records a dedication or a payment made by A. Secundus for the public good.

EDCS-30500629.summary: This inscription likely identifies Manius, a freedman of Donatus, possibly on a tombstone or object.

EDCS-30500632.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Iulius Maro, possibly for a monument or a grave.

EDCS-30500637.text_edition: \n Tertullo et Sacerdote consulibus Iulius Alexander fecit\n
EDCS-30500637.translation: Julius Alexander made this in the consulship of Tertullus and Sacerdos.
EDCS-30500637.summary: This inscription records that Julius Alexander erected a monument or structure during the consulship of Tertullus and Sacerdos.

EDCS-30500639.summary: This inscription records that Marcus erected a monument or structure.

EDCS-30500642.translation: Fronto, son of Paullinus, made this.
EDCS-30500642.summary: This inscription records that Fronto, son of Paullinus, erected a monument or structure.

EDCS-30500645.summary: This is a dedication to Gaius Iulius V, possibly for a tomb or a shrine.

EDCS-30500648.translation: A vow to Sol, by Iulius.
EDCS-30500648.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Iulius to the god Sol.

EDCS-30500667.translation: The First Cohort of Vindelicians, the Antoninian.
EDCS-30500667.summary: This inscription likely served as a designation for a military unit, the First Cohort of Vindelicians, which was designated as 'Antoninian', suggesting a connection to one of the Antonine emperors.

EDCS-31300300.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori municipii Aeli Hadriani Napocae cura IIvirorum quinquennalium Cai Numeri Deciani et Iuli Ingenui votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-31300300.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Preserver of the municipium of Aelius Hadrianus Napoca, the two quinquennial duovirs Caius Numerius Decianus and Gaius Iulius Ingenuus fulfilled their vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-31300300.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter, made by local magistrates of the Roman municipium of Aelius Hadrianus Napoca, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-31400109.text_edition: \n Pro salute Luciliae Musae Felicis nostri Florentinus et Suriacus\n
EDCS-31400109.translation: For the well-being of our Lucilia Musa Felix, Florentinus and Suriacus.
EDCS-31400109.summary: This inscription is a dedication made by Florentinus and Suriacus for the good health of Lucilia Musa Felix.

EDCS-31400112.translation: Aurelius Aeternalis placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-31400112.summary: This is a votive inscription by Aurelius Aeternalis, indicating he dedicated something in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-31400114.text_edition: \n Invicto Mithrae I Q II votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-31400114.translation: To the Invincible Mithras, the second decurion fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-31400114.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Mithras, dedicated by a decurion (a military officer of a decury).

EDCS-31400116.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name Ferox and the duration of their life.

EDCS-31400117.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Traiani Parthici filio divi Nervae nepoti Traiano Hadriano Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XVII consuli III patri patriae Suri sagiattarii sub Tito Flavio Constante procuratore Augusti \n
EDCS-31400117.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 17th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, the Suri archers under Titus Flavius Constans, procurator of the Emperor, dedicated this.
EDCS-31400117.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the Suri archers, likely soldiers, to Emperor Hadrian, mentioning specific imperial titles and the procurator under whom they served, dating it to Hadrian's reign.

EDCS-31400118.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Traiani Parthici filio divi Nervae nepoti Traiano Hadriano Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XXII consuli III patri patriae Suri sagittarii sub Tito Flavio Constante procuratore Augusti\n
EDCS-31400118.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 22nd time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, the Suri archers under Titus Flavius Constans, procurator of the Emperor, dedicated this.
EDCS-31400118.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the Suri archers, likely soldiers, to Emperor Hadrian, mentioning specific imperial titles and the procurator under whom they served, dating it to Hadrian's reign.

EDCS-31400119.text_edition: \n Valerius Valerianus eques librarius cohortis I Hispanorum ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-31400119.translation: Valerius Valerianus, a cavalryman and scribe of the First Cohort of Hispaniards, placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-31400119.summary: This is a votive inscription by Valerius Valerianus, identified as a cavalryman and scribe of a specific military cohort, indicating he dedicated something in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-31500007.summary: This inscription is a designation for the Roman legion known as the Thirteenth Gemina.

EDCS-31700005.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus basilicam legionis V Macedonicae \n
EDCS-31700005.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus [rebuilt/dedicated] the basilica of the Fifth Macedonian Legion.
EDCS-31700005.summary: This inscription records the rebuilding or dedication of a legionary basilica by Emperor Gordian III, likely in the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-31800206.translation: Claudius Iulianus, a soldier of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, in the century of Claudius Marius, acquired by mancipation and received in marriage the woman named Theudote, or by whatever other name she may be known, a Cretan, freed from further claim, for two unciae and four hundred and twenty denarii, from Claudius Philetus, acting on behalf of Alexander, son of Antipater. Claudius Iulianus, the above-written soldier, promised that the woman, delivered healthy to the buyer above-written, and if anyone should successfully claim the said woman or any part of her, preventing the buyer above-written, or whoever will possess this property, from rightly enjoying, possessing, and holding her, then that amount which is thus evicted or taken away from her, or which is not permitted to be held, shall be duly paid. Alexander, son of Antipater, ordered this to be binding on his own responsibility. Claudius Philetus stated that he accepted and has the price of the woman above-written, namely 420 denarii, from Claudius Iulianus, the above-written soldier. Done in the camp of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, on the fourth day before the Nones of October, during the consulship of Bradua and Varus.
EDCS-31800206.summary: This inscription details a legal transaction, likely a marriage contract or sale, involving a soldier, Claudius Iulianus, a woman named Theudote, and Claudius Philetus, with Alexander Antipatri acting as guarantor, dated to 143 CE.

EDCS-31900186.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus us Iusti VIII P Philippianae MI vi<v=B>i vixit LXXXV IVO AN fecit Valeria Iustina XXXV \n
EDCS-31900186.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iustus of Philippiana lived 88 years. Valeria Iustina made [this] at age 35.
EDCS-31900186.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iustus, who lived to be 88, with his wife Valeria Iustina, who is 35, setting up the monument.

EDCS-31900187.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulio Claudio vixit annos II Aelia Va pie Cleo filio bene merenti fecrunt\n
EDCS-31900187.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Claudius lived for 2 years. Aelia Va, loving mother, made [this] for her deserving son.
EDCS-31900187.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Claudius, who died at the age of two, set up by his mother Aelia Va.

EDCS-31900188.text_edition: \n AREATINO cohorte I Ba<t=I>(avorum) vixit annos Claudia Actav soro[r ------ \n
EDCS-31900188.translation: From the cohort of the Areatini, first of the Batavians. Claudia Actav lived for [number] years. Her sister [restored name] [restored phrase].
EDCS-31900188.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Claudia Actav, a member of the first cohort of Batavians, with her sister setting up the monument.

EDCS-31900189.text_edition: \n birsi mili<t=I>i cohortis annorum XXX Marcus Aurelius as frater Aur(elia)soror ponendum curaverunt fratri \n
EDCS-31900189.translation: Marcus Aurelius, a soldier of the cohort, aged 30 years, and his sister Aurelia, took care to place [this] for their brother.
EDCS-31900189.summary: This funerary inscription records the memorialization of a deceased soldier by his brother and sister.

EDCS-31900190.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelius Drebias <v=B>otum rettulit \n
EDCS-31900190.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Aurelius Drebias dedicated his vow.
EDCS-31900190.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aurelius Drebias.

EDCS-31900191.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Aurelius Cup[itus ------\n
EDCS-31900191.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Marcus Aurelius Cupitus [dedicated/made...].
EDCS-31900191.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Aurelius Cupitus.

EDCS-31900192.summary: This is a dedication to the household god Silvanus.

EDCS-31900193.summary: This fragment appears to contain parts of personal names, likely from a larger inscription, possibly funerary or dedicatory.

EDCS-31900196.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Dasas Capito vixit annos LV-- ipse posuit hic est situs\n
EDCS-31900196.translation: To the divine spirits. Dasas Capito lived 55 years. He himself placed [this]. Here he is buried.
EDCS-31900196.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespan of Dasas Capito, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-32200976.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Iunoni Reginae Iulius Valerius Emeritus praefectus cohortis \n
EDCS-32200976.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina. Iulius Valerius Emeritus, prefect of a cohort, [dedicated this].
EDCS-32200976.summary: A dedication to Jupiter and Juno by Iulius Valerius Emeritus, a prefect of a cohort, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-32200978.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Diophanto veterano cohortis V Gallorum vixit annos LXXXVI hic situs est Aelia Ammis filia et Aelia Eutychia libertae heredes fecerunt\n
EDCS-32200978.translation: To the divine spirits. Publius Aelius Diophantus, a veteran of the 5th Gallic cohort, lived 86 years. Here he is buried. Aelia Ammis, his daughter, and Aelia Eutychia, freedwomen, his heirs, made [this].
EDCS-32200978.summary: A tombstone for Publius Aelius Diophantus, a veteran of the 5th Gallic cohort, erected by his daughter and freedwomen heirs, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-32200979.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelius Bassus veteranus legionis V Macedonicae vixit nnis LXVI mensibus X diebus XVI Aelius Helpizon libertus et heres eius posuit\n
EDCS-32200979.translation: To the divine spirits. Aelius Bassus, a veteran of the 5th Macedonian legion, lived 66 years, 10 months, and 16 days. Aelius Helpizon, his freedman and heir, placed [this].
EDCS-32200979.summary: A tombstone for Aelius Bassus, a veteran of the 5th Macedonian legion, erected by his freedman and heir, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-32200980.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulius Melchidianus veteranus ex beneficiario consularis legionis V Macedonicae vixit annos LXI Ulpia Marcellina coniugi pientissimo bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-32200980.translation: To the divine spirits. Iulius Melchidianus, a veteran and former decurio beneficarius of the 5th Macedonian legion, lived 61 years. Ulpia Marcellina placed [this] for her most devoted and deserving husband.
EDCS-32200980.summary: A tombstone for Iulius Melchidianus, a veteran and former decurio beneficarius of the 5th Macedonian legion, erected by his wife, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-32200981.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Iulio Verecundo veterano ex decurione alae Claudiae Claudius Longinus amicus et heres pos(uit\n
EDCS-32200981.translation: To the divine spirits. For Gaius Julius Verecundus, a veteran and former decurion of the 1st Claudian ala. Claudius Longinus, his friend and heir, placed [this].
EDCS-32200981.summary: A tombstone for Gaius Julius Verecundus, a veteran and former decurion of the Claudian ala, erected by his friend and heir, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-32200982.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valeria Gemina vixit nnos XVII dies X hic sita est Publius Aelius Papirianus |(centurio) cohortis III Campestris coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-32200982.translation: To the divine spirits. Valeria Gemina lived 17 years and 10 days. Here she is buried. Publius Aelius Papirianus, a centurion of the 3rd Campestrian cohort, placed [this] for his deserving wife.
EDCS-32200982.summary: A tombstone for Valeria Gemina, erected by her husband Publius Aelius Papirianus, a centurion, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-32200983.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Primius Aelius Ionicus negotiator vixit annos L Iulia Priscilla uxor et Aelius Iulianus filius coheredes per Aelio! Primitivo sic liberto!\n
EDCS-32200983.translation: To the divine spirits. Primius Aelius Ionicus, a merchant, lived 50 years. Iulia Priscilla, his wife, and Aelius Iulianus, his son, co-heirs through his freedman Aelius Primitivus, [placed this].
EDCS-32200983.summary: A tombstone for Primius Aelius Ionicus, a merchant, erected by his wife, son, and freedman, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-32200985.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Asclepiae Chronae vixit annos XL hic sita est Asclepius Asclepiades patronus coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-32200985.translation: To the divine spirits. Asclepias Chrona lived 40 years. Here she is buried. Asclepius Asclepiades, patron, placed [this] for his deserving wife.
EDCS-32200985.summary: A tombstone for Asclepias Chrona, erected by her husband Asclepius Asclepiades, a patron, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-32200986.text_edition: \n Sabina Labrionis vixit annos L Senecio Suri vixit annos XX\n
EDCS-32200986.translation: Sabina Labrionis lived 50 years. Senecio Suri lived 20 years.
EDCS-32200986.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespans of two individuals, Sabina Labrionis and Senecio Suri, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-32200988.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the divine [Caesar].
EDCS-32200988.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records the titles of an emperor, possibly Hadrian, given the mention of his deified father. [Date uncertain]

EDCS-32200989.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Caius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate II consul proconsul pater patriae et Caius Iulius Verus Maximinus nobilissimus Caesar III milia passuum\n
EDCS-32200989.translation: Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the second time, consul, proconsul, father of the fatherland, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus, most noble Caesar, [dedicated this] three miles.
EDCS-32200989.summary: This inscription records the titles and dignities of Emperor Maximinus Thrax and his son, Caesar Maximinus. [Dated c. 236-238 AD]

EDCS-32200990.translation: The Legio XIII Gemina Antoniniana made [this] for Viro.
EDCS-32200990.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by the Legio XIII Gemina Antoniniana, likely for a person named Viro. [Date uncertain, Antoniniana suggests 3rd century AD]

EDCS-32500064.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iuliae Gratae filiae Iulius Strato |(centurio) legionis IIII Flaviae et Aelia Adaucta parentes fecerunt\n
EDCS-32500064.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Julia Grata, his daughter. Julius Strato, a centurion of the IV Flavia Legion, and Aelia Adaucta, his parents, made [this].
EDCS-32500064.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by parents for their deceased daughter, Julia Grata, with the father identified as a centurion. [Date uncertain]

EDCS-32500065.text_edition: \n Dianae Reginae Marcus Ulpius Herculanus ex praefecto et Iulia Teupiboea eius votum solverunt\n
EDCS-32500065.translation: Marcus Ulpius Herculanus, formerly prefect, and his wife Iulia Teupiboea, fulfilled their vow to Diana Regina.
EDCS-32500065.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Marcus Ulpius Herculanus, a former prefect, and his wife Iulia Teupiboea to the goddess Diana Regina. [Date uncertain]

EDCS-32500067.translation: [---] Aurelius Posinus [dedicated this to the gods].
EDCS-32500067.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription possibly recording a dedication made by Aurelius Posinus. [Date uncertain]

EDCS-32500070.text_edition: \n Titus Iulius Euanthus matri piissimae fecit\n
EDCS-32500070.translation: Titus Iulius Euanthus made [this] for his most devoted mother.
EDCS-32500070.summary: This inscription records Titus Iulius Euanthus dedicating something to his mother. [Date uncertain]

EDCS-32500279.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Scenobarbus Dassi filius vixit \n
EDCS-32500279.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Scenobarbus, son of Dasius, lived [for X years].
EDCS-32500279.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Scenobarbus, son of Dasius, likely recording his lifespan. [Date uncertain]

EDCS-34100094.text_edition: \n Genio |(centuriae) Valeri Iustini Aurelius Muca pro salute commanuculorum posuit\n
EDCS-34100094.translation: Aurelius Muca set up [this] for the genius of Valerius Iustinus's century, for the well-being of his comrades.
EDCS-34100094.summary: This inscription records Aurelius Muca setting up a monument to the genius of his century and for the welfare of his fellow soldiers. [Date uncertain]

EDCS-34100095.text_edition: \n Genio armamentarii posuit Aelius Vitalis custos armorum\n
EDCS-34100095.translation: Aelius Vitalis, guardian of the arms, set up [this] for the genius of the armory.
EDCS-34100095.summary: This inscription records Aelius Vitalis, who was in charge of weaponry, dedicating something to the genius of the armory. [Date uncertain]

EDCS-34100096.text_edition: \n Terrae Matri Septimius Asclepius Hermes libertus numinis Aesculapi habens ornamenta decurionalia coloniae Apulensis et Augustalis coloniae eiusdem votum posuit\n
EDCS-34100096.translation: To Mother Earth, Septimius Asclepius Hermes, freedman of the divinity of Asclepius, holding the decurional ornaments of the colony of Apula and the Augustal ornaments of the same colony, set up his vow.
EDCS-34100096.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Terra Mater by Septimius Asclepius Hermes, a freedman who held civic offices in the colony of Apula.

EDCS-34100099.text_edition: \n Marti Toutatico Caius Valerius Hermes pro salute sua votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-34100099.translation: To Mars Toutaticus, Caius Valerius Hermes willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for his own health.
EDCS-34100099.summary: This inscription records Caius Valerius Hermes fulfilling a vow to the god Mars Toutaticus for his own well-being.

EDCS-34100104.summary: This inscription consists of a single name, likely marking a tomb or a dedication by Iulius Sempronius.

EDCS-34100105.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno pro salute Impperatorum et cohortis primae sagittariorum Silvanus Flavius et Athennais sic sacerdotes cohortis supra scriptae ex voto posuerunt\n
EDCS-34100105.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the god of Doliche, for the health of the Emperors and of the first cohort of archers, Silvanus Flavius and Athennais, priests of the aforementioned cohort, set up their vow.
EDCS-34100105.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus by the priests of the first cohort of archers, Silvanus Flavius and Athennais, for the health of the Emperors.

EDCS-34500866.summary: This inscription appears to be an ownership mark or a fragment of a longer text, possibly indicating property belonging to Marcus Ulpius P.

EDCS-34500875.summary: This inscription consists of a name, Quintus Aponius Bassus, likely indicating ownership or a dedication.

EDCS-34500878.summary: This inscription is a partial name, Marcus Iulius D., possibly denoting ownership or as part of a funerary monument.

EDCS-34500885.translation: Of Pomponius Fortis of the XIII Gemina Legion.
EDCS-34500885.summary: This inscription likely records the name of Pomponius Fortis, a soldier of the XIII Gemina Legion, possibly on a tomb or military marker.

EDCS-34500903.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Mercury Augustus.

EDCS-34500905.translation: Augustus, son of Papiria Domitia N.
EDCS-34500905.summary: This inscription appears to be a patronymic and a cognomen, possibly part of a dedication or funerary text, mentioning Augustus, son of Papiria Domitia.

EDCS-34500912.text_edition: \n Caio Iulio Rufino filio Cai Iuli I coll(egii) decuriae IX \n
EDCS-34500912.translation: To Gaius Julius Rufinus, son of Gaius Julius, of the college of the ninth decuria.
EDCS-34500912.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Gaius Julius Rufinus, likely a member of a collegium (association or guild) in ancient Rome.

EDCS-34500965.translation: The troop of Marcus Carteius Severus.
EDCS-34500965.summary: This inscription records a military unit, the turma (troop) of Marcus Carteius Severus.

EDCS-36800074.summary: This inscription simply names the Fifth Macedonian Legion, a famous Roman army unit.

EDCS-36800092.translation: Arrius Antoninus, consular governor of Dacia and consular governor of Dalmatia, to Helvius Pertinax, the consular governor.
EDCS-36800092.summary: This bilingual inscription records a dedication by Arrius Antoninus, a high-ranking official with consular authority in Dacia and Dalmatia, to Helvius Pertinax, likely another official of similar rank.

EDCS-37200091.text_edition: \n Terentius Fegel Tit<i>us Degibitus Loscaiius sic Gemellus Titus A Marcianus ABCDEFGHIKLM NOPQRSTVXYZ\n
EDCS-37200091.translation: Terentius Fegel Titius Degibitus Loscaiius, called Gemellus Titus, and [names of other individuals from the alphabet].
EDCS-37200091.summary: This inscription lists names, including Terentius Fegel Titius Degibitus Loscaiius and possibly others represented by a sequence of letters, whose purpose is unclear but may relate to a group or list.

EDCS-38100329.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico Publius Aelius Maximus sesquiplicarius\n
EDCS-38100329.translation: To Silvanus Domesticius, Publius Aelius Maximus, sesquiplicarius.
EDCS-38100329.summary: This is a dedication to the deity Silvanus Domesticius by Publius Aelius Maximus, identified by his military rank of sesquiplicarius.

EDCS-38100330.text_edition: \n Herculi Apro eques promissum fecit votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-38100330.translation: To Hercules Apro, the horseman made a promise and willingly fulfilled his vow as deserved.
EDCS-38100330.summary: This is a votive inscription recording that an individual, identified as an eques (horseman), fulfilled a vow made to the god Hercules Apro.

EDCS-38100333.text_edition: \n propro tili Rufini conductoris salinarum Ursio servus actor verna\n
EDCS-38100333.translation: For Rufinus, conductor of the saltworks, Ursio, his steward and home-born slave.
EDCS-38100333.summary: This inscription is likely a funerary dedication or a record of service on behalf of Rufinus, who managed saltworks, by his slave and steward Ursio.

EDCS-38100338.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caius Domitius Alexander veteranus legionis V Macedonicae ex signifero vixit annos LXX Caius Domitius Nicostratus Augustalis coloniae Septimiae Drobetensium patrono secundum voluntatem testamenti\n
EDCS-38100338.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead: Gaius Domitius Alexander, veteran of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, ex-signifer, lived 70 years. Gaius Domitius Nicostratus, Augustalis of the colony Septimia Drobeta, to his patron, according to his will.
EDCS-38100338.summary: This is a double funerary inscription recording the death of a veteran, Gaius Domitius Alexander, and a dedication by Gaius Domitius Nicostratus to his patron.

EDCS-38800044.translation: To the gods of the underworld: Epictetus, formerly freedman of Epictetus, son of Apollonius, and his wife, on behalf of themselves and their freedmen.
EDCS-38800044.summary: This is a dedication to underworld deities by Epictetus, a freedman, for himself, his wife, and their freedmen.

EDCS-41300196.text_edition: \n Apollini Granno et Sironae dis praesentibus Quintus Axius Aelianus vir egregius procurator Auggustorum\n
EDCS-41300196.translation: To Apollo Grannus and Sirona, present deities, Quintus Axius Aelianus, a distinguished man and procurator of the Emperors, (dedicated this).
EDCS-41300196.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the deities Apollo Grannus and Sirona by Quintus Axius Aelianus, a procurator, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-41300197.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Antoni<n>o Pio Augusto colonia Sarmizaegethusa sic\n
EDCS-41300197.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Antoninus Pius Augustus, the colony Sarmizegethusa thus (dedicated this).
EDCS-41300197.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the colony of Sarmizegethusa to Emperor Antoninus Pius, likely from the mid-2nd century CE.

EDCS-44200277.text_edition: \n Titus Aelius Celer praefectus equitum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-44200277.translation: Titus Aelius Celer, prefect of cavalry, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-44200277.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Titus Aelius Celer, a prefect of cavalry, probably from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-44200281.summary: This inscription is too fragmentary to provide a meaningful translation or summary.

EDCS-44200282.translation: Carago B placed an altar to Silvanus.
EDCS-44200282.summary: This inscription records an altar dedicated to Silvanus by Carago B, the context suggesting a private dedication during the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-44200284.text_edition: \n Nemesi Iunia Abascanti coniuncx sic ex visu votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-44200284.translation: To Nemesis, Iunia Abascanti, his wife, gladly and deservedly fulfilled (her) vow after seeing (her) in a vision.
EDCS-44200284.summary: This inscription records a vow made to Nemesis by Iunia Abascanti, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-44200285.text_edition: \n Minervae Augustae et Genio numeri Palmyrenorum Tibiscensium Valerius Rufinus actarius\n
EDCS-44200285.translation: To Minerva Augusta and the Genius of the detachment of Palmyrenes of Tibiscum, Valerius Rufinus, scribe, (dedicated this).
EDCS-44200285.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Minerva Augusta and the Genius of a military unit by Valerius Rufinus, a scribe, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-44800149.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Pio pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XII imperatori II consuli III patri patriae et Aurelio Vero Caesari consuli II colonia Ulpia Oescus ob \n
EDCS-44800149.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Pius, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 12th time, imperator for the 2nd time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, and to Aurelius Ver Caesar, consul for the 2nd time, the colony Ulpia Oescus (dedicated this) because...
EDCS-44800149.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the colony Ulpia Oescus to Emperors Antoninus Pius and Marcus Aurelius (as Caesar), likely from the period when they were consuls together, around 140-161 CE.

EDCS-44800179.translation: To the Ala Frontoniana, of Valerius Firmus.
EDCS-44800179.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or dedication-related text concerning the cavalry unit Ala Frontoniana and a person named Valerius Firmus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-45100233.translation: Aelius Nisa Proculina, with the approval of the decurions, (dedicated this).
EDCS-45100233.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Aelius Nisa Proculina, approved by the decurions, likely from a Roman colony during the imperial period.

EDCS-45100234.text_edition: \n Sever annos XVII ius vixit annos \n
EDCS-45100234.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of a person named Sever.

EDCS-45100235.text_edition: \n nnos L a vixit nnos Martinianus bene merentibus posuit\n
EDCS-45100235.translation: Martianus erected this for the well-deserving, who lived for 50 years.
EDCS-45100235.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Martinianus to a well-deserving person who lived for 50 years.

EDCS-45100244.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori Aelius Super Augustalis municipii Septimi M Potaissensis votum libens posuit\n
EDCS-45100244.translation: Aelius Super Augustalis of the municipality of Potaissa, willingly dedicated his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator.
EDCS-45100244.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator by Aelius Super Augustalis, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-45100245.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Capitolino votum libens a<n=R>(imo solvit Aurelius Castor mensor legionis V Macedonicae piae \n
EDCS-45100245.translation: Aurelius Castor, a surveyor of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, piously fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Capitolinus.
EDCS-45100245.summary: This is a votive dedication by Aurelius Castor, a surveyor from the Fifth Macedonian Legion, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Capitolinus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-45100246.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aelius NDN N IX\n
EDCS-45100246.translation: Aelius [---] NDN IX to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-45100246.summary: This is a partial inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, possibly by Aelius, with some numbers or names following.

EDCS-45100249.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico Aurelius Maximus miles legionis V Macedonicae piae Gordianae votum laetus libens\n
EDCS-45100249.translation: Aurelius Maximus, a soldier of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, piously and gladly fulfilled his vow to Silvanus Domestticus.
EDCS-45100249.summary: This is a votive dedication by Aurelius Maximus, a soldier of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, to Silvanus Domestticus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-45100250.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico Aurelius Ianuarius votum libens posuit\n
EDCS-45100250.translation: Aurelius Ianuarius willingly erected this vow to Silvanus Domestticus.
EDCS-45100250.summary: This is a votive dedication by Aurelius Ianuarius to Silvanus Domestticus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-45100255.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valeria Maximilla vixit annos XXIX menses VII Valerius Maximus veteranus ex |(centurione) pater et Valeria Marcellina mater filiae pientissimae et Publius Aelius Tertius veteranus ex decurione coniugi optimae et pientissimae et Terentius et Valeria et Maximianus filii matri carissimae fecerunt\n
EDCS-45100255.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Valeria Maximilla lived 29 years and 7 months. Her father Valerius Maximus, a veteran ex-centurion, and her mother Valeria Marcellina, for their most dutiful daughter, and Publius Aelius Tertius, a veteran ex-decurion, for his most excellent and dutiful wife, and their children Terentius, Valeria, and Maximianus, for their dearest mother, made this.
EDCS-45100255.summary: This is a complex funerary monument set up by parents, husband, and children for their deceased family members, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-45100256.text_edition: \n Valeria Dulae vixit annos XXI ITTVIVN posuit Volusius Titianus coniugi pie bene merenti\n
EDCS-45100256.translation: Valeria Dulae lived 21 years. Volusius Titianus erected this for his dutiful and well-deserving wife.
EDCS-45100256.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Volusius Titianus for his wife Valeria Dulae, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-45100259.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus ianus vixit nnos PANIANVS EI \n
EDCS-45100259.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Ianus lived [---] PANIANVS EI.
EDCS-45100259.summary: This is a partial funerary inscription for a person named Ianus, with illegible text following.

EDCS-45100260.text_edition: \n itius Re supra script- filius eoannos XI Domiius filius posuit\n
EDCS-45100260.translation: Domitius, son of Titus Re, and his son Ioannes, placed [this monument].
EDCS-45100260.summary: This inscription records the placement of a monument by Domitius and his son Ioannes, likely in honor of Titus Re, possibly from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-45100261.text_edition: \n vixit --- annis IX Marcus Ulpius Valentinus vixit annis X Aureli\n
EDCS-45100261.translation: Marcus Ulpius Valentinus lived 10 years; Aurelius lived 9 years.
EDCS-45100261.summary: This inscription records the ages of two individuals, Marcus Ulpius Valentinus and Aurelius, likely a funerary epitaph from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-45100262.summary: This inscription likely records the name of Bassinus Vitalis Quintus, possibly as a funerary epitaph, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-45100263.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription that names Aurelius Crescens, its full purpose and date are uncertain.

EDCS-45100273.translation: To the departed spirits, Caius Iulius Aem[ilianus]...
EDCS-45100273.summary: This inscription is dedicated to the departed spirits (Dis Manibus) and names Caius Iulius Aemilianus, likely a funerary monument from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-45100274.text_edition: \n Volkano sic Augusto pro salute Imperatoris Luci Septimi Severi Pertinacis Augusti et Marci Aureli Antonini Caesaris destinati collegium fabrum municipii Septimii Potaissensis\n
EDCS-45100274.translation: For Vulcan, as well as for the safety of the Emperor Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Caesar, the guild of craftsmen of the municipium of Septimius Potaissa made this vow.
EDCS-45100274.summary: This inscription records a vow made by the guild of craftsmen of Septimius Potaissa to Vulcan and for the safety of Emperors Septimius Severus and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, dating to their period of rule (late 2nd/early 3rd century CE).

EDCS-45100278.text_edition: \n IunoniIunoniginae votum solvit Aelia Cal<li=P>ope pro se et suis libens merito \n
EDCS-45100278.translation: Aelia Caliope willingly and deservedly paid her vow to Juno Regina for herself and her family.
EDCS-45100278.summary: This inscription records Aelia Caliope fulfilling a vow to Juno Regina for herself and her family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-45100288.text_edition: \n Iunoni Raeginae sic Aelius Iulius veteranus ex |(centurione) votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-45100288.translation: Aelius Iulius, a veteran and former centurion, willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Juno Regina.
EDCS-45100288.summary: This inscription records Aelius Iulius, a veteran and former centurion, fulfilling a vow to Juno Regina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-45100290.text_edition: \n Apollini Aelius Iulius veteranus ex |(centurione) votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-45100290.translation: Aelius Iulius, a veteran and former centurion, willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Apollo.
EDCS-45100290.summary: This inscription records Aelius Iulius, a veteran and former centurion, fulfilling a vow to Apollo, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-45100291.text_edition: \n Mercurio Aelius Iulius veteranus ex |(centurione) votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-45100291.translation: Aelius Iulius, a veteran and former centurion, willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Mercury.
EDCS-45100291.summary: This inscription records Aelius Iulius, a veteran and former centurion, fulfilling a vow to Mercury, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-45100292.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico Aelius Iulius veteranus ex |(centurione) votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-45100292.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Aelius Iulius, a veteran formerly a centurion, gladly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-45100292.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus Domesticus by a former centurion.

EDCS-45100293.text_edition: \n Dibus deabus et Fortunae Conservatrici Aelius Iulius veteranus ex |(centurione) votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-45100293.translation: To the Gods and Goddesses, and to Fortune the Preserver, Aelius Iulius, a veteran formerly a centurion, gladly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-45100293.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the gods and Fortune by a former centurion.

EDCS-45100294.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription dedicated to Silvanus Domesticus, possibly mentioning a name or title.

EDCS-45100298.text_edition: \n Dianae verae et bonae votum solvit libens merito M\n
EDCS-45100298.translation: To Diana, true and good, M. gladly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-45100298.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Diana by an individual identified only by the initial M.

EDCS-45100320.translation: To the most holy mother Augusta.
EDCS-45100320.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription possibly dedicating something to the 'mother Augusta'.

EDCS-45100324.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Porolissensis (likely referring to a person from Porolissum) and a procurator.

EDCS-45100325.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Malachus flamen quinquennalis municipii Septimii Porolissensis et sacerdos Dei numeri Palmyrenorum Porolissensium votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-45100325.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Publius Aelius Malachus, quinquennial priest of the municipium of Septimius Porolissensis and priest of the God of the Palmyrene Gods of Porolissum, gladly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-45100325.summary: This is a votive inscription by a prominent local official in Porolissum, dedicating to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and also mentioning his priesthoods.

EDCS-45100326.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Iacubus decurio municipii Porolissensis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-45100326.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Publius Aelius Iacubus, decurion of the municipium of Porolissensis, gladly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-45100326.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a decurion of the municipium of Porolissum.

EDCS-45100329.text_edition: \n Deo Libero Patri Titus Flavius Valentianus beneficiarius consularis\n
EDCS-45100329.translation: To the God Liber Pater, Titus Flavius Valentianus, a beneficiarius consularis.
EDCS-45100329.summary: This is an inscription recording a dedication to Liber Pater by a beneficiarius consularis.

EDCS-45100331.text_edition: \n annos X Aelius Firmus |(centurio) cohortis I Brittonum filiae piissimae\n
EDCS-45100331.translation: For ten years, Aelius Firmus, a centurion of the I Brittonum cohort, [dedicated this] to his most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-45100331.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a daughter, set up by her father who was a centurion in the I Brittonum cohort.

EDCS-45100332.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulia Iustina vixit nnos XLII Cassius Martialis beneficiarius consularis coniugi carissimae Iustina coniux sit tibi terra levis\n
